textEn:
Directions: In the following passage some words have been deleted. Fill in the blanks with the help of the alternatives given. Select the most appropriate option for each number.

In spite of all the honours that were heaped upon him, Pasteur remained a simple man ___(1)___ heart. Perhaps the memories of his boyhood days, when he ___(2)___ the familiar scenes of his birthplace, and the ___(3)___ to be a great artist never wholly left him. He did ___(4)___ a great artist but after his sixteenth year he ___(5)___ the brush forever. He put his soul and energy ___(6)___ his work, and it was this very energy that in the end ___(7)___ him out. For him, each sufferer was more than ___(8)___ a case that was to be cured. He looked upon the ___(9)___ against hydrophobia as a battle and he was ___(10)___ to win at any cost.

Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank no. 1.
textHi:
Directions: In the following passage some words have been deleted. Fill in the blanks with the help of the alternatives given. Select the most appropriate option for each number.

Perhaps the memories of his boyhood days, when he ___(2)___ the familiar scenes of his birthplace, and the ___(3)___ to be a great artist never wholly left him. He did ___(4)___ a great artist but after his sixteenth year he ___(5)___ the brush forever. He put his soul and energy ___(6)___ his work, and it was this very energy that in the end ___(7)___ him out. For him, each sufferer was more than ___(8)___ a case that was to be cured. He looked upon the ___(9)___ against hydrophobia as a battle and he was ___(10)___ to win at any cost.

Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank no. 1.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'at.'

Key Points

  • The given sentence ''In spite of all the honours that were heaped upon him, Pasteur remained a simple man ___(1)___ heart​​'' is telling something about the simplicity of Pasteur.
  • Let us explore the given options:
    • The preposition 'at' is used for stating what makes someone react in a particular way.
      • ''At heart'' means in one's real nature, in contrast to how one may appear.
    • The preposition 'by' is used for saying who or what does something.
      • ''By heart'' means by remembering exactly; from memory.
    • The preposition 'in' means inside or to a position inside a particular area or object.
    • The preposition 'on' means supported by, fixed to or touching something, especially a surface.

Conclusion: Pasteur remained a simple man even though he got a large number of honours.

Hence, the correct answer is option 1.

Complete Sentence: In spite of all the honours that were heaped upon him, Pasteur remained a simple man at heart​​.

Hinglish

  • दिया गया वाक्य 'In spite of all the honours that were heaped upon him, Pasteur remained a simple man ___(1)___ heart​​'' पाश्चर की सादगी के बारे में कुछ बता रहा है।
  • आइए दिए गए विकल्पों का पता लगाएं:
    • Preposition 'at' का उपयोग यह बताने के लिए किया जाता है कि कोई व्यक्ति किसी विशेष तरीके से क्या प्रतिक्रिया करता है।
      • ''At heart'' का अर्थ है किसी के वास्तविक स्वरूप में, इसके विपरीत कि कोई कैसे प्रकट हो सकता है।
    • Preposition 'by' यह कहने के लिए प्रयोग किया जाता है कि कौन या क्या कुछ करता है।
      • ''By heart'' का अर्थ है ठीक-ठीक याद करना; याद से।
    • Preposition 'in' का अर्थ है किसी विशेष क्षेत्र या वस्तु के अंदर या स्थिति के अंदर।
    • Preposition 'on' का अर्थ है पर, के ऊपर।


Conclusion: इतने सारे सम्मानों के बावजूद, पाश्चर दिल से एक साधारण व्यक्ति बने रहे।

अत: सही उत्तर विकल्प 1 है ।

  • 'Draw' is the base form of the verb. ('Draw' क्रिया का मूल रूप है)
  • 'Draws' is the third-person present singular form of the verb. ('Draws' क्रिया का third-person present singular रूप है)

Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank no. 5.
textHi:
Directions: In the following passage some words have been deleted. Fill in the blanks with the help of the alternatives given. Select the most appropriate option for each number.

In spite of all the honours that were heaped upon him, Pasteur remained a simple man ___(1)___ heart. Perhaps the memories of his boyhood days, when he ___(2)___ the familiar scenes of his birthplace, and the ___(3)___ to be a great artist never wholly left him. He did ___(4)___ a great artist but after his sixteenth year he ___(5)___ the brush forever. He put his soul and energy ___(6)___ his work, and it was this very energy that in the end ___(7)___ him out. For him, each sufferer was more than ___(8)___ a case that was to be cured. He looked upon the ___(9)___ against hydrophobia as a battle and he was ___(10)___ to win at any cost.

Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank no. 5.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'abandoned.'

Key Points

  • The given sentence "He did become a great artist but after his sixteenth year he ___(5)___ the brush forever'' is indicating something happened after Pasteur's sixteenth year.
  • The use of the word ''forever'' in the sentence indicates for all future time; for always.
  • Therefore, the most appropriate word to be filled in the blank is 'abandoned'.
  • The word 'abandoned' means to stop doing something without finishing it or without achieving what you wanted to do.
    • Example: She abandoned law for the fine arts.

Hence, the correct answer is option 4.

Complete Sentence: He did become a great artist but after his sixteenth year he abandoned the brush forever.
Additional Information

  • Let us explore the other options:
    • 'Discharged' means allowed somebody officially to leave; sent somebody away. (किसी को बाहर या दूर भेज देना या मुक्त कर देना)
    • 'Released' means to stop holding something so that it can move, fly, fall, etc. freely. (किसी वस्‍तु को खुला छोड़ देना, बंधन ढीला करना)
    • 'Expelled' means forced somebody to leave a country, school, club, etc. (निष्‍कासित करना)

  • इसलिए, रिक्त स्थान में भरने के लिए सबसे उपयुक्त शब्द 'abandoned' है।
  • 'Abandoned' शब्द का अर्थ है किसी काम को अधूरा या उद्देश्‍य प्राप्‍त किए बिना बीच में ही रोक देना।
    • उदाहरण: She abandoned law for the fine arts.

explanationEn:

Key Points

  • The given sentence ''He put his soul and energy ___(6)___ his work...​'' is telling something about Pasteur's soul and energy and his work.
  • Let us explore the given options:
    • The preposition 'inside' means in, on, or to the inner part or surface of something.
    • The preposition 'among' means in or concerning a particular group of people or things.
    • The preposition 'into' is used to indicate an object of attention or interest.
    • The preposition 'over' means at a higher level or layer than.

Conclusion: Giving one's hundred percent to the work.

Hence, the correct answer is option 3.

    • Preposition 'over' का अर्थ है तुलना में उच्च स्तर या परत पर।

explanationEn:

Key Points

  • The given sentence "I was walking home from work when I saw someone ___(1)___ I thought was my friend Sandra'' is indicating identifying someone like my friend Sandra.
  • Therefore, the most appropriate word to be filled in the blank is 'who'.
  • The word 'who' is used for saying exactly which person or what kind of person you are talking about.
    • Example: Dhoni is not someone who accepts failure easily.

Hence, the correct answer is option 4.

Complete Sentence: I was walking home from work when I saw someone who I thought was my friend Sandra.
Additional Information

  • Let us explore the other options:
    • 'Such' is used for referring to somebody or something that you mentioned earlier of this or that type. (पूर्व निर्दिष्ट व्‍यक्ति या वस्‍तु का संकेत करने के लिए प्रयुक्त, ऐसा, वैसा)
    • 'Which' is used for saying exactly what thing or things you are talking about. (जो; जो कि, बिल्‍कुल सही वस्‍तु या वस्‍तुओं का निर्देश करने के लिए प्रयुक्त)
    • 'How' means in what way. (किस प्रकार, कैसे)

textHi:

She stared at my face and ___(6)___. To be honest, her face shocked me. She ___(7)___ me of a witch from a children's ___(8)___, with her long nose and staring eyes! ___(9)___ I could realize what she was up to, she ___(10)___ my purse and escaped.

Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank no. 3.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'Surprisingly.'

Key Points

  • The given sentence "___(3)___, when the woman turned around...'' is indicating what happened when a woman turned around.
  • The use of the word ''when'' in the sentence indicates after which, and just then (implying suddenness).
  • Therefore, the most appropriate word to be filled in the blank is 'surprisingly'.
  • The word 'surprisingly' means reasons for doing something.
    • Example: Surprisingly, he agreed straight away.

Hence, the correct answer is option 1.

Complete Sentence: Surprisingly, when the woman turned around...
Additional Information

Let us explore the other options:

  • 'Correctly' means in a way that is true, factual, or appropriate; accurately. (सही तौर पर)
  • 'Gladly' is used for politely agreeing to a request or accepting an invitation. (नम्रतापूर्वक अनुरोध को मानने या निमंत्रण को स्‍वीकार करने के लिए प्रयुक्त; ख़ुशी से)
  • 'Smoothly' means without any difficulty. (बिना किसी बाधा के; निर्विध्न या अबाध रूप से)

Hinglish

  • दिया गया वाक्य "I went up to her and touched her on the arm. ___(3)___...'' यह संकेत दे रहा है कि क्या हुआ जब एक महिला ने मुड़कर देखा।

  • 'Surprisingly' शब्द का अर्थ है आश्‍चर्यजनक रूप से।
    • उदाहरण: Surprisingly, he agreed straight away.

explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'at.'

Key Points

  • The given sentence ''Surprisingly, when the woman turned around, it wasn't Sandra ___(4)___ all!'' is telling something about the woman wasn't Sandra.
  • The phrase ''at all'' means in any way; to any extent.
  • Let us explore the other given options:
    • The preposition 'of' means belonging to, connected with, or part of something or somebody.
    • The preposition 'by' is used for identifying the agent performing an action.
    • The preposition 'with' means in the company of somebody or something.

Conclusion: The woman was not at all Sandra from any angle.

Hence, the correct answer is option 3.

Complete Sentence: Surprisingly, when the woman turned around, it wasn't Sandra at all!

Hinglish

  • दिया गया वाक्य ''Surprisingly, when the woman turned around, it wasn't Sandra ___(4)___ all!'' कुछ बता रही है कि महिला सैंड्रा नहीं थी।
  • वाक्यांश ''at all'' ​​का अर्थ है किसी भी तरह से; किसी भी हद तक।
  • आइए दिए गए विकल्पों का पता लगाएं:
    • Preposition 'of' का अर्थ है किसी व्‍यक्ति या वस्‍तु के स्‍वामित्‍ववाला, से संबंधित या उसका भाग; का।

    • Preposition 'with' का अर्थ है व्‍यक्ति या वस्‍तु के साथ।


Conclusion: महिला किसी भी कोण से सांद्रा नहीं थी।

अत: सही उत्तर विकल्प 3 है ।

textHi:

She stared at my face and ___(6)___. To be honest, her face shocked me. Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank no. 5.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'mistook.'

Key Points

  • The given sentence "I just said, "I am sorry. I ___(5)___ you for a friend"'' is indicating saying sorry, to the girl for misunderstanding and touching her.
  • The use of the word ''for'' in the sentence indicates relating to someone.
  • Therefore, the most appropriate word to be filled in the blank is 'mistook'.
  • The word 'mistook' means to think wrongly that somebody or something is somebody or something else.
    • Example: The doctor mistook the symptoms for blood poisoning.

Hence, the correct answer is option 3.

Complete Sentence: I just said, "I am sorry. I mistook you for a friend".
Additional Information

Let us explore the other options:

  • 'Misjudged' means to form a wrong opinion of somebody or something, usually in a way which is unfair to him/her/it. (किसी व्यक्ति या वस्‍तु के विषय में ग़लत धारणा बना लेना प्रायः अनुचित रूप से)
  • 'Disguised' means to change the appearance, sound, etc. of somebody or something so that people cannot recognize him/her/it. (आकृति, आवाज़ आदि को ऐसे बदल लेना या बदल देना कि पहचाना न जा सके)
  • 'Recognised' means to know again somebody or something that you have seen or heard before. (किसी को पहचानना; पहले देखे या सुने को दुबारा जानना)

Hinglish

  • दिया गया वाक्य "I just said, "I am sorry. I ___(5)___ you for a friend"'' लड़की को दोस्त समझ के छूने के लिए सॉरी कहने का इशारा कर रहा है।
  • वाक्य में 'for' शब्द का प्रयोग किसी से संबंधित होने का संकेत देता है।।
  • इसलिए, रिक्त स्थान की पूर्ति के लिए सर्वाधिक उपयुक्त शब्द 'mistook' है।
  • 'Mistook' शब्द का अर्थ है किसी व्‍यक्ति या वस्‍तु को ग़लती से कोई अन्‍य व्‍यक्ति या वस्‍तु समझ लेना, पहचानने-समझने में ग़लती कर देना।
    • उदाहरण: The doctor mistook the symptoms for blood poisoning.


अत: सही उत्तर विकल्प 3 है।

textEn:
Directions: Read the passage and answer the questions that follow.

The term 'dietary fibres' refers collectively to indigestible carbohydrates present in plant foods. The importance of these dietary fibres came into the picture when it was observed that the people taking a diet rich in these fibres had a low incidence of coronary heart disease, irritable bowel syndrome, dental caries and gall stones.

The foodstuffs rich in these dietary fibres are cereals and grains, legumes, fruits with seeds, citrus fruits, carrots, cabbage, green leafy vegetables, apples, melons, peaches, pears etc. These dietary fibres are not digested by the enzymes of the stomach and the small intestine. They have the property of holding water and because of it, these get swollen and behave like a sponge as these pass through the gastrointestinal tract. The fibres add bulk to the diet and increase transit time in the gut and decrease the time of the release of ingested food in the colon. These fibres hold water so the stools are soft, bulky and readily eliminated.

In recent years, it has been considered essential to have some amount of fibres in the diet. Their beneficial effects lie in preventing heart disease and decreasing cholesterol levels. The fibres like gum and pectin are reported to decrease postprandial (after meals) glucose levels in the blood. They are also recommended for the management of certain types of diabetes. The fibres increase motility of the small intestine and the colon and so there is less time for exposure of the mucosa to harmful toxic substances. Therefore, there is less desire to eat and the energy intake can be maintained within the range of requirements. This phenomenon helps in keeping a check on obesity.

The dietary fibres may have some adverse effects on nutrition by binding some trace metals like calcium, magnesium, phosphorus, zinc and others and therefore preventing their proper absorption. This may pose a possibility of nutritional deficiency especially when diets contain marginal levels of mineral elements. This may put constraints on increasing dietary fibres. It is suggested that an intake of 40-gram dietary fibres per day is desirable.

The dietary fibres behave like a sponge because they:

The fibres add bulk to the diet and increase transit time in the gut and decrease the time of the release of ingested food in the colon. These fibres hold water so the stools are soft, bulky and readily eliminated.

The dietary fibres behave like a sponge because they:
optionsEn: can absorb water and swell up
prevent the absorption of trace metals
increase the motility of the small intestine
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'can absorb water and swell up.'

Key Points

textEn:
Directions: Read the passage and answer the questions that follow.

The term 'dietary fibres' refers collectively to indigestible carbohydrates present in plant foods. The importance of these dietary fibres came into the picture when it was observed that the people taking a diet rich in these fibres had a low incidence of coronary heart disease, irritable bowel syndrome, dental caries and gall stones.

The foodstuffs rich in these dietary fibres are cereals and grains, legumes, fruits with seeds, citrus fruits, carrots, cabbage, green leafy vegetables, apples, melons, peaches, pears etc. These dietary fibres are not digested by the enzymes of the stomach and the small intestine. They have the property of holding water and because of it, these get swollen and behave like a sponge as these pass through the gastrointestinal tract. The fibres add bulk to the diet and increase transit time in the gut and decrease the time of the release of ingested food in the colon. These fibres hold water so the stools are soft, bulky and readily eliminated.

In recent years, it has been considered essential to have some amount of fibres in the diet. Their beneficial effects lie in preventing heart disease and decreasing cholesterol levels. The fibres like gum and pectin are reported to decrease postprandial (after meals) glucose levels in the blood. They are also recommended for the management of certain types of diabetes. The fibres increase motility of the small intestine and the colon and so there is less time for exposure of the mucosa to harmful toxic substances. Therefore, there is less desire to eat and the energy intake can be maintained within the range of requirements. This phenomenon helps in keeping a check on obesity.

The dietary fibres may have some adverse effects on nutrition by binding some trace metals like calcium, magnesium, phosphorus, zinc and others and therefore preventing their proper absorption. This may pose a possibility of nutritional deficiency especially when diets contain marginal levels of mineral elements. This may put constraints on increasing dietary fibres. It is suggested that an intake of 40-gram dietary fibres per day is desirable.

What are dietary fibres?

(तीसरे पैराग्राफ का पहला वाक्य कहता है, हाल के वर्षों में, आहार में कुछ मात्रा में फाइबर का होना आवश्यक माना गया है)
  • From the above sentences, we can say that the theme of the passage is the importance of dietary fibres. (उपरोक्त वाक्यों से हम कह सकते हैं कि परिच्छेद का विषय dietary fibresका महत्व है)

Hence, the only possible answer is option 4.

textEn:
Directions: Read the passage and answer the questions that follow.

When Tao Ying rides on the bus alone, quite often she does not bother to buy a ticket. Why should she? Without her, the bus would still be stopping at every stop, a driver and a conductor would still have to be employed, and the same amount of petrol used. Clearly, Tao Ying has to be astute. When the bus conductor looked like the responsible type, she would buy a ticket as soon as she got on board. But if he appeared to be casual and careless, she would not dream of paying, considering it a small punishment for him and a little saving for herself.

Today she is with her son Xiao Ye. She follows him onto the bus. As the doors shut her jacket is caught, ballooning up like a tent behind her. She twists this way and that, finally wrenching herself free. 'Mama, tickets!' Xiao Ye says. Children are often more conscious of rituals than adults. Without a ticket in his hand, the ride doesn't count as a proper ride. On the peeling paint of the door, somebody has painted the shape of a pale finger. It points at a number: 1.10 m. Between Xiao Ye's round head and the tip of the painted digit setting out the height requirement for a ticket rests the beautiful slender fingers of Tao Ying. 'Xiao Ye, you are not quite tall enough, still one centimetre away,' she tells him softly.

'Mama! I'm tall enough, I'm tall enough!' Xiao Ye shouts at the top of his voice, stamping on the floor as if it were a tin drum. 'You told me the last time I could have a ticket the next time, this is the next time. You don't keep your word!' He looks up at his mother angrily. Tao Ying looks down at her son. A ticket costs twenty cents. Twenty cents is not to be scoffed at. It can buy a cucumber, two tomatoes or, at a reduced price, three bunches of radishes or enough spinach to last four days. But Xiao Ye's face is raised up like a half-open blossom, waiting to receive his promise from the sun. She says, 'Two tickets, please.' The fierce conductor has beady eyes. 'This child is one centimetre short of requiring a ticket.'

Xiao Ye shrinks, not just one but several centimetres— the need for a ticket has all of a sudden become interwoven with the pride of a small child. To be able to purchase self-esteem with twenty cents is something that can only happen in childhood and certainly, no mother can resist an opportunity to make her son happy. 'I would like to buy two tickets,' she says politely.

For the child, the ticket was a symbol of:

  • From the above sentence, we can say that Xiao Ye shrinks, not just one but several centimetres because he feels humiliated and hurt. (उपरोक्त वाक्य से, हम कह सकते हैं कि Xiao Ye सिर्फ एक नहीं बल्कि कई सेंटीमीटर सिकुड़ता है क्योंकि वह अपमानित और आहत महसूस करता है)

Hence, the only possible answer is option 1.

textEn:
Directions: Read the passage and answer the questions that follow.

When Tao Ying rides on the bus alone, quite often she does not bother to buy a ticket. Why should she? Without her, the bus would still be stopping at every stop, a driver and a conductor would still have to be employed, and the same amount of petrol used. Clearly, Tao Ying has to be astute. When the bus conductor looked like the responsible type, she would buy a ticket as soon as she got on board. But if he appeared to be casual and careless, she would not dream of paying, considering it a small punishment for him and a little saving for herself.

Today she is with her son Xiao Ye. She follows him onto the bus. As the doors shut her jacket is caught, ballooning up like a tent behind her. She twists this way and that, finally wrenching herself free. 'Mama, tickets!' Xiao Ye says. Children are often more conscious of rituals than adults. Without a ticket in his hand, the ride doesn't count as a proper ride. On the peeling paint of the door, somebody has painted the shape of a pale finger. It points at a number: 1.10 m. Between Xiao Ye's round head and the tip of the painted digit setting out the height requirement for a ticket rests the beautiful slender fingers of Tao Ying. 'Xiao Ye, you are not quite tall enough, still one centimetre away,' she tells him softly.

'Mama! I'm tall enough, I'm tall enough!' Xiao Ye shouts at the top of his voice, stamping on the floor as if it were a tin drum. 'You told me the last time I could have a ticket the next time, this is the next time. You don't keep your word!' He looks up at his mother angrily. Tao Ying looks down at her son. A ticket costs twenty cents. Twenty cents is not to be scoffed at. It can buy a cucumber, two tomatoes or, at a reduced price, three bunches of radishes or enough spinach to last four days. But Xiao Ye's face is raised up like a half-open blossom, waiting to receive his promise from the sun. She says, 'Two tickets, please.' The fierce conductor has beady eyes. 'This child is one centimetre short of requiring a ticket.'

Xiao Ye shrinks, not just one but several centimetres— the need for a ticket has all of a sudden become interwoven with the pride of a small child. To be able to purchase self-esteem with twenty cents is something that can only happen in childhood and certainly, no mother can resist an opportunity to make her son happy. 'I would like to buy two tickets,' she says politely.

Which of the following statements is NOT true?

textEn:
Directions: Read the passage and answer the questions that follow.

The method of embalming, or treating the dead body, that the ancient Egyptians used is called 'mummification'. Using special processes, the Egyptians removed all moisture from the body, leaving only a dried form that would not easily decay. It was important in their religion to preserve the dead body in as life-like a manner as possible.

The mummification process took seventy days. Special priests worked as embalmers, treating and wrapping the body. Beyond knowing the correct rituals and prayers to be performed at various stages, the priests also needed a detailed knowledge of human anatomy. The first step in the process was the removal of all internal parts that might decay rapidly. The brain was removed by carefully inserting special hooked instruments up through the nostrils in order to pull out bits of brain tissue. The embalmers then removed the organs of the abdomen and chest through a cut usually made on the left side of the abdomen. They left only the heart in place, believing it to be the center of a person's being and intelligence. The other organs were preserved separately, with the stomach, liver, lungs, and intestines placed in special boxes or jars, today called 'canopic jars'. These were buried with the mummy.

The embalmers next removed all moisture from the body. This they did by covering the body with 'natron', a type of salt that has great drying properties, and by placing additional natron packets inside the body. When the body had dried out completely, embalmers removed the internal packets and lightly washed the natron off the body. The result was a very dried-out but recognizable human form. To make the mummy seem even more life-like, sunken areas of the body were filled out with linen, and other materials and false eyes were added. Next, the wrapping began. Each mummy needed hundreds of yards of linen. The priests carefully wound the long strips of linen around the body, sometimes even wrapping each finger and toe separately before wrapping the entire hand or foot. In order to protect the dead from mishaps in the afterlife, amulets were placed among the wrappings and prayers and magical words written on some of the linen strips. Often the priests placed a mask of the person's face between the layers of head bandages. At last, the priests wrapped the final cloth or shroud in place and secured it with linen strips. The mummy was complete. It was then placed in the tomb along with food, furniture, vessels, perfumes, jewels, and other things required in the afterlife.

Mummification was done to:

They left only the heart in place, believing it to be the center of a person's being and intelligence. The other organs were preserved separately, with the stomach, liver, lungs, and intestines placed in special boxes or jars, today called 'canopic jars'. These were buried with the mummy.

The embalmers next removed all moisture from the body. This they did by covering the body with 'natron', a type of salt that has great drying properties, and by placing additional natron packets inside the body. When the body had dried out completely, embalmers removed the internal packets and lightly washed the natron off the body. The result was a very dried-out but recognizable human form. To make the mummy seem even more life-like, sunken areas of the body were filled out with linen, and other materials and false eyes were added. Next, the wrapping began. Each mummy needed hundreds of yards of linen. The priests carefully wound the long strips of linen around the body, sometimes even wrapping each finger and toe separately before wrapping the entire hand or foot. In order to protect the dead from mishaps in the afterlife, amulets were placed among the wrappings and prayers and magical words written on some of the linen strips. Often the priests placed a mask of the person's face between the layers of head bandages. At last, the priests wrapped the final cloth or shroud in place and secured it with linen strips. The mummy was complete. It was then placed in the tomb along with food, furniture, vessels, perfumes, jewels, and other things required in the afterlife.

What was the initial step in the process of mummification?
optionsEn: Placing a face mask between the head bandages
Wrapping the body with strips of linen
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'Removal of the internal parts.'

Key Points

  • The fourth sentence of the second paragraph says "The first step in the process was the removal of all internal parts that might decay rapidly''. (दूसरे पैराग्राफ का चौथा वाक्य कहता है, इस प्रक्रिया में पहला कदम उन सभी आंतरिक भागों को हटाना था जो तेजी से क्षय हो सकते हैं)
  • From the above sentence, we can say that the initial step in the process of mummification was the removal of the internal parts. (उपरोक्त वाक्य से, हम कह सकते हैं कि ममीकरण की प्रक्रिया में प्रारंभिक चरण आंतरिक भागों को हटाना था)

Hence, the only possible answer is option 4.

textEn:
Directions: Read the passage and answer the questions that follow.

Vasco Da Gama was the first European to find an ocean trading route to India. He accomplished what many explorers before him could not do. His discovery of this sea route helped the Portuguese establish a long-lasting colonial empire in Asia and Africa. The new ocean route around Africa allowed Portuguese sailors to avoid the Arab trading hold in the Mediterranean and Middle East. Vasco da Gama opened a new world of riches by opening up an Indian Ocean route. His voyage and explorations helped change the world for Europeans. Vasco da Gama's maritime career was during the period when Portugal was searching for a trade route around Africa to India. The Ottoman Empire controlled almost all European trade routes to Asia. This meant they could, and did, charge high prices for ships passing through ports. When Manuel I became King of Portugal in 1495, he continued efforts to open a trade route to India by going around Africa. Although other people were considered for the job, Manuel I finally chose 37-year-old Vasco da Gama for this task.

On 8 July 1497, Vasco da Gama sailed from Lisbon with a fleet of four ships and a crew of 170 men. Da Gama commanded the Sao Gabriel. Paulo da Gama – brother to Vasco – commanded the São Rafael, a three-mast ship. They sailed past the Canary Islands, and reached the Cape Verde islands, and then continued sailing, though storms still delayed them for a while. They rounded the Cape of Good Hope on 22 November and anchored at Mossel Bay, South Africa. They began sailing again and continued until they reached the Rio dos Bons Sinais (River of Good Omens). Here they erected a statue in the name of Portugal.

They stayed here for a month because much of the crew were sick from scurvy – a disease caused by lack of Vitamin C. Da Gama's fleet eventually began sailing again. Finally, on 20 May 1498, they reached India. They headed for Kappad, near the large city of Calicut. In Calicut, da Gama met with the king. But the king of Calicut was not impressed with da Gama, and the gifts he brought as offerings. They spent several months trading in India and studying their customs. They left India at the end of August.

In which month did Vasco da Gama's ship reach the Cape of Good Hope?

On 8 July 1497, Vasco da Gama sailed from Lisbon with a fleet of four ships and a crew of 170 men. Da Gama commanded the Sao Gabriel. Paulo da Gama – brother to Vasco – commanded the São Rafael, a three-mast ship. They sailed past the Canary Islands, and reached the Cape Verde islands, and then continued sailing, though storms still delayed them for a while. They rounded the Cape of Good Hope on 22 November and anchored at Mossel Bay, South Africa. They began sailing again and continued until they reached the Rio dos Bons Sinais (River of Good Omens). Here they erected a statue in the name of Portugal.

They stayed here for a month because much of the crew were sick from scurvy – a disease caused by lack of Vitamin C. Da Gama's fleet eventually began sailing again. Finally, on 20 May 1498, they reached India. They headed for Kappad, near the large city of Calicut. In Calicut, da Gama met with the king. But the king of Calicut was not impressed with da Gama, and the gifts he brought as offerings. They spent several months trading in India and studying their customs. They left India at the end of August.

In which month did Vasco da Gama's ship reach the Cape of Good Hope?
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'November.'

Key Points

  • The fifth sentence of the second paragraph says "They rounded the Cape of Good Hope on 22 November and anchored at Mossel Bay, South Africa''. (दूसरे पैराग्राफ का पाँचवाँ वाक्य कहता है, उन्होंने 22 नवंबर को केप ऑफ गुड होप का चक्कर लगाया और दक्षिण अफ्रीका के मोसेल बे में लंगर डाला)
  • From the above sentence, we can say that Vasco da Gama's ship reached the Cape of Good Hope in November. (उपरोक्त वाक्य से हम कह सकते हैं कि वास्को डी गामा का जहाज नवंबर में केप ऑफ गुड होप पहुंचा था)

Hence, the only possible answer is option 3.

textEn:
Directions: Read the passage and answer the questions that follow.

Vasco Da Gama was the first European to find an ocean trading route to India. He accomplished what many explorers before him could not do. His discovery of this sea route helped the Portuguese establish a long-lasting colonial empire in Asia and Africa. The new ocean route around Africa allowed Portuguese sailors to avoid the Arab trading hold in the Mediterranean and Middle East. Vasco da Gama opened a new world of riches by opening up an Indian Ocean route. His voyage and explorations helped change the world for Europeans. Vasco da Gama's maritime career was during the period when Portugal was searching for a trade route around Africa to India. The Ottoman Empire controlled almost all European trade routes to Asia. This meant they could, and did, charge high prices for ships passing through ports. When Manuel I became King of Portugal in 1495, he continued efforts to open a trade route to India by going around Africa. Although other people were considered for the job, Manuel I finally chose 37-year-old Vasco da Gama for this task.

On 8 July 1497, Vasco da Gama sailed from Lisbon with a fleet of four ships and a crew of 170 men. Da Gama commanded the Sao Gabriel. Paulo da Gama – brother to Vasco – commanded the São Rafael, a three-mast ship. They sailed past the Canary Islands, and reached the Cape Verde islands, and then continued sailing, though storms still delayed them for a while. They rounded the Cape of Good Hope on 22 November and anchored at Mossel Bay, South Africa. They began sailing again and continued until they reached the Rio dos Bons Sinais (River of Good Omens). Here they erected a statue in the name of Portugal.

They stayed here for a month because much of the crew were sick from scurvy – a disease caused by lack of Vitamin C. Da Gama's fleet eventually began sailing again. Finally, on 20 May 1498, they reached India. They headed for Kappad, near the large city of Calicut. In Calicut, da Gama met with the king. But the king of Calicut was not impressed with da Gama, and the gifts he brought as offerings. They spent several months trading in India and studying their customs. They left India at the end of August.

Which of the following statements is NOT true?

Finally, on 20 May 1498, they reached India. They headed for Kappad, near the large city of Calicut. In Calicut, da Gama met with the king. But the king of Calicut was not impressed with da Gama, and the gifts he brought as offerings. They spent several months trading in India and studying their customs. They left India at the end of August.

Why did Vasco da Gama's crew stay at Rio dos Bons Sinais for a month?
optionsEn: To erect a statue in the name of Portugal
Several crew members were suffering from scurvy.
There were severe storms in the area.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'Several crew members were suffering from scurvy.'

Key Points

  • The first sentence of the last paragraph says "They stayed here for a month because much of the crew were sick from scurvy – a disease caused by lack of Vitamin C''. (अंतिम पैराग्राफ का पहला वाक्य कहता है, वे यहां एक महीने तक रहे क्योंकि अधिकांश चालक दल स्कर्वी से बीमार थे - विटामिन सी की कमी के कारण होने वाली बीमारी)
  • From the above sentence, we can say that Vasco da Gama's crew stayed at Rio dos Bons Sinais for a month because several crew members were suffering from scurvy. (उपरोक्त वाक्य से हम कह सकते हैं कि वास्को डी गामा का दल एक महीने के लिए रियो डॉस बोन्स सिनाईस में रुका था क्योंकि चालक दल के कई सदस्य स्कर्वी से पीड़ित थे)

Hence, the only possible answer is option 2.

textEn:
Directions: Read the passage and answer the questions that follow.

My grandmother and I were good friends. My parents left me with her when they went to live in the city and we were constantly together. She used to wake me up in the morning and get me ready for school. She said her morning prayer in a monotonous sing-song while she bathed and dressed me in the hope that I would listen and get to know it by heart; I listened because I loved her voice but never bothered to learn it. Then she would fetch my wooden slate which she had already washed and plastered with yellow chalk, a tiny earthen ink-pot, and a red pen, tie them all in a bundle and hand it to me. After a breakfast of a thick, stale chapatti with a little butter and sugar spread on it, we went to school. She carried several stale chapattis with her for the village dogs.

My grandmother always went to school with me because the school was attached to the temple. The priest taught us the alphabet and the morning prayer. While the children sat in rows on either side of the verandah singing the alphabet or the prayer in a chorus, my grandmother sat inside reading the scriptures. When we had both finished, we would walk back together. This time the village dogs would meet us at the temple door. They followed us to our home growling and fighting with each other for the chapattis we threw to them. When my parents were comfortably settled in the city, they sent for us. That was a turning point in our friendship. Although we shared the same room, my grandmother no longer came to school with me. I used to go to an English school on a motor bus. There were no dogs in the streets and she took to feeding sparrows in the courtyard of our city house. As the years rolled by we saw less of each other. For some time she continued to wake me up and get me ready for school. When I came back she would ask me what the teacher had taught me. I would tell her English words and little things of western science and learning, the law of gravity, Archimedes' Principle, the world is round, etc. This made her unhappy. She could not help me with my lessons. She did not believe in the things they taught at the English school and was distressed that there was no teaching about God and the scriptures.

Why did grandmother carry stale chapattis with her?

That was a turning point in our friendship. Although we shared the same room, my grandmother no longer came to school with me. I used to go to an English school on a motor bus. There were no dogs in the streets and she took to feeding sparrows in the courtyard of our city house. As the years rolled by we saw less of each other. For some time she continued to wake me up and get me ready for school. When I came back she would ask me what the teacher had taught me. I would tell her English words and little things of western science and learning, the law of gravity, Archimedes' Principle, the world is round, etc. This made her unhappy. She could not help me with my lessons. She did not believe in the things they taught at the English school and was distressed that there was no teaching about God and the scriptures.

What kind of woman was the grandmother?

Hence, the only possible answer is option 4.

textEn:
Directions: In the following passage some words have been deleted. Fill in the blanks with the help of the alternatives given. Select the most appropriate option for each number.

'Make in India' is a call to the global manufacturers ___(1)___ at making India a manufacturing ___(2)___. It is a national program designed ___(3)___ facilitate investment, foster innovation, enhance skill ___(4)___ and build best-in-class manufacturing ___(5)___. The program lays emphasis on various sectors with a focus on job creation.

Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank no. 1.
textHi:
Directions: In the following passage some words have been deleted. Fill in the blanks with the help of the alternatives given. Select the most appropriate option for each number.

'Make in India' is a call to the global manufacturers ___(1)___ at making India a manufacturing ___(2)___. It is a national program designed ___(3)___ facilitate investment, foster innovation, enhance skill ___(4)___ and build best-in-class manufacturing ___(5)___. The program lays emphasis on various sectors with a focus on job creation.

Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank no. 1.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'aimed.'

Key Points

  • The given sentence "'Make in India' is a call to the global manufacturers ___(1)___ at making India​...'' is indicating something about ''Make in India''.
  • The use of the word ''making'' in the sentence indicates the act of doing or producing something.
  • Therefore, the most appropriate word to be filled in the blank is 'aimed'.
  • The word 'aimed' means to intend to do or achieve something.
    • Example: It was a new production technique aimed at minimizing wastage.

Hence, the correct answer is option 2.

Complete Sentence: 'Make in India' is a call to the global manufacturers aimed at making India​...
Additional Information

  • Let us explore the other options:
    • 'Assisted' means to help. (सहायता करना)
    • 'Managed' means to succeed in doing or dealing with something difficult; to be able to do something. (कोई कठिन काम करने या निपटाने में सफल होना; कुछ कर सकना)
    • 'Conducted' means to organize and do something. (व्‍यवस्थित रूप से कुछ करना)

Hinglish

  • दिया गया वाक्य "'Make in India' is a call to the global manufacturers ___(1)___ at making India​...'' 'मेक इन इंडिया' के बारे में कुछ संकेत दे रहा है।
  • वाक्य में ''making'' शब्द का प्रयोग कुछ करने या उत्पन्न करने की क्रिया को इंगित करता है।
  • इसलिए, रिक्त स्थान में भरने के लिए सबसे उपयुक्त शब्द 'aimed' है।
  • 'Aimed' शब्द का अर्थ है कोई काम करने या कुछ प्राप्‍त करने का इरादा रखना; लक्ष्‍य में रखना, उद्देश्‍य बनाना।
    • उदाहरण: It was a new production technique aimed at minimizing wastage.


अत: सही उत्तर विकल्प 2 है।

textHi:
Directions: In the following passage some words have been deleted. Fill in the blanks with the help of the alternatives given. Select the most appropriate option for each number.

'Make in India' is a call to the global manufacturers ___(1)___ at making India a manufacturing ___(2)___. It is a national program designed ___(3)___ facilitate investment, foster innovation, enhance skill ___(4)___ and build best-in-class manufacturing ___(5)___. The program lays emphasis on various sectors with a focus on job creation.

Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank no. 3.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'to.'

Key Points

  • The given sentence ''It is a national program designed ___(3)___ facilitate investment...'' is telling something about a program designed to make the investment easier.
  • Let us explore the given options:
    • The preposition 'for' means belonging to, connected with, or part of something or somebody.
    • The preposition 'at' is used to show where somebody or something is or where something happens.
    • The preposition 'to' is used for showing the purpose of an action.
    • The preposition 'with' means in the company of somebody or something.

Conclusion: A national program designed to make investment easier in India.

Hence, the correct answer is option 3.

Complete Sentence: It is a national program designed to facilitate investment...

Hinglish

  • दिया गया वाक्य ''It is a national program designed ___(3)___ facilitate investment ...'' निवेश को आसान बनाने के लिए डिज़ाइन किए गए प्रोग्राम के बारे में कुछ बता रहा है।
  • आइए दिए गए विकल्पों का पता लगाएं:
    • Preposition 'for' का अर्थ है किसी चीज़ या किसी से संबंधित, उससे जुड़ा या उसका हिस्सा।
    • Preposition 'at' का प्रयोग यह दिखाने के लिए किया जाता है कि कोई व्यक्ति या वस्तु कहाँ है या कहाँ कुछ घटित होता है।
    • Preposition 'to' का प्रयोग क्रिया के उद्देश्य को दर्शाने के लिए किया जाता है।
    • Preposition 'with' का अर्थ है किसी या किसी चीज़ की संगति में।


Conclusion: भारत में निवेश को आसान बनाने के लिए बनाया गया एक राष्ट्रीय कार्यक्रम।

अत: सही उत्तर विकल्प 3 है ।

पुरा वाक्य: It is a national program designed to facilitate investment...
explanationHi:
The correct answer is 'to.'

Key Points

  • The given sentence ''It is a national program designed ___(3)___ facilitate investment...'' is telling something about a program designed to make the investment easier.
  • Let us explore the given options:
    • The preposition 'for' means belonging to, connected with, or part of something or somebody.
    • The preposition 'at' is used to show where somebody or something is or where something happens.
    • The preposition 'to' is used for showing the purpose of an action.
    • The preposition 'with' means in the company of somebody or something.

Conclusion: A national program designed to make investment easier in India.

Hence, the correct answer is option 3.

Complete Sentence: It is a national program designed to facilitate investment...

Hinglish

  • दिया गया वाक्य ''It is a national program designed ___(3)___ facilitate investment ...'' निवेश को आसान बनाने के लिए डिज़ाइन किए गए प्रोग्राम के बारे में कुछ बता रहा है।
  • आइए दिए गए विकल्पों का पता लगाएं:
    • Preposition 'for' का अर्थ है किसी चीज़ या किसी से संबंधित, उससे जुड़ा या उसका हिस्सा।
    • Preposition 'at' का प्रयोग यह दिखाने के लिए किया जाता है कि कोई व्यक्ति या वस्तु कहाँ है या कहाँ कुछ घटित होता है।
    • Preposition 'to' का प्रयोग क्रिया के उद्देश्य को दर्शाने के लिए किया जाता है।
    • Preposition 'with' का अर्थ है किसी या किसी चीज़ की संगति में।


Conclusion: भारत में निवेश को आसान बनाने के लिए बनाया गया एक राष्ट्रीय कार्यक्रम।

अत: सही उत्तर विकल्प 3 है ।

पुरा वाक्य: It is a national program designed to facilitate investment...

textEn:
Select the correct active form of the given sentence.

Have all the bills been verified by someone?
textHi:
Select the correct active form of the given sentence.

Have all the bills been verified by someone?
optionsEn: Has someone verified all the bills?
Have someone verified all the bills?
Has someone verify all the bills?
Is someone verifying all the bills?
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'Has someone verified all the bills?'

Key Points

  • In Active Voice, a sentence emphasizes the subject, performing an action.
  • In Passive Voice, a sentence emphasizes the action or the object of the sentence.
  • The given sentence is in the passive voice and it is in the present perfect tense.
  • When we convert this sentence into an active voice, the subject 'all the bills' of the passive voice becomes the object, the object 'someone' becomes the subject.
  • The passive format "have + been + V3 (verified)" should be converted into the active format "has + V3 (verified)".
  • This is the active and passive voice rule for the present perfect tense.


Hence, the correct answer is option 1.

Additional Information

Verbs of passive format have a different set of tenses (*V3 - Past participle or Third form of the Verb) as given below in the table:

TENSE ACTIVE PASSIVE PASSIVE CONVERSION
Present Simple He plays football am/is/are + V3 Football is played by him
Present Continuous He is playing football am/is/are + being +V3 Football is being played by him
Present Perfect He has played football Has/have + been+ V3 Football has been played by him
Present Perfect Continuous He has been playing football Not used and unusual. Not used and unusual
Past Simple He played football Was/were + V3 Football was played by him
Past Continuous He was playing football Was/were + being + V3 Football was being played by him
Past Perfect He had played football Had + been + V3 Football had been played by him
Past Perfect Continuous He had been playing football Not used and unusual Not used and unusual
Future Simple He will play football Will + be + V3 Football will be played by him
Future Continuous He will be playing football Not used and unusual Not used and unusual
Future Perfect He will have played football Will have been + V3 Football will have been played by him
Future Perfect Continuous He will have been playing football Not used and unusual Not used and unusual


Hinglish

  • Active Voice में, एक वाक्य विषय पर जोर देता है, एक क्रिया करता है।
  • Passive Voice में, एक वाक्य वाक्य की क्रिया या वस्तु पर जोर देता है।
  • दिया गया वाक्य passive voice में है और यह present perfect tense में है।
  • Passive format "have + been + V3 (verified)" को active format "has + V3 (verified)" में परिवर्तित किया जाना चाहिए।
  • यह present perfect tense के लिए active and passive voice rule है।

अतः सही उत्तर विकल्प 1 है।

The correct answer is 'Has someone verified all the bills?'

textHi:
Directions: Select the most appropriate one-word substitution for the given group of words.

To give one's authority to another
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'delegate.'

Key Points

  • Let us explore the marked option:
    • 'Delegate' is to give one's authority to another. (किसी एक व्यक्ति का अधिकार, कार्य या जिम्मेदारी दूसरे को सौंपना)
      • For example - He plans to delegate more authority to his deputies.

Hence, the correct answer is option 2.

Additional Information

  • Let us explore the other options:
    • 'Shirk' means to avoid doing something that is difficult or unpleasant, especially because you are too lazy. (कठिन या अप्रिय काम से जी चुराना; टालना)
      • For example - The Government will not shirk from considering the need for further action.
    • 'Usurp' means to take somebody's position and power without having the right to do this. (किसी के पद और या शक्ति को हड़प लेना)
      • For example - The vice - president is trying to usurp the president's authority.
    • 'Supervise' means to watch somebody or something to make sure that work is being done properly or that people are behaving correctly. (देखभाल करना, निगरानी करना)
      • For example - An executive officer was appointed to supervise the arrangements.

textEn:
Directions: Select the most appropriate option to substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. If there is no need to substitute it, select 'No substitution'.

I wished I have tried more harder.
textHi:
Directions: Select the most appropriate option to substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. If there is no need to substitute it, select 'No substitution'.

I wished I have tried more harder.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'had tried harder.'

Key Points

  • In the underlined segment, the use of the present form of the verb 'have' is incorrect and the use of the word 'more' is superfluous or not required.
  • To refer to a past wish (here, wished), we use a past perfect verb after the object and to form the past perfect, use 'had' followed by a past participle verb.
  • Therefore, the past tense form of the verb 'had' should be used in place of the verb 'have'.
  • Also, the comparative form of the adverb 'hard' is 'harder', not ''more harder''.
  • Therefore, the comparative form 'harder' should be used in place of ''more harder''.

Hence, the correct answer is option 3.

Correct Sentence: I wished I had tried harder.

Hinglish

  • रेखांकित खण्ड में 'have' क्रिया के वर्तमान रूप का प्रयोग गलत है और 'more' शब्द का प्रयोग अतिश्योक्तिपूर्ण है या आवश्यक नहीं है।
  • एक अतीत की इच्छा (यहाँ, wished) को संदर्भित करने के लिए, हम वस्तु के बाद एक past perfect verb का उपयोग करते हैं और past perfect बनाने के लिए, 'had' का उपयोग करते हैं, उसके बाद एक past participle verb का उपयोग करते हैं।
  • इसलिए, past tense form of the verb 'had' का प्रयोग verb 'have' के स्थान पर किया जाना चाहिए।
  • साथ ही, comparative form of the adverb 'hard', 'harder' है, न कि 'more harder'।
  • इसलिए, 'more harder' के स्थान पर comparative form 'harder' का प्रयोग किया जाना चाहिए।

अत: सही उत्तर विकल्प 3 है।

सही वाक्य: I wished I had tried harder.

  • Therefore, the past tense form of the verb 'had' should be used in place of the verb 'have'.
  • Also, the comparative form of the adverb 'hard' is 'harder', not ''more harder''.
  • Therefore, the comparative form 'harder' should be used in place of ''more harder''.

Hence, the correct answer is option 3.

Correct Sentence: I wished I had tried harder.

Hinglish

  • रेखांकित खण्ड में 'have' क्रिया के वर्तमान रूप का प्रयोग गलत है और 'more' शब्द का प्रयोग अतिश्योक्तिपूर्ण है या आवश्यक नहीं है।
  • एक अतीत की इच्छा (यहाँ, wished) को संदर्भित करने के लिए, हम वस्तु के बाद एक past perfect verb का उपयोग करते हैं और past perfect बनाने के लिए, 'had' का उपयोग करते हैं, उसके बाद एक past participle verb का उपयोग करते हैं।
  • इसलिए, past tense form of the verb 'had' का प्रयोग verb 'have' के स्थान पर किया जाना चाहिए।
  • साथ ही, comparative form of the adverb 'hard', 'harder' है, न कि 'more harder'।
  • इसलिए, 'more harder' के स्थान पर comparative form 'harder' का प्रयोग किया जाना चाहिए।

अत: सही उत्तर विकल्प 3 है।

सही वाक्य: I wished I had tried harder.

textEn:

You ______ carry all the books to school daily.
textHi:
Directions: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank.

You ______ carry all the books to school daily.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'need not.'

Key Points

  • The given sentence is saying something related to carrying books to school. (दिया गया वाक्य स्कूल में किताबें ले जाने से संबंधित कुछ कह रहा है)
  • Need not means something not needed. (किसी चीज की जरूरत ना होना)
    • Example: You need not worry about anything.

Conclusion: No need of carrying all the books to school daily. (रोजाना स्कूल में किताबें ना ले जाने की जरूरत)

Hence, the correct answer is option 4.

Complete Sentence: You need not carry all the books to school daily.

Additional Information

  • Let us explore the other options:
    • ''Ought not'' is used to indicate what is not expected. (क्या अपेक्षित नहीं है)
    • ''Would not'' is used to say that someone refused to do something. (किसी ने कुछ करने से मना कर दिया)

textEn:
Directions: Select the correct direct form of the given sentence.

He told you that he had not known that it was your birthday that day.
textHi:
Directions: Select the correct direct form of the given sentence.

He told you that he had not known that it was your birthday that day.
optionsEn: He said to you, "You did not know that it was my birthday today."
He said to you, "I did not know that it is your birthday today."
He said to you, "I do not know that it was your birthday that day."
You said to him, "I do not know that it is your birthday today."
explanationEn:
The correct answer is He said to you, "I did not know that it is your birthday today."

Key Points

  • The given sentence is an indirect speech.
  • The basic rules for changing or converting indirect speech into direct speech:​
    • The commas, inverted commas are added, and 'that' is removed, 'told' is replaced by 'said to'.
    • The third-person pronoun 'he' will be changed into the first-person pronoun 'I' and ''that day'' becomes 'today'.
    • The past perfect tense format 'Subject + had + V3 (known) + Object' will be changed into the past simple tense format 'Subject + V2 (did + know) + Object'.
    • The past simple tense format 'Subject + V2 (was) + Object' will be changed into the present simple tense format 'Subject + V1 (is) + Object'.
  • From the following rules, we get the correct sentence as option 2.

Additional Information

  • The following table will enable us to know how tense changes into Indirect speech:
    Direct Speech Indirect Speech
    Present simple (Subject +V1st + Object) Past simple (Subject +V2 + Object)
    Present continuous (Subject +is/am/are+V1 +ing+ Object) Past Continuous (Subject +was/were+V1 +ing+ Object)
    Present perfect (Subject + has/have+V3+Object) Past perfect (Subject+had+V3+Object)
    Past simple (Subject+V2+Object) Past perfect (Subject+had+V3+Object)
    Past Continuous (Subject +was/were+V1 +ing+ Object) Past perfect continuous (Subject +had been+V1 +ing+ Object)
    Future simple (Subject+ will/shall+V1+object) Present Conditional (Subject+ would+V1+object)
    Future Continuous (Subject +will/shall+be+V1 +ing+ Object) Conditional Continuous (Subject +would+be+V1 +ing+ Object)

Hinglish

  • दिया गया वाक्य indirect speech है।
  • Indirect speech को direct speech में बदलने या बदलने के लिए बुनियादी नियम:
    • Commas, inverted commas जोड़े जाते हैं, और 'that' हटा दिया जाता है, 'said to' को 'told' से बदल दिया जाता है।
    • Third-person pronoun 'he', first-person pronoun 'I' बन जाता है और 'that day', 'today' बन जाता है।
    • Past perfect tense format 'Subject + had + V3 (known) + Object' को past simple tense format 'Subject + V2 (did + know) + Object' में बदल दिया जाएगा।
    • Past simple tense format 'Subject + V2 (was) + Object' को present simple tense format 'Subject + V1 (is) + Object' में बदल दिया जाएगा।

अत: सही उत्तर विकल्प 2 है।

textEn:
Directions: Select the most appropriate meaning of the underlined idiom in the given sentence.

He runs for two hours at a stretch to build his stamina.
textHi:
Directions: Select the most appropriate meaning of the underlined idiom in the given sentence.

He runs for two hours at a stretch to build his stamina.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'continuously.'

Key Points

  • Given Idiom: At a stretch means continuously; without stopping. (लगातार; बिना रुके)
    • Example: She worked for six hours at a stretch.
  • From the given options, the fourth option is the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom.

Hence, the correct answer is option 4.

Mistake Points

  • We may think from the phrase "At a stretch" that it is all about straightening or extending one's body or a part of one's body.

Additional Information

  • This idiom, first recorded in 1774, does not imply being seated while engaging in a single continuous activity. Rather, it transfers the meaning of stretch as ''a continuous length'' to ''a continuous time period.''

textEn:
Directions: Select the correct active form of the given sentence.

The entire area was being given a face-lift.
textHi:
Directions: Select the correct active form of the given sentence.

The entire area was being given a face-lift.
optionsEn: They were giving a face-lift to the entire area.
They will be giving a face-lift to the entire area.
They have given a face-lift to the entire area.
They are giving a face-lift to the entire area.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'They were giving a face-lift to the entire area.'

Key Points

  • In Active Voice, a sentence emphasizes the subject, performing an action.
  • In Passive Voice, a sentence emphasizes the action or the object of the sentence.
  • The given sentence is in the passive voice and it is in the past continuous tense.
  • When we convert this sentence into an active voice, the subject 'The entire area' of the passive voice becomes the object, the hidden object 'them' becomes the subject 'They'.
  • The passive format "was + being + V3 (given)" should be converted into the active format "were + present participle form of the verb (giving)".
  • This is the active and passive voice rule for the past continuous tense.


Hence, the correct answer is option 1.

Future Continuous He will be playing football Not used and unusual Not used and unusual
Future Perfect He will have played football Will have been + V3 Football will have been played by him
Future Perfect Continuous He will have been playing football Not used and unusual Not used and unusual

  • Passive format "was + being + V3 (given)" को active format "were + present participle form of the verb (giving)" में परिवर्तित किया जाना चाहिए।
  • यह past continuous tense के लिए active and passive voice rule है।

अतः सही उत्तर विकल्प 1 है।

textEn:
Directions: Select the correct indirect form of the given sentence.

She said, "I will finish reading the novel today."
textHi:
Directions: Select the correct indirect form of the given sentence.

She said, "I will finish reading the novel today."
optionsEn: She said that she will finished reading the novel that day.
She said she will finish reading the novel today.
She said that she would finish reading the novel that day.
She said that I would finish reading the novel today.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is She said that she would finish reading the novel that day.

Key Points

  • The given sentence is a direct speech.
  • The basic rules for changing or converting direct speech into indirect speech:​
    • The commas, inverted commas are removed, and 'that' is added.
    • The first-person pronoun 'I' becomes the third-person pronoun 'she' and 'today' becomes ''that day''.
    • The present simple tense format 'Subject + V1 (will) + Object' will be changed into the past simple tense format 'Subject + V2 (would) + Object'.

Hence, the correct answer is option 3.

Confusion Points

  • You may think reporting verb ''would finish'' should be used.
  • Option 3 & 4 are wrong because 'would finish' is used in the present conditional tense of the indirect speech.
  • Thus, the correct answer is option 3.

Additional Information

  • The following table will enable us to know how tense changes into Indirect speech:-
    Direct Speech Indirect Speech
    Present simple (Subject +V1st + Object) Past simple (Subject +V2 + Object)
    Present continuous (Subject +is/am/are+V1 +ing+ Object) Past Continuous (Subject +was/were+V1 +ing+ Object)
    Present perfect (Subject + has/have+V3+Object) Past perfect (Subject+had+V3+Object)
    Past simple (Subject+V2+Object) Past perfect (Subject+had+V3+Object)
    Past Continuous (Subject +was/were+V1+ing+ Object) Past perfect continuous (Subject+had been+V1 +ing+ Object)
    Future simple (Subject+ will/shall+V1+object) Present Conditional (Subject+would+V1+object)
    Future Continuous (Subject +will/shall+be+V1 +ing+ Object) Conditional Continuous (Subject +would+be+V1 +ing+ Object)

Hinglish

  • दिया गया वाक्य direct speech है।
  • Direct speech को indirect speech में बदलने या बदलने के लिए बुनियादी नियम:
    • Commas, inverted commas हटा दिए जाते हैं और 'that' जोड़ा जाता है।
    • First-person pronoun 'I', third-person pronoun 'she' बन जाता है और 'today', 'that day' बन जाता है।
    • Present simple tense format 'Subject + V1 (will) + Object' को past simple tense format 'Subject + V2 (would) + Object' में बदल दिया जाएगा।

अत: सही उत्तर विकल्प 3 है।
explanationHi:
The correct answer is She said that she would finish reading the novel that day.

Key Points

  • The given sentence is a direct speech.
  • The basic rules for changing or converting direct speech into indirect speech:​
    • The commas, inverted commas are removed, and 'that' is added.
    • The first-person pronoun 'I' becomes the third-person pronoun 'she' and 'today' becomes ''that day''.
    • The present simple tense format 'Subject + V1 (will) + Object' will be changed into the past simple tense format 'Subject + V2 (would) + Object'.

Hence, the correct answer is option 3.

Confusion Points

  • You may think reporting verb ''would finish'' should be used.
  • Option 3 & 4 are wrong because 'would finish' is used in the present conditional tense of the indirect speech.
  • Thus, the correct answer is option 3.

Additional Information

  • The following table will enable us to know how tense changes into Indirect speech:-
    Direct Speech Indirect Speech
    Present simple (Subject +V1st + Object) Past simple (Subject +V2 + Object)
    Present continuous (Subject +is/am/are+V1 +ing+ Object) Past Continuous (Subject +was/were+V1 +ing+ Object)
    Present perfect (Subject + has/have+V3+Object) Past perfect (Subject+had+V3+Object)
    Past simple (Subject+V2+Object) Past perfect (Subject+had+V3+Object)
    Past Continuous (Subject +was/were+V1+ing+ Object) Past perfect continuous (Subject+had been+V1 +ing+ Object)
    Future simple (Subject+ will/shall+V1+object) Present Conditional (Subject+would+V1+object)
    Future Continuous (Subject +will/shall+be+V1 +ing+ Object) Conditional Continuous (Subject +would+be+V1 +ing+ Object)

Hinglish

  • दिया गया वाक्य direct speech है।
  • Direct speech को indirect speech में बदलने या बदलने के लिए बुनियादी नियम:
    • Commas, inverted commas हटा दिए जाते हैं और 'that' जोड़ा जाता है।
    • First-person pronoun 'I', third-person pronoun 'she' बन जाता है और 'today', 'that day' बन जाता है।
    • Present simple tense format 'Subject + V1 (will) + Object' को past simple tense format 'Subject + V2 (would) + Object' में बदल दिया जाएगा।

अत: सही उत्तर विकल्प 3 है।

textEn:
Directions: Select the most appropriate one-word substitution for the given group of words.

A speech expressing one's thoughts aloud to oneself
textHi:
Directions: Select the most appropriate one-word substitution for the given group of words.

A speech expressing one's thoughts aloud to oneself
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'soliloquy.'

Key Points

  • Let us explore the marked option:
    • 'Soliloquy' means a speech expressing one's thoughts aloud to oneself. (अपने विचारों को ज़ोर से व्यक्त करने वाला भाषण)
      • For example - The actress's soliloquy lets the audience hear the character's inner thoughts right before the climax.

Hence, the correct answer is option 2.

Additional Information

  • Let us explore the other options:
    • 'Harangue' means to speak in a loud and angry way, often for a long time, in order to criticize somebody or something or to try to persuade people to do something. (उग्र या जोशीला भाषण देना)
      • For example - We had to listen to a long harangue about our own shortcomings.
    • 'Hyperbole' means a way of speaking or writing that makes something sound better, more exciting, dangerous, etc. than it really is. (किसी वस्‍तु की विशेषता को बढ़ा-चढ़ाकर बखान करने का ढंग, अतिशयोक्तिपूर्ण कथन)
      • For example - I can say without hyperbole this is the greatest meal I have ever had!
    • 'Sacrilege' means treating a religious object or place without the respect that it deserves. (पवित्र वस्‍तु या स्‍थान का अपमान; अपवित्रीकरण)
      • For example - The cult of the Roman emperor was sacrilege to Jews and Christians.

textEn:
Directions: Select the correct active form of the given sentence.

Are the attendance rules being relaxed by the authorities?
textHi:
Directions: Select the correct active form of the given sentence.

Are the attendance rules being relaxed by the authorities?
optionsEn: Have the authorities been relaxing the attendance rules?
Have the authorities relaxed the attendance rules?
Are the authorities going to relax the attendance rules?
Are the authorities relaxing the attendance rules?
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'Are the authorities relaxing the attendance rules?'

Key Points

  • In Active Voice, a sentence emphasizes the subject, performing an action.
  • In Passive Voice, a sentence emphasizes the action or the object of the sentence.
  • The given sentence is in the passive voice and it is in the present continuous tense.
  • When we convert this sentence into an active voice, the subject 'the attendance rules' of the passive voice becomes the object, the object 'the authorities' becomes the subject.
  • The passive format "are + being + V3 (relaxed)" should be converted into the active format "are + present participle form of the verb (relaxing)".
  • This is the active and passive voice rule for the present continuous tense.


Hence, the correct answer is option 4.

textEn:
Directions: Select the most appropriate option to substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. If there is no need to substitute it, select 'No substitution'.

She asked from me that I had seen the movie or not.
textHi:
Directions: Select the most appropriate option to substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. If there is no need to substitute it, select 'No substitution'.

She asked from me that I had seen the movie or not.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'me whether I had seen.'

Key Points

  • In the given sentence, the use of the preposition 'from' is superfluous or not required and the conjunction 'that' is incorrect.
  • The preposition 'from' means showing that somebody or something is taken away, removed, or separated from somebody or something else.
  • The given sentence is expressing doubt between two or more possibilities.
  • The conjunction 'that' is used for introducing a subordinate clause expressing a statement or hypothesis.
  • Therefore, the conjunction 'whether' should be used in place of the conjunction 'that'.
  • The conjunction 'whether' is used for expressing a choice or doubt between two or more possibilities.

Hence, the correct answer is option 3.

Correct Sentence: She asked me whether I had seen the movie or not.

Hinglish

  • दिए गए वाक्य में, preposition 'from' का प्रयोग अतिश्योक्तिपूर्ण है या आवश्यक नहीं है और conjunction 'that' गलत है।
  • Preposition 'from' का अर्थ है किसी व्‍यक्ति या वस्‍तु का किसी अन्‍य व्‍यक्ति या वस्‍तु से अलग होने या किए जाने का सूचक; से।
  • दिया गया वाक्य दो या दो से अधिक संभावनाओं के बीच संदेह व्यक्त कर रहा है।
  • एक बयान या परिकल्पना व्यक्त करने वाले subordinate clause को पेश करने के लिए conjunction 'that' का उपयोग किया जाता है।
  • इसलिए, conjunction 'that' के स्थान पर conjunction 'whether' का प्रयोग किया जाना चाहिए।
  • दो या दो से अधिक संभावनाओं के बीच एक विकल्प या संदेह व्यक्त करने के लिए conjunction 'whether' का उपयोग किया जाता है।

अतः सही उत्तर option 3 है।

Present Continuous He is playing football am/is/are + being +V3 Football is being played by him
Present Perfect He has played football Has/have + been+ V3 Football has been played by him
Present Perfect Continuous He has been playing football Not used and unusual. Not used and unusual
Past Simple He played football Was/were + V3 Football was played by him
Past Continuous He was playing football Was/were + being + V3 Football was being played by him
Past Perfect He had played football Had + been + V3 Football had been played by him
Past Perfect Continuous He had been playing football Not used and unusual Not used and unusual
Future Simple He will play football Will + be + V3 Football will be played by him
Future Continuous He will be playing football Not used and unusual Not used and unusual
Future Perfect He will have played football Will have been + V3 Football will have been played by him
Future Perfect Continuous He will have been playing football Not used and unusual Not used and unusual

Hinglish

  • Active Voice में , एक वाक्य विषय पर जोर देता है, एक क्रिया करता है।
  • Passive Voice में , एक वाक्य वाक्य की क्रिया या वस्तु पर जोर देता है।
  • दिया गया वाक्य active voice में है और यह present continuous tense में है।
  • जब हम इस वाक्य को passive voice में बदलते हैं, तो active voice का subject 'They', hidden object 'them' बन जाता है और object 'the homeless', subject बन जाता है।
  • Active format "are + present participle (providing)" को passive format "are + being + V3 (provided)" में परिवर्तित किया जाना चाहिए।
  • यह present continuous tense के लिए active and passive voice rule है।

अतः सही उत्तर विकल्प 3 है।

textEn:
Directions: Select the correct indirect form of the given sentence.

He said to me, "What a pity! You missed the party."
textHi:
Directions: Select the correct indirect form of the given sentence.

He said to me, "What a pity! You missed the party."
optionsEn: He exclaimed what a pity it was that you missed the party.
He told to me that it was a pity I had missed the party.
He exclaimed that it was a pity I had missed the party.
He exclaimed that it was a pity you missed the party.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is He exclaimed that it was a pity I had missed the party.

Key Points

  • The given sentence is a direct speech.
  • The basic rules for changing or converting direct speech into indirect speech:​
    • The commas, inverted commas, exclamation mark (!) are removed, and 'that' is added.
    • The exclamation mark ''What a pity!'' becomes ''it was a pity''.
    • The second-person pronoun 'You' becomes the first-person pronoun 'I'.
    • The past simple tense format 'Subject + V2 (missed) + Object' will be changed into the past perfect tense format 'Subject + had + V3 (missed) + Object'.

Hence, the correct answer is option 3.

Confusion Points

  • You may think the reporting verb ''told'' should be used.
  • Option 2 is wrong because the reporting verb 'exclaimed' is used to express the state of joy, sorrow, or wonder.
  • Thus, the correct answer is option 3.

Additional Information

  • The following table will enable us to know how tense changes into Indirect speech:-
    Direct Speech Indirect Speech
    Present simple (Subject +V1st + Object) Past simple (Subject +V2 + Object)
    Present continuous (Subject +is/am/are+V1 +ing+ Object) Past Continuous (Subject +was/were+V1 +ing+ Object)
    Present perfect (Subject + has/have+V3+Object) Past perfect (Subject+had+V3+Object)
    Past simple (Subject+V2+Object) Past perfect (Subject+had+V3+Object)
    Past Continuous (Subject +was/were+V1+ing+ Object) Past perfect continuous (Subject+had been+V1 +ing+ Object)
    Future simple (Subject+ will/shall+V1+object) Present Conditional (Subject+would+V1+object)
    Future Continuous (Subject +will/shall+be+V1 +ing+ Object) Conditional Continuous (Subject +would+be+V1 +ing+ Object)

Hinglish

  • दिया गया वाक्य direct speech है।
  • Direct speech को indirect speech में बदलने या बदलने के लिए बुनियादी नियम:
    • Commas, inverted commas, exclamation mark (!) हटा दिए जाते हैं और 'that' जोड़ा जाता है।
    • Exclamation mark ''What a pity!'' बन जाता है ''it was a pity''।
    • Second-person pronoun 'You', first-person pronoun 'I' बन जाता है।
    • Past simple tense format 'Subject + V2 (missed) + Object' को past perfect tense format 'Subject + had + V3 (missed) + Object' में बदल दिया जाएगा।

अत: सही उत्तर विकल्प 3 है।

textEn:
Directions: Select the most appropriate option to substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. If there is no need to substitute it, select 'No substitution'.

They who wants to play should leave the class.
textHi:
Directions: Select the most appropriate option to substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. If there is no need to substitute it, select 'No substitution'.

They who wants to play should leave the class.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'Those who want.'

Key Points

  • In the given sentence, the use of the pronoun 'They' and the singular form of the verb 'wants' is incorrect.
  • The pronoun 'They' is used to refer to two or more people or things previously mentioned or easily identified.
  • Therefore, the pronoun 'Those' should be used in place of the pronoun 'They'.
  • The pronoun 'Those' is used in singling out someone or something and ascribing a distinctive feature to them.
  • Also, the plural form of the verb should be used with the plural subject.
  • Therefore, the plural form of the verb 'want' should be used in place of the singular form of the verb 'wants'.

Hence, the correct answer is option 2.

Correct Sentence: Those who want to play should leave the class.

Hinglish

  • दिए गए वाक्य में pronoun 'They' का प्रयोग और 'wants' क्रिया का एकवचन रूप गलत है।
  • Pronoun 'They' का प्रयोग दो या दो से अधिक लोगों या पहले बताई गई या आसानी से पहचानी जाने वाली चीजों को संदर्भित करने के लिए किया जाता है।
  • इसलिए, pronoun 'They' के स्थान पर pronoun 'Those' के ठीक बाद आना चाहिए।
  • Pronoun 'Those' का प्रयोग किसी व्यक्ति या वस्तु को अलग करने और उन्हें एक विशिष्ट विशेषता बताने के लिए किया जाता है।
  • साथ ही, verb के बहुवचन रूप का प्रयोग बहुवचन subject के साथ किया जाना चाहिए।
  • इसलिए, क्रिया के एकवचन रूप 'wants' के स्थान पर क्रिया के बहुवचन रूप 'want' का प्रयोग करना चाहिए।

अतः सही उत्तर option 2 है।

The correct answer is 'Those who want.'

textEn:
Directions: Select the most appropriate option to substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. If there is no need to substitute it, select 'No substitution'.

He promised to deliver the goods yesterday, did he?
textHi:
Directions: Select the most appropriate option to substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. If there is no need to substitute it, select 'No substitution'.

He promised to deliver the goods yesterday, did he?
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'didn't he.'

Key Points

  • The given sentence is a tag question. We use tag questions to ask for confirmation.
  • A positive sentence takes a negative tag and a negative sentence takes a positive tag.
  • The given sentence is a positive statement and therefore, a negative tag should be used, also, the given sentence is in the past tense, therefore, the tag should be in the past tense.
  • Therefore, the negative tag 'didn't' should be used in place of the positive tag 'did'.

Hence, the correct answer is option 1.

Correct Sentence: He promised to deliver the goods yesterday, didn't he?

Hinglish

  • दिया गया वाक्य एक टैग प्रश्न है। पुष्टि के लिए पूछने के लिए हम टैग प्रश्नों का उपयोग करते हैं।
  • एक सकारात्मक वाक्य एक नकारात्मक टैग लेता है और एक नकारात्मक वाक्य एक सकारात्मक टैग लेता है।
  • दिया गया वाक्य एक सकारात्मक कथन है और इसलिए, एक नकारात्मक टैग का उपयोग किया जाना चाहिए, साथ ही, दिया गया वाक्य भूत काल में है, इसलिए, टैग भूत काल में होना चाहिए।
  • इसलिए, सकारात्मक टैग 'did' के स्थान पर नकारात्मक टैग 'didn't' का उपयोग किया जाना चाहिए।

textEn:
Directions: Select the correct indirect form of the given sentence.

The teacher said, "Too many cooks spoil the broth."
textHi:
Directions: Select the correct indirect form of the given sentence.

The teacher said, "Too many cooks spoil the broth."
optionsEn: The teacher said that too many cooks spoil the broth.
The teacher told too many cooks had spoilt the broth.
The teacher said that too many cooks spoiled the broth.
The teacher said too many cooks spoils the broth.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is The teacher said that too many cooks spoil the broth.

Key Points

  • The given sentence is a direct speech.
  • The basic rules for changing or converting direct speech into indirect speech:
    • The commas, inverted commas are removed, and 'that' is added.
    • The given reported speech is a proverb.
    • No change is made to the verb of the reported speech if it shows a historical fact, a habitual fact, a universal truth, or a proverb.

Hence, the correct answer is option 1.

Confusion Points

  • You may think the past tense form of the verb ''spoiled'' should be used.
  • Option 3 is wrong because 'spoiled' is used in the past simple tense of the indirect speech.
  • Thus, the correct answer is option 1.

Additional Information

    Future simple (Subject + will/shall + V1 + object) Present Conditional (Subject + would + V1 + object)
