textEn:
Directions: Given below are four sentences in jumbled order. Pick the option that gives their correct order.

A. The event was held to raise funds for the slum adopted by the school.

B. The Principal thanked everyone for the contributions which would help in the development activities.

C. The contributions from the parents and teachers were amazing.

D. The school Cultural Society organized a musical night last week.
textHi:
Directions: Given below are four sentences in jumbled order. Pick the option that gives their correct order.

A. The event was held to raise funds for the slum adopted by the school.

B. The Principal thanked everyone for the contributions which would help in the development activities.

C. The contributions from the parents and teachers were amazing.

D. The school Cultural Society organized a musical night last week.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'DACB'.

Key Points

  • The sentence 'D' is independent of any other sentence as it is giving general information about "organizing a musical night". Hence, 'D' is the first part.
  • Sentence A will come after D because it explains the purpose of organizing the musical night.
  • Sentence C will come after A because it explains the contribution of the teachers and parents to raise funds.
  • The sentence 'B' is the concluding part because it explains how the overall contribution would help.

Paragraph after rearranging the sentences: The school Cultural Society organized a musical night last week. The event was held to raise funds for the slum adopted by the school. The contributions from the parents and teachers were amazing. The Principal thanked everyone for the contributions which would help in the development activities.

Hint

  • Important Rules to Solve the Para Jumbles:
    • Read the sentences attentively and try to comprehend the idea or theme of the sentence and eliminate the irrelevant options.
    • Try to identify the opening (independent sentence) and closing sentences (concluding sentence).
    • Try to identify the mandatory pairs. Two sentences can be connected through various connectors and other determinants.

Hinglish

  • वाक्य 'D' किसी अन्य वाक्य से स्वतंत्र है क्योंकि यह "एक संगीतमय रात के आयोजन" के बारे में सामान्य जानकारी दे रहा है। अतः 'D' पहला भाग है।
  • वाक्य A, D के बाद आएगा क्योंकि यह संगीतमय रात्रि के आयोजन का उद्देश्य बताता है।
  • वाक्य C, A के बाद आएगा क्योंकि यह धन जुटाने के लिए शिक्षकों और माता-पिता के योगदान की व्याख्या करता है।
  • वाक्य 'B' अंतिम भाग है क्योंकि यह बताता है कि समग्र योगदान कैसे मदद करेगा।
explanationHi:
The correct answer is 'DACB'.

Key Points

  • The sentence 'D' is independent of any other sentence as it is giving general information about "organizing a musical night". Hence, 'D' is the first part.
  • Sentence A will come after D because it explains the purpose of organizing the musical night.
  • Sentence C will come after A because it explains the contribution of the teachers and parents to raise funds.
  • The sentence 'B' is the concluding part because it explains how the overall contribution would help.

Paragraph after rearranging the sentences: The school Cultural Society organized a musical night last week. The event was held to raise funds for the slum adopted by the school. The contributions from the parents and teachers were amazing. The Principal thanked everyone for the contributions which would help in the development activities.

Hint

textEn:
Directions: Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word.

AMATEUR
textHi:
Directions: Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word.

AMATEUR
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'professional.'

Key Points

  • The given word 'Amateur' means a person who does not have skill or experience when doing something. (आवश्‍यक कौशल या अनुभव के बिना काम करने वाला व्‍यक्ति)
    • Example: The amateur pianist made one or two false starts before finally playing the piece right through from the beginning.
  • ​Let's see the meaning of other given options:-
    • 'Apprentice' is a person who works for low pay, in order to learn the skills needed in a particular job. (हुनर-विशेष सीखने के लिए कम वेतन पर काम करने को तैयार व्‍यक्ति; प्रशिक्षार्थी)
      • Example: My son is an apprentice in a furniture maker's workshop.
    • 'Novice' is a person who is new and without experience in a certain job, situation, etc. (किसी विशेष काम, परिसि्‍थति आदि के लिए नया और अनुभवहीन व्यक्ति; नौसिखिया)
      • Example: Novice programmers sometimes make fundamental errors.
    • 'Professional' is a person who has a lot of skill and experience. (अति निपुण या अनुभवी व्‍यक्ति)
      • Example: She wants to become a professional cook.
    • 'Learner' is a person who is learning. (जो अभी सीख रहा है या ज्ञान प्राप्‍त कर रहा है; शिक्षार्थी)
      • Example: She was a quick learner, and her German got better by the day.

Hence, the correct answer is option 3.

Additional Information

  • The antonyms of the word 'Amateur' are "professional, master, expert".
  • The synonyms of the word 'Amateur' are "inexperienced, unprofessional, unskilled".

textEn:
Directions: Given below are four sentences in jumbled order. Pick the option that gives their correct order.

A. It then came to the main road and smashed all the stalls selling fruits, mud pots, and clothes.

B. Finally, the elephant entered a school ground, breaking through the brick wall.

C. The people on the main road ran helter-skelter in panic.

D. The wild elephant first reached the outskirts of our town, breaking down fences like matchsticks.
textHi:
Directions: Given below are four sentences in jumbled order. Pick the option that gives their correct order.

A. It then came to the main road and smashed all the stalls selling fruits, mud pots, and clothes.

B. Finally, the elephant entered a school ground, breaking through the brick wall.

C. The people on the main road ran helter-skelter in panic.

D. The wild elephant first reached the outskirts of our town, breaking down fences like matchsticks.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'DACB'.

Key Points

  • The sentence 'D' is independent of any other sentence as it is giving general information about "what the wild elephant does on entering the outskirts of a town". Hence, 'D' is the first part.
  • Sentence A will come after D because it explains what the elephant does when it went to the main road.
  • Sentence C will come after A because it explains what happened with the people who were standing on the main road.
  • The sentence 'B' is the concluding part because it explains where and how did the elephant finally entered.

Finally, the elephant entered a school ground, breaking through the brick wall.

textEn:
Directions: Given below are six sentences A-F, of which A and F are static and B-E have been jumbled. Pick the option that gives the correct order of the jumbled sentences.

A. Timothy, the tiger cub was discovered by Grandfather in the Terai jungle near Dehra.

B. Grandfather picked him and brought him home.

C. One day when Grandfather was strolling in the jungle, he spotted a little tiger about 18 inches long.

D. The tiger cub was named Timothy by Grandfather and initially brought up entirely on milk.

E. The frightened animal was hiding among the intricate roots of a banyan tree.

F. Later, he was put on a diet of raw mutton and cod liver oil.

Directions: Given below are six sentences A-F, of which A and F are static and B-E have been jumbled. Pick the option that gives the correct order of the jumbled sentences.

A. A singer called Mukandan Misra and his wife lived in Behat near Gwalior.

B. Tired, the group settled down to rest in a shady grove.

C. Once Swami Haridas was traveling through the forest with his disciples.

D. Tansen, their only child was very naughty.

E. Often he would run away to the woods and soon learned to imitate the calls of birds and animals.

F. Tansen thought it would be fun to frighten the resting men with the roar of a tiger.

Tired, the group settled down to rest in a shady grove. Tansen thought it would be fun to frighten the resting men with the roar of a tiger.

  • Important Rules to Solve the Para Jumbles:
    • Read the sentences attentively and try to comprehend the idea or theme of the sentence and eliminate the irrelevant options.

  • वाक्य D, A के बाद आएगा क्योंकि यह मुकंदन मिश्रा और उनकी पत्नी की इकलौती संतान की आदत की व्याख्या करता है।
  • वाक्य E, D के बाद आएगा क्योंकि यह बताता है कि बच्चा अक्सर क्या करता था।
  • वाक्य C, E के बाद आएगा क्योंकि यह बताता है कि एक दिन उस जंगल में क्या हुआ जहां बच्चा भागता था।

explanationHi:
The correct answer is 'ADECBF'.

  • Sentence B will come after C because it explains what the disciples did when felt tired.
  • The sentence 'F' is the concluding part because it explains what Tansen has done with his disciples.

textEn:
Directions: Select the correct indirect form of the given sentence.

He said, "Manan came to college yesterday."
textHi:
Directions: Select the correct indirect form of the given sentence.

He said, "Manan came to college yesterday."
optionsEn: He said Manan was coming to college yesterday.
He said that Manan had come to college the previous day.
He said that Manan had came to college yesterday.
He said that Manan came to college the previous day.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is He said that Manan had come to college the previous day.

Key Points

  • The given sentence is a direct speech.
  • The basic rules for changing or converting direct speech into indirect speech:​
    • The commas, inverted commas are removed, and 'that' is added, 'yesterday' becomes 'the previous day'.
    • The past simple tense format 'Subject + V2 (came) + Object' will be changed into the past perfect tense format 'Subject + had + V3 (come) + Object'.

Hence, the correct answer is option 2.

Confusion Points

  • You may think that option 3 should be correct but 'yesterday' has to be changed into the ''previous day'' in the indirect speech.
  • Thus, the correct answer is option 2.

Additional Information

  • The following table will enable us to know how tense changes into Indirect speech:-
    Direct Speech Indirect Speech
    Present simple (Subject +V1st + Object) Past simple (Subject +V2 + Object)
    Present continuous (Subject +is/am/are+V1 +ing+ Object) Past Continuous (Subject +was/were+V1 +ing+ Object)
    Present perfect (Subject + has/have+V3+Object) Past perfect (Subject+had+V3+Object)
    Past simple (Subject+V2+Object) Past perfect (Subject+had+V3+Object)
    Past Continuous (Subject +was/were+V1+ing+ Object) Past perfect continuous (Subject+had been+V1 +ing+ Object)
    Future simple (Subject+ will/shall+V1+object) Present Conditional (Subject+would+V1+object)
    Future Continuous (Subject +will/shall+be+V1 +ing+ Object) Conditional Continuous (Subject +would+be+V1 +ing+ Object)

Hinglish

  • दिया गया वाक्य direct speech है।
  • Direct speech को indirect speech में बदलने या बदलने के लिए बुनियादी नियम:
    • Commas, inverted commas हटा दिए जाते हैं और 'that' जोड़ा जाता है, 'yesterday' बन जाता है 'the previous day'।
    • Past simple tense format 'Subject + V2 (came) + Object' को past perfect tense format 'Subject + had + V3 (come) + Object' में बदल दिया जाएगा।

अत: सही उत्तर विकल्प 2 है।
explanationHi:
The correct answer is He said that Manan had come to college the previous day.

textEn:
Directions: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank.

Either side of the road ______ lined by toy shops.
textHi:
Directions: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank.

Either side of the road ______ lined by toy shops.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'is.'

Key Points

  • The given sentence is saying something about toy shops on either side of the road.
  • The given sentence is the present simple tense of passive voice and stating a fact.
  • In the present simple, the passive is: am / is / are + past participle (here, lined).
  • Also, we know that the singular subject will always take a singular verb.
  • Therefore, the singular verb 'is' should be used in the blank.

Hence, the correct answer is option 1.

Complete Sentence: Either side of the road is lined by toy shops.

Additional Information

  • Let us explore the other options:
    • ''Has'' should be used in the present perfect tense and with a singular subject. ('Has' का प्रयोग present perfect tense ​​में और एकवचन subject के साथ किया जाना चाहिए)
    • ''Have'' should be used in the present perfect tense and with a plural subject. ('Have' का प्रयोग present perfect tense ​​में और बहुवचन subject के साथ किया जाना चाहिए)
    • ''Are'' should be used in the present simple tense and with a plural subject. ('Are' का प्रयोग present simple tense ​​में और बहुवचन subject के साथ किया जाना चाहिए)

Hinglish

  • दिया गया वाक्य सड़क के दोनों ओर खिलौनों की दुकानों के बारे में कुछ कह रहा है।
  • दिया गया वाक्य present simple tense of passive voice है और एक तथ्य बताता है।
  • Present simple, passive है: am / is / are + past participle (यहाँ, lined)।
  • साथ ही, हम जानते हैं कि एकवचन subject हमेशा एकवचन verb लेगा।
  • इसलिए,रिक्त स्थान में एकवचन क्रिया 'is' का प्रयोग करना चाहिए।

अत: सही उत्तर विकल्प 1 है।

textEn:

One who rides horses in races
textHi:

One who rides horses in races
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'jockey.'

Key Points

  • Let us explore the marked option:
    • 'Jockey' is a person who rides horses in races, especially as a profession. (घुड़दौड़ में भाग लेने वाला घुड़सवार)
      • For example - A​ jockey must be brave to ride a horse at high speed.

      • For example - The porter was weighed down by all the luggage.

textEn:
Directions: Identify the segment in the sentence which contains a grammatical error.

You need / to sign / these important documents / by ink.
textHi:
Directions: Identify the segment in the sentence which contains a grammatical error.

You need / to sign / these important documents / by ink.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'by ink' i.e. this part of the sentence has an error.

Key Points

  • In the given sentence, the use of the preposition 'by' is incorrect.
  • The preposition 'by' means through doing or using something; by means of something.
  • Therefore, the preposition 'in' should be used in place of the preposition 'by'.
  • The preposition 'in' is used for stating what you use for writing, drawing, or painting.
  • ''In ink'' means written or signed with a pen that uses ink, not with a pencil.

Hence, the correct answer is option 4.

Correct Sentence: You need to sign these important documents in ink.

Hinglish

  • दिए गए वाक्य में preposition 'by' का प्रयोग गलत है।
  • Preposition 'by' का अर्थ है के द्वारा; के ज़रिये से।
  • इसलिए, preposition 'in' का प्रयोग preposition 'by' के स्थान पर किया जाना चाहिए।
  • Preposition 'in' का उपयोग यह बताने के लिए किया जाता है कि आप लेखन, ड्राइंग या पेंटिंग के लिए क्या उपयोग करते हैं।
  • ''In ink'' का अर्थ पेन से लिखा या हस्ताक्षरित है जिसमें स्याही का उपयोग होता है, पेंसिल से नहीं।

अतः सही उत्तर option 4 है।

सही वाक्य: You need to sign these important documents in ink.

textEn:
Directions: Identify the segment in the sentence which contains a grammatical error.

He is / afraid from / all insects / that crawl.
textHi:
Directions: Identify the segment in the sentence which contains a grammatical error.

He is / afraid from / all insects / that crawl.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'afraid from' i.e. this part of the sentence has an error.

Key Points

  • In the given sentence, the use of the preposition 'from' is incorrect.
  • The preposition 'from' is used for showing the person who sent or gave something.
  • Therefore, the preposition 'of' should be used in place of the preposition 'from'.
  • The preposition 'of' is used after some adjectives (here, afraid) for saying a person or thing that a feeling is directed towards.

Hence, the correct answer is option 3.

Correct Sentence: He is afraid of all insects that crawl.

Hinglish

  • दिए गए वाक्य में preposition 'from' का प्रयोग गलत है।
  • Preposition 'from' का प्रयोग उस व्यक्ति को दिखाने के लिए किया जाता है जिसने कुछ भेजा या दिया।
  • इसलिए, preposition 'of' का प्रयोग preposition 'from' के स्थान पर किया जाना चाहिए।
  • किसी व्यक्ति या बात को कहने के लिए जिसकी ओर एक भावना निर्देशित होती है, कुछ adjectives (यहां, afraid) के बाद preposition 'of' का प्रयोग किया जाता है।

अतः सही उत्तर option 3 है।

सही वाक्य: He is afraid of all insects that crawl.

textEn:
Directions: Select the correct active form of the given sentence.

All meetings are held in the conference room.
textHi:
Directions: Select the correct active form of the given sentence.

All meetings are held in the conference room.
optionsEn: We are holding all meetings in the conference room.
We will hold all meetings in the conference room.
We hold all meetings in the conference room.
We held all meetings in the conference room.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'We hold all meetings in the conference room.'

Key Points

  • In Active Voice, a sentence emphasizes the subject, performing an action.
  • In Passive Voice, a sentence emphasizes the action or the object of the sentence.

textEn:
Directions: Select the most appropriate option to substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. If there is no need to substitute it, select 'No substitution'.

Don't lose sight of your goal lest you should fail.
textHi:
Directions: Select the most appropriate option to substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. If there is no need to substitute it, select 'No substitution'.

Don't lose sight of your goal lest you should fail.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'No substitution.'

Key Points

  • In the underlined segment of the sentence, no substitution is required.
  • 'Lest...should' is used to extend a warning and also suggest the consequences of not paying attention to that warning.
  • The use of the conjunction 'lest' in any sentence conveys a negative sense as such, 'no' or 'not' should not be used in that sentence.
  • 'Lest...should' already conveys a negative sense.

Hence, the correct answer is option 2.

Correct sentence: Don't lose sight of your goal lest you should fail.

Additional Information

  • 'Lest' is always followed by 'should'.
  • 'Should' here can not be replaced by 'would' or 'may'.

Hinglish

  • वाक्य के रेखांकित खंड में किसी प्रतिस्थापन की आवश्यकता नहीं है।
  • चेतावनी देने के लिए 'Lest... should' का उपयोग किया जाता है और उस चेतावनी पर ध्यान न देने के परिणामों का भी सुझाव दिया जाता है।
  • किसी भी वाक्य में conjunction 'lest' का प्रयोग एक नकारात्मक भाव को व्यक्त करता है जैसे कि उस वाक्य में 'नहीं' का प्रयोग नहीं किया जाना चाहिए।
  • 'Lest...should' पहले से ही एक नकारात्मक भाव व्यक्त करता है।

अतः सही उत्तर option 2 है।

सही वाक्य: Don't lose sight of your goal lest you should fail.

textEn:
Directions: Given below are six sentences A-F, of which A and F are static and B-E have been jumbled. Pick the option that gives the correct order of the jumbled sentences.

A. A great treat for Toto monkey in cold weather was the large bowl of warm water given to him for his bath.

B. He would then rush straight to the kitchen fire to dry himself.

C. When the water became cold, he would get out and run as quickly as he could.

D. He would cunningly test the temperature with his hand and then step into the bath gradually.

E. Once comfortable, he would take the soap in his hands or feet and rub himself all over.

F. But if anyone made fun of his activities, he would feel hurt.
textHi:
Directions: Given below are six sentences A-F, of which A and F are static and B-E have been jumbled. Pick the option that gives the correct order of the jumbled sentences.

A. A great treat for Toto monkey in cold weather was the large bowl of warm water given to him for his bath.

B. He would then rush straight to the kitchen fire to dry himself.

C. When the water became cold, he would get out and run as quickly as he could.

D. He would cunningly test the temperature with his hand and then step into the bath gradually.

E. Once comfortable, he would take the soap in his hands or feet and rub himself all over.

F. But if anyone made fun of his activities, he would feel hurt.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'ADECBF'.

Key Points

  • The sentence 'A' is independent of any other sentence as it is giving general information about "Toto monkey and his bathing arrangement". Hence, 'A' is the first part.
  • Sentence D will come after A because it explains what the monkey did with the warm water.
  • Sentence E will come after D because it explains what the monkey does after checking the temperature of the water.
  • Sentence C will come after E because it explains what the monkey does when the water becomes cold.
  • Sentence B will come after C because it explains where the monkey runs straight away after bath and what he does there.
  • The sentence 'F' is the concluding part because it explains how he feels when someone mocks him for such activities.

Paragraph after rearranging the sentences: A great treat for Toto monkey in cold weather was the large bowl of warm water given to him for his bath. He would cunningly test the temperature with his hand and then step into the bath gradually. Once comfortable, he would take the soap in his hands or feet and rub himself all over. When the water became cold, he would get out and run as quickly as he could. He would then rush straight to the kitchen fire to dry himself. But if anyone made fun of his activities, he would feel hurt.

Hint

  • Important Rules to Solve the Para Jumbles:
    • Read the sentences attentively and try to comprehend the idea or theme of the sentence and eliminate the irrelevant options.
    • Try to identify the opening (independent sentence) and closing sentences (concluding sentence).
    • Try to identify the mandatory pairs. Two sentences can be connected through various connectors and other determinants.

Hinglish

  • वाक्य E, D के बाद आएगा क्योंकि यह बताता है कि बंदर पानी का तापमान जांचने के बाद क्या करता है।
  • वाक्य C, E के बाद आएगा क्योंकि यह बताता है कि पानी ठंडा होने पर बंदर क्या करता है।
  • वाक्य B, C के बाद आएगा क्योंकि यह बताता है कि बंदर नहाने के बाद सीधे कहां भागता है और वहां क्या करता है।
  • वाक्य 'F' अंतिम भाग है क्योंकि यह बताता है कि जब कोई उसकी ऐसी गतिविधियों के लिए उसका मजाक उड़ाता है तो वह कैसा महसूस करता है।

textEn:
Directions: Given below are six sentences A-F, of which A and F are static and B-E have been jumbled. Pick the option that gives the correct order of the jumbled sentences.

A. Dr. Sadao Hoki's house was built on a spot on the Japanese coast where he had played as a little boy.

B. His father had said, "Those islands are the stepping stones to the future for Japan.

C. His father had taken him to the islands of those seas and taught him things he had not forgotten.

D. As a boy, Sadao had climbed the pines, supporting himself on his bare feet as the men did in the South Seas.

E. The square stone house was set upon rocks above a beach outlined with bent pines.

F. "Where shall we step from them?" Sadao had asked seriously.
textHi:
Directions: Given below are six sentences A-F, of which A and F are static and B-E have been jumbled. Pick the option that gives the correct order of the jumbled sentences.

A. Dr. Sadao Hoki's house was built on a spot on the Japanese coast where he had played as a little boy.

B. His father had said, "Those islands are the stepping stones to the future for Japan.

C. His father had taken him to the islands of those seas and taught him things he had not forgotten.

D. As a boy, Sadao had climbed the pines, supporting himself on his bare feet as the men did in the South Seas.

E. The square stone house was set upon rocks above a beach outlined with bent pines.

F. "Where shall we step from them?" Sadao had asked seriously.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'AEDCBF'.

Key Points

  • The sentence 'A' is independent of any other sentence as it is giving general information about "Dr. Sadao Hoki's house". Hence, 'A' is the first part.
  • Sentence E will come after A because it explains the setup of the doctor's house.
  • Sentence D will come after E because it explains what Dr. Sadao used to do in the South Seas when he was a child.
  • Sentence C will come after D because it explains what the doctor's father used to do with the doctor.
  • Sentence B will come after C because it explains what the doctor's father said about the islands of the South Seas.
  • The sentence 'F' is the concluding part because it explains what the doctor finally asked his father.

Paragraph after rearranging the sentences: Dr. Sadao Hoki's house was built on a spot on the Japanese coast where he had played as a little boy. The square stone house was set upon rocks above a beach outlined with bent pines. As a boy, Sadao had climbed the pines, supporting himself on his bare feet as the men did in the South Seas. His father had taken him to the islands of those seas and taught him things he had not forgotten. His father had said, "Those islands are the stepping stones to the future for Japan. "Where shall we step from them?" Sadao had asked seriously.

Hint

  • Important Rules to Solve the Para Jumbles:
    • Read the sentences attentively and try to comprehend the idea or theme of the sentence and eliminate the irrelevant options.

  • वाक्य E, A के बाद आएगा क्योंकि यह डॉक्टर के घर की व्यवस्था की व्याख्या करता है।
  • वाक्य D, E के बाद आएगा क्योंकि यह बताता है कि डॉ. सदाओ जब बच्चा था तब दक्षिण समुद्र में क्या किया करता था।
  • वाक्य C, D के बाद आएगा क्योंकि यह बताता है कि डॉक्टर के पिता डॉक्टर के साथ क्या करते थे।
  • वाक्य B, C के बाद आएगा क्योंकि यह बताता है कि डॉक्टर के पिता ने दक्षिण समुद्र के द्वीपों के बारे में क्या कहा।
  • वाक्य 'F' अंतिम भाग है क्योंकि यह बताता है कि डॉक्टर ने अपने पिता से आखिर में क्या पूछा।
explanationHi:
The correct answer is 'AEDCBF'.

Key Points

  • The sentence 'A' is independent of any other sentence as it is giving general information about "Dr. Sadao Hoki's house". Hence, 'A' is the first part.
  • Sentence E will come after A because it explains the setup of the doctor's house.
  • Sentence D will come after E because it explains what Dr. Sadao used to do in the South Seas when he was a child.
  • Sentence C will come after D because it explains what the doctor's father used to do with the doctor.
  • Sentence B will come after C because it explains what the doctor's father said about the islands of the South Seas.
  • The sentence 'F' is the concluding part because it explains what the doctor finally asked his father.

Paragraph after rearranging the sentences: Dr. Sadao Hoki's house was built on a spot on the Japanese coast where he had played as a little boy. The square stone house was set upon rocks above a beach outlined with bent pines. As a boy, Sadao had climbed the pines, supporting himself on his bare feet as the men did in the South Seas. His father had taken him to the islands of those seas and taught him things he had not forgotten. His father had said, "Those islands are the stepping stones to the future for Japan. "Where shall we step from them?" Sadao had asked seriously.

    • Try to identify the opening (independent sentence) and closing sentences (concluding sentence).
    • Try to identify the mandatory pairs. Two sentences can be connected through various connectors and other determinants.

textEn:
Directions: Select the most appropriate option to substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. If there is no need to substitute it, select 'No substitution'.

None other beside you are knowing the truth.
textHi:
Directions: Select the most appropriate option to substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. If there is no need to substitute it, select 'No substitution'.

None other beside you are knowing the truth.
optionsEn: beside you are know
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'than you knows'.

Key Points

  • 'None other' and 'than' are correlative conjunctions i.e., they are always used in pairs.
    • ​For example: None other than you is invited to the meeting.
  • In this question, "none other than" is a phrase that means "no one else except for."
  • The subject of the sentence is "none," which is a singular noun. The verb should agree with the singular subject, so "knows" is the correct verb form.
  • Therefore, "than you knows" is the correct form of the verb in this sentence. "Beside" is not the correct preposition to use in this sentence as "none other than" already implies that there is no one else beside the person being referred to.

Therefore, the correct answer is: than you knows.

Confusion Points

  • In this sentence, the verb knows does not agree with you, but with none.
    None is a singular indefinite pronoun that can mean no one or not any.

  • In this sentence, none means no one, and it is the subject of the verb knows. The phrase than you is not the subject, but a complement that shows the comparison.

  • Therefore, the verb knows agrees with the singular subject none, not the second person pronoun you.

    • For example, you can say:None of us knows the answer. (None is the subject and knows is the verb. Of us is a prepositional phrase that modifies none.)

    • He knows more than you. (He is the subject and knows is the verb. Than you is a complement that shows the comparison.)

textEn:
Directions: Identify the segment in the sentence which contains a grammatical error.

No sooner / did the chairman arrived / than the / proceedings commenced.
textHi:
Directions: Identify the segment in the sentence which contains a grammatical error.

No sooner / did the chairman arrived / than the / proceedings commenced.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'did the chairman arrived' i.e. this part of the sentence has an error.

Key Points

  • In the given sentence, the use of the past form of the verb 'arrived' is incorrect.
  • The auxiliary verb 'did' is marked for past tense, but the main verb appears in its base form.
  • We know that the verb that follows 'did' should be used in its base form.
  • Therefore, the base form of the verb 'arrive' should be used in place of the past tense form of the verb 'arrived'.

Hence, the correct answer is option 2.

Correct Sentence: No sooner did the chairman arrive than the proceedings commenced.

Hinglish

  • दिए गए वाक्य में past form of the verb 'arrived' का प्रयोग गलत है।
  • Auxiliary verb 'did' भूतकाल के लिए चिह्नित है, लेकिन मुख्य क्रिया अपने मूल रूप में प्रकट होती है।
  • हम जानते हैं कि 'did' के बाद आने वाली क्रिया को उसके मूल रूप में प्रयोग किया जाना चाहिए।
  • इसलिए past tense form of the verb 'arrived' के स्थान पर base form of the verb 'arrive' का प्रयोग किया जाना चाहिए।

अतः सही उत्तर option 2 है।

सही वाक्य: No sooner did the chairman arrive than the proceedings commenced.

textEn:

textHi:

explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'As soon he heard' i.e. this part of the sentence has an error.

Key Points

Hence, the correct answer is option 3.

  • Phrase ''As soon as'' का अर्थ है कुछ घटित होने के तुरंत बाद।

textEn:
Directions: Select the correct active form of the given sentence.

I am trusted by all my friends.
textHi:
Directions: Select the correct active form of the given sentence.

I am trusted by all my friends.
optionsEn: I have trusted all my friends.
I have been trusted all my friends.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'All my friends trust me.'

Key Points

  • In Active Voice, a sentence emphasizes the subject, performing an action.
  • In Passive Voice, a sentence emphasizes the action or the object of the sentence.
  • The given sentence is in the passive voice and it is in the present simple tense.
  • When we convert this sentence into an active voice, the subject 'I' of the passive voice becomes the object 'me', the object 'All my friends' becomes the subject.
  • The passive format "am + V3 (trusted)" should be converted into the active format "V1 (trust)".
  • This is the active and passive voice rule for the present simple tense.


Hence, the correct answer is option 3.

Additional Information

Verbs of passive format have a different set of tenses (*V3 - Past participle or Third form of the Verb) as given below in the table:

TENSE ACTIVE PASSIVE PASSIVE CONVERSION
Present Simple He plays football am/is/are + V3 Football is played by him
Present Continuous He is playing football am/is/are + being +V3 Football is being played by him
Present Perfect He has played football Has/have + been+ V3 Football has been played by him
Present Perfect Continuous He has been playing football Not used and unusual. Not used and unusual
Past Simple He played football Was/were + V3 Football was played by him
Past Continuous He was playing football Was/were + being + V3 Football was being played by him
Past Perfect He had played football Had + been + V3 Football had been played by him
Past Perfect Continuous He had been playing football Not used and unusual Not used and unusual
Future Simple He will play football Will + be + V3 Football will be played by him
Future Continuous He will be playing football Not used and unusual Not used and unusual
Future Perfect He will have played football Will have been + V3 Football will have been played by him
Future Perfect Continuous He will have been playing football Not used and unusual Not used and unusual


Hinglish

  • Active Voice में, एक वाक्य विषय पर जोर देता है, एक क्रिया करता है।
  • Passive Voice में, एक वाक्य वाक्य की क्रिया या वस्तु पर जोर देता है।
  • दिया गया वाक्य passive voice में है और यह present simple tense में है।
  • जब हम इस वाक्य को active voice में बदलते हैं, तो passive voice का subject 'I', object 'me' बन जाता है और object 'All my friends', subject बन जाता है।
  • Passive format "am + V3 (trusted)" को active format "V1 (trust)" में परिवर्तित किया जाना चाहिए।
  • यह present simple tense के लिए active and passive voice rule है।

अतः सही उत्तर विकल्प 3 है।

The correct answer is 'All my friends trust me.'

textEn:
Directions: Select the correct indirect form of the given sentence.

He said to me, "What can I do for you?"
textHi:
Directions: Select the correct indirect form of the given sentence.

He said to me, "What can I do for you?"
optionsEn: He asked me what he could do for me.
He asked me that what could I do for you.
He asked me what I could do for him.
He asked me what he can do for me.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is He asked me what he could do for me.

Key Points

  • The given sentence is a direct speech.
  • The basic rules for changing or converting direct speech into indirect speech:​
    • The commas, inverted commas are removed.
    • The reporting verb ''said to'' becomes ''asked'' and the question mark (?) becomes the full stop (.).
    • The first-person pronoun ''I'' becomes the third-person pronoun ''he'' and the second-person pronoun ''you'' becomes the first-person pronoun ''me''.
    • The present simple tense format 'Subject + V1 (can) + Object' will be changed into the past simple tense format 'Subject + V2 (could) + Object'.

Hence, the correct answer is option 1.

Confusion Points

  • You may think the pronouns in other options should be used but the pronouns used in the first option are correctly used.
  • Thus, the correct answer is option 1.

Additional Information

  • The following table will enable us to know how tense changes into Indirect speech:-
    Direct Speech Indirect Speech
    Present simple (Subject +V1st + Object) Past simple (Subject +V2 + Object)
    Present continuous (Subject +is/am/are+V1 +ing+ Object) Past Continuous (Subject +was/were+V1 +ing+ Object)
    Present perfect (Subject + has/have+V3+Object) Past perfect (Subject+had+V3+Object)
    Past simple (Subject+V2+Object) Past perfect (Subject+had+V3+Object)
    Past Continuous (Subject +was/were+V1+ing+ Object) Past perfect continuous (Subject+had been+V1 +ing+ Object)
    Future simple (Subject+ will/shall+V1+object) Present Conditional (Subject+would+V1+object)
    Future Continuous (Subject +will/shall+be+V1 +ing+ Object) Conditional Continuous (Subject +would+be+V1 +ing+ Object)

Hinglish

  • दिया गया वाक्य direct speech है।
  • Direct speech को indirect speech में बदलने या बदलने के लिए बुनियादी नियम:
    • Commas, inverted commas हटा दिए जाते हैं।
    • Reporting verb ''said to'' बन जाती है ''asked'' और question mark (?) पूर्ण विराम (.) बन जाता है।
    • The first-person pronoun 'I', third-person pronoun 'he' बन जाता है और second-person pronoun 'you', the first-person pronoun 'me' बन जाता है।
    • Present simple tense format 'Subject + V1 (can) + Object' को past simple tense format 'Subject + V2 (could) + Object' में बदल दिया जाएगा।

अत: सही उत्तर विकल्प 1 है।

textEn:
Directions: Identify the segment in the sentence which contains a grammatical error.

He wanted to know / the reason / why his low score / in Mathematics.
textHi:
Directions: Identify the segment in the sentence which contains a grammatical error.

He wanted to know / the reason / why his low score / in Mathematics.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'why his low score' i.e. this part of the sentence has an error.

Key Points

  • In the given sentence, the use of the adverb 'why' is incorrect.
  • The adverb 'why' means for what reason or purpose.
  • Therefore, the preposition 'for' should be used in place of the adverb 'why'.
  • The preposition 'for' is used after nouns such as 'reason' or 'cause' or for talking about reasons or causes.

Hence, the correct answer is option 1.

Correct Sentence: He wanted to know the reason for his low score in Mathematics.

Hinglish

  • दिए गए वाक्य में adverb 'why' का प्रयोग गलत है।

  • इसलिए, adverb 'why' के स्थान पर preposition 'for' का प्रयोग किया जाना चाहिए।
  • Preposition 'for' का प्रयोग nouns जैसे 'reason' या 'cause' के बाद या कारणों के बारे में बात करने के लिए किया जाता है।

अतः सही उत्तर option 1 है।

सही वाक्य: He wanted to know the reason for his low score in Mathematics.
explanationHi:
The correct answer is 'why his low score' i.e. this part of the sentence has an error.

  • The adverb 'why' means for what reason or purpose.
  • Therefore, the preposition 'for' should be used in place of the adverb 'why'.
  • The preposition 'for' is used after nouns such as 'reason' or 'cause' or for talking about reasons or causes.

Hence, the correct answer is option 1.

explanationEn:

  • The word 'much' means very.

Hence, the correct answer is option 1.

explanationEn:

Key Points

    • Distinct means clearly different. (स्‍पष्‍टतया भिन्‍न)

textEn:
Directions: Select the correct direct form of the given sentence.

The manager lauded the salesman and said that his performance had been exceptional.
textHi:
Directions: Select the correct direct form of the given sentence.

The manager lauded the salesman and said that his performance had been exceptional.
optionsEn: The manager said to the salesman, "Oh! Your performance had been exceptional."
The manager said to the salesman, "Bravo! How exceptional his performance is !"
The manager said to the salesman, "Well done! Your performance has being exceptional."
The manager said to the salesman, "Well done! Your performance has been exceptional."
explanationEn:
The correct answer is The manager said to the salesman, "Well done! Your performance has been exceptional."

Key Points

  • The given sentence is an indirect speech.
  • The basic rules for changing or converting indirect speech into direct speech:​
    • The commas, inverted commas are added, and 'that' is removed, 'lauded' is replaced by 'said to', and 'said' is replaced by the exclamation ''well done!''.
    • The third-person pronoun 'his' will be changed into the second-person pronoun 'Your'.
    • The past perfect tense format 'Subject + had + V3 (been) + Object' will be changed into the present perfect tense format 'Subject + has + V3 (been) + Object'.
  • From the following rules, we get the correct sentence as option 4.

Additional Information

  • The following table will enable us to know how tense changes into Indirect speech:
    Direct Speech Indirect Speech
    Present simple (Subject +V1st + Object) Past simple (Subject +V2 + Object)
    Present continuous (Subject +is/am/are+V1 +ing+ Object) Past Continuous (Subject +was/were+V1 +ing+ Object)
    Present perfect (Subject + has/have+V3+Object) Past perfect (Subject+had+V3+Object)
    Past simple (Subject+V2+Object) Past perfect (Subject+had+V3+Object)
    Past Continuous (Subject +was/were+V1 +ing+ Object) Past perfect continuous (Subject +had been+V1 +ing+ Object)
    Future simple (Subject+ will/shall+V1+object) Present Conditional (Subject+ would+V1+object)
    Future Continuous (Subject +will/shall+be+V1 +ing+ Object) Conditional Continuous (Subject +would+be+V1 +ing+ Object)

Hinglish

  • दिया गया वाक्य indirect speech है।
  • Indirect speech को direct speech में बदलने या बदलने के लिए बुनियादी नियम:
    • Commas, inverted commas जोड़े जाते हैं, और 'that' हटा दिया जाता है, 'lauded' को 'said to' से बदल दिया जाता है, और 'said' को exclamation mark ''well done'' से बदल दिया जाता है।
    • Third-person pronoun 'his', second-person pronoun 'Your' बन जाता है।
    • Past perfect tense format 'Subject + had + V3 (been) + Object' को present perfect tense format 'Subject + has + V3 (been) + Object' में बदल दिया जाएगा।

अत: सही उत्तर विकल्प 4 है।

textEn:
Directions: Given below are four sentences in jumbled order. Pick the option that gives their correct order.

A. It was the advertisement for a metal detector called Wonder Sweeper 5000.

B. Jacob believed he could find the buried treasure of the pirates if he had that detector.

C. Jacob was reading one of his comic books when he came upon something interesting.

D. So, he decided to save every penny till he had enough money to buy one.
textHi:
Directions: Given below are four sentences in jumbled order. Pick the option that gives their correct order.

A. It was the advertisement for a metal detector called Wonder Sweeper 5000.

B. Jacob believed he could find the buried treasure of the pirates if he had that detector.

C. Jacob was reading one of his comic books when he came upon something interesting.

D. So, he decided to save every penny till he had enough money to buy one.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'CABD'.

Key Points

  • The sentence 'C' is independent of any other sentence as it is giving general information about "what was Jacob doing". Hence, 'C' is the first part.
  • Sentence A will come after C because it explains what Jacob had found interesting in the book.
  • Sentence B will come after A because it explains what Jacob could have done with the metal detector.
  • The sentence 'D' is the concluding part because it explains what Jacob finally decided to do to buy a metal detector.

Paragraph after rearranging the sentences: Jacob was reading one of his comic books when he came upon something interesting. It was the advertisement for a metal detector called Wonder Sweeper 5000. Jacob believed he could find the buried treasure of the pirates if he had that detector. So, he decided to save every penny till he had enough money to buy one.

Hint

  • Important Rules to Solve the Para Jumbles:
    • Read the sentences attentively and try to comprehend the idea or theme of the sentence and eliminate the irrelevant options.
    • Try to identify the opening (independent sentence) and closing sentences (concluding sentence).
    • Try to identify the mandatory pairs. Two sentences can be connected through various connectors and other determinants.

Hinglish

  • वाक्य 'C' किसी अन्य वाक्य से स्वतंत्र है क्योंकि यह "जैकब क्या कर रहा था" के बारे में सामान्य जानकारी दे रहा है। अतः 'C' पहला भाग है।
  • वाक्य A, C के बाद आएगा क्योंकि यह बताता है कि जैकब ने किताब में क्या दिलचस्प पाया था।
  • वाक्य B, A के बाद आएगा क्योंकि यह बताता है कि जैकब मेटल डिटेक्टर के साथ क्या कर सकता था।
  • वाक्य 'D' अंतिम भाग है क्योंकि यह बताता है कि जैकब ने आखिरकार मेटल डिटेक्टर खरीदने के लिए क्या करने का फैसला किया।
explanationHi:
The correct answer is 'CABD'.

Key Points

  • The sentence 'C' is independent of any other sentence as it is giving general information about "what was Jacob doing". Hence, 'C' is the first part.
  • Sentence A will come after C because it explains what Jacob had found interesting in the book.
  • Sentence B will come after A because it explains what Jacob could have done with the metal detector.
  • The sentence 'D' is the concluding part because it explains what Jacob finally decided to do to buy a metal detector.

textEn:
Directions: Select the correct passive form of the given sentence.

People say he has lived on a liquid diet for years.
textHi:
Directions: Select the correct passive form of the given sentence.

People say he has lived on a liquid diet for years.
optionsEn: He is said to have lived on a liquid diet for years.
He is said to live on a liquid diet for years.
He has said to have lived on a liquid diet for years.
He is said to have been living on a liquid diet for years.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'He is said to have lived on a liquid diet for years.'

Key Points

  • In Active Voice, a sentence emphasizes the subject, performing an action.
  • In Passive Voice, a sentence emphasizes the action or the object of the sentence.
  • The given sentence is in the active voice and it is in the present simple tense.
  • When we convert this sentence into a passive voice, the subject 'People' of the active voice becomes the hidden object, the object 'he' becomes the subject.
  • The active format "V1 (say)" should be converted into the passive format "is + V3 (said)".
  • This is the active and passive voice rule for the present simple tense.

Hence, the correct answer is option 1.

Additional Information

Verbs of passive format have a different set of tenses (*V3 - Past participle or Third form of the Verb) as given below in the table:

TENSE ACTIVE PASSIVE PASSIVE CONVERSION
Present Simple He plays football am/is/are + V3 Football is played by him
Present Continuous He is playing football am/is/are + being +V3 Football is being played by him
Present Perfect He has played football Has/have + been+ V3 Football has been played by him
Present Perfect Continuous He has been playing football Not used and unusual. Not used and unusual
Past Simple He played football Was/were + V3 Football was played by him
Past Continuous He was playing football Was/were + being + V3 Football was being played by him
Past Perfect He had played football Had + been + V3 Football had been played by him
Past Perfect Continuous He had been playing football Not used and unusual Not used and unusual
Future Simple He will play football Will + be + V3 Football will be played by him
Future Continuous He will be playing football Not used and unusual Not used and unusual
Future Perfect He will have played football Will have been + V3 Football will have been played by him
Future Perfect Continuous He will have been playing football Not used and unusual Not used and unusual

Hinglish

  • Active Voice में , एक वाक्य विषय पर जोर देता है, एक क्रिया करता है।
  • Passive Voice में , एक वाक्य वाक्य की क्रिया या वस्तु पर जोर देता है।
  • दिया गया वाक्य active voice में है और यह present simple tense में है।
  • जब हम इस वाक्य को passive voice में बदलते हैं, तो active voice का subject 'People', hidden object बन जाता है और object 'he', subject बन जाता है।
  • Active format "V1 (say)" को passive format "is + V3 (said)" में परिवर्तित किया जाना चाहिए।
  • यह present simple tense के लिए active and passive voice rule है।

अतः सही उत्तर विकल्प 1 है।

The correct answer is 'He is said to have lived on a liquid diet for years.'

Key Points

  • Active format "V1 (say)" को passive format "is + V3 (said)" में परिवर्तित किया जाना चाहिए।
  • यह present simple tense के लिए active and passive voice rule है।

अतः सही उत्तर विकल्प 1 है।

textEn:

textHi:
Directions: Identify the segment in the sentence which contains a grammatical error.

He is / too immature / for handle / the situation.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'for handle' i.e. this part of the sentence has an error.

Key Points

  • In the given sentence, the use of the preposition 'for' is incorrect.
  • The preposition 'for' is used before the present participle or gerund form of the verb.
  • The to-infinitive is used for expressing the purpose of something or someone's opinion about something.
  • The to-infinitive is: to + base form of the verb (here, handle).
  • Therefore, the preposition 'to' should be used in place of the preposition 'for'.

Hence, the correct answer is option 2.

Correct Sentence: He is too immature to handle the situation.

textEn:
Directions: Select the correct passive form of the given sentence.

Nobody will help you if you are rude.
textHi:
Directions: Select the correct passive form of the given sentence.

Nobody will help you if you are rude.
optionsEn: You will not been helped by nobody if you are rude.
You are not being helped by anybody if you are rude.
You are not helped by anybody if you are rude.
You will not be helped by anybody if you are rude.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'You will not be helped by anybody if you are rude.'

Key Points

  • In Active Voice, a sentence emphasizes the subject, performing an action.
  • In Passive Voice, a sentence emphasizes the action or the object of the sentence.
  • The given sentence is in the active voice and it is in the future simple tense.
  • When we convert this sentence into a passive voice, the subject 'Nobody' of the active voice becomes the object 'anybody', the object 'you' becomes the subject.
  • The active format "will + V1 (help)" should be converted into the passive format "will + be + V3 (helped)".
  • This is the active and passive voice rule for the future simple tense.

Hence, the correct answer is option 4.

Additional Information

Verbs of passive format have a different set of tenses (*V3 - Past participle or Third form of the Verb) as given below in the table:

TENSE ACTIVE PASSIVE PASSIVE CONVERSION
Present Simple He plays football am/is/are + V3 Football is played by him
Present Continuous He is playing football am/is/are + being +V3 Football is being played by him
Present Perfect He has played football Has/have + been+ V3 Football has been played by him
Present Perfect Continuous He has been playing football Not used and unusual. Not used and unusual
Past Simple He played football Was/were + V3 Football was played by him
Past Continuous He was playing football Was/were + being + V3 Football was being played by him
Past Perfect He had played football Had + been + V3 Football had been played by him
Past Perfect Continuous He had been playing football Not used and unusual Not used and unusual
Future Simple He will play football Will + be + V3 Football will be played by him
Future Continuous He will be playing football Not used and unusual Not used and unusual
Future Perfect He will have played football Will have been + V3 Football will have been played by him
Future Perfect Continuous He will have been playing football Not used and unusual Not used and unusual

Hinglish

  • A ctive Voice में , एक वाक्य विषय पर जोर देता है, एक क्रिया करता है।
  • Passive Voice में , एक वाक्य वाक्य की क्रिया या वस्तु पर जोर देता है।
  • दिया गया वाक्य active voice में है और यह future simple tense में है।
  • जब हम इस वाक्य को passive voice में बदलते हैं, तो active voice का subject 'Nobody', object 'anybody' बन जाता है और object 'you', subject बन जाता है।
  • Active format "will + V1 (help)" को passive format "will + be + V3 (helped)" में परिवर्तित किया जाना चाहिए।
  • यह future simple tense के लिए active and passive voice rule है।

अतः सही उत्तर विकल्प 4 है।

textEn:
Directions: Identify the segment in the sentence which contains a grammatical error.

I saw several / French colonial villas / within my visit / to Puducherry.
textHi:
Directions: Identify the segment in the sentence which contains a grammatical error.

I saw several / French colonial villas / within my visit / to Puducherry.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'within my visit' i.e. this part of the sentence has an error.

Key Points

  • In the given sentence, the use of the preposition 'within' is incorrect.
  • The preposition 'within' means not outside the limits of somebody or something.
  • The use of the word 'visit' in the sentence indicates going to see a person or place for a period of time.
  • Therefore, the preposition 'during' should be used in place of the preposition 'within'.
  • The preposition 'during' means within the period of time mentioned.

Hence, the correct answer is option 3.

Correct Sentence: I saw several French colonial villas during my visit to Puducherry.

Hinglish

  • दिए गए वाक्य में preposition 'within' का प्रयोग गलत है।
  • Preposition 'within' का अर्थ है किसी व्‍यक्ति या वस्‍तु की सीमा के अंदर या भीतर।
  • वाक्य में 'visit' शब्द का प्रयोग, किसी व्यक्ति या स्थान को कुछ समय के लिए देखने जाने का संकेत देता है।
  • इसलिए, preposition 'during' का प्रयोग preposition 'within' के स्थान पर किया जाना चाहिए।
  • Preposition 'during' का अर्थ है के दौरान, की अवधि तक, निर्दिष्ट कालावधि में।

अतः सही उत्तर option 3 है।

सही वाक्य: I saw several French colonial villas during my visit to Puducherry.

textEn:
Directions: Select the most appropriate option to substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. If there is no need to substitute it, select 'No substitution'.

No sooner she had put the baby to sleep when a loud noise woke him up.
textHi:
Directions: Select the most appropriate option to substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. If there is no need to substitute it, select 'No substitution'.

No sooner she had put the baby to sleep when a loud noise woke him up.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'Scarcely had she.'

Key Points

  • In the underlined segment, the use of the negative adverbial 'No sooner' is incorrect.
  • The negative adverbial ''No sooner'' is followed by 'than'.
  • The adverbials hardly, scarcely, barely, and no sooner are often used to emphasize that one event quickly followed another.
  • The negative adverbials - Hardly, scarcely, and barely are followed by 'when'.
  • Also, If hardly, scarcely, barely, and no sooner are in the initial position, the subject and auxiliary are inverted (here, had she).
  • Therefore, the negative adverbial 'Scarcely' should be used in place of the negative adverbial 'No sooner'.

Hence, the correct answer is option 1.

Correct Sentence: Scarcely had she put the baby to sleep when a loud noise woke him up.

Hinglish

  • रेखांकित खण्ड में negative adverbial 'No sooner' का प्रयोग गलत है।
  • Negative adverbial ''No sooner'' के बाद 'than' आता है।
  • Adverbials hardly, scarcely, barely, और no sooner इस बात पर जोर देने के लिए कि एक घटना दूसरे के तुरंत बाद आती है।।
  • Negative adverbials - Hardly, scarcely, और barely, के बाद 'when' आता है।
  • इसके अलावा, hardly, scarcely, barely, और no sooner प्रारंभिक स्थिति में हैं, तो subject और auxiliary उलटे होते हैं (यहाँ, had she)।
  • इसलिए negative adverbial 'No sooner' के स्थान पर negative adverbial 'Scarcely' का प्रयोग किया जाना चाहिए।

अतः सही उत्तर option 1 है।

सही वाक्य: Scarcely had she put the baby to sleep when a loud noise woke him up.
explanationHi:
The correct answer is 'Scarcely had she.'

textEn:
Given below are six sentences A-F, of which A and F are static and B-E have been jumbled. Pick the option that gives the correct order of the jumbled sentences.

A. Absulum, the reindeer elf worked in the reindeer barn.

B. He also had to keep the reindeer well-groomed.

C. He had to clean it every day, and that was hard work.

D. He had to give them all a bath and brush their fur.

E. It was such a dirty job to mop, slop, scour and scrape every corner.

F. He worked his fingers to the bone, but it seemed that no one cared.
textHi:
Given below are six sentences A-F, of which A and F are static and B-E have been jumbled. Pick the option that gives the correct order of the jumbled sentences.

A. Absulum, the reindeer elf worked in the reindeer barn.

B. He also had to keep the reindeer well-groomed.

C. He had to clean it every day, and that was hard work.

D. He had to give them all a bath and brush their fur.

E. It was such a dirty job to mop, slop, scour and scrape every corner.

F. He worked his fingers to the bone, but it seemed that no one cared.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'ACEBDF'.

Key Points

  • The given question is an example of a Para Jumble.
  • We know that the first statement of a para jumble question is usually an independent general statement, a noun, a universal fact, starting off a story, starting of an incident or an event, or it starts with 'most' or 'once'.
  • In the given question, Sentence A is the first sentence and sentence F is the last one.
  • We have to rearrange the rest of the sentences accordingly.
  • Sentence A is 'Absulum, the reindeer elf worked in the reindeer barn.' This sentence tells us that 'Absulum' is an elf who worked in the reindeer barn.
  • 'Absulum' had to clean this reindeer barn (a large farm building used for storing grain, hay, or straw or for housing livestock). Hence, Sentence B will be the next one.
  • As he had to clean this barn every day, it was a very dirty job. Hence, Sentence E will be the next sentence.
  • Sentence B will be the next sentence because 'Absulum' kept the reindeer well-groomed.
  • Sentence D will be the next sentence because it is connected with Sentence B because it tells what he did to keep the reindeer well-groomed.
  • Thus, the sequence becomes:
    • A: Absulum, the reindeer elf worked in the reindeer barn.
    • C: He had to clean it every day, and that was hard work.
    • E: It was such a dirty job to mop, slop, scour and scrape every corner.
    • B: He also had to keep the reindeer well-groomed.
    • D: He had to give them all a bath and brush their fur.
    • F: He worked his fingers to the bone, but it seemed that no one cared.
  • Therefore, as per the points mentioned above, we find that the correct answer is Option 2.
    ​

Correct Sequence: Absulum, the reindeer elf worked in the reindeer barn. He had to clean it every day, and that was hard work. It was such a dirty job to mop, slop, scour and scrape every corner. He also had to keep the reindeer well-groomed. He had to give them all a bath and brush their fur. He worked his fingers to the bone, but it seemed that no one cared.

The correct answer is 'ACEBDF'.

    • B: He also had to keep the reindeer well-groomed.
    • D: He had to give them all a bath and brush their fur.

textEn:

AGGRAVATE
textHi:
Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word.

AGGRAVATE
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'intensify'

Key Points

  • The word 'Aggravate' means 'to make something worse or more serious. (स्थिति को) बिगाड़ देना; बदतर करना या होना, अधिक गंभीर बनाना'.
  • The synonyms of the word 'Aggravate' are "annoy, bother, bug, burn (up), chafe, eat, exasperate, frost, gall, get, grate, gripe, hack (off), irk, irritate, itch, nark, nettle, peeve, intensify, persecute, pique, put out, rasp, rile, ruffle, spite, vex".
  • From the synonym of the given word, we can say that the word 'Intensify' is the most similar in meaning.
  • The word 'Intensify' means 'to become or to make something greater or stronger. (तीव्रतर, प्रबल या उग्रतर हो जाना या कर देना)

Hence, the correct answer is option 3. Let's see the meaning of other given options:-

Words Meaning
Influence the power to affect, change or control somebody/something.

Illustrate to explain or make something clear by using examples, pictures, or diagrams.

The correct answer is 'intensify'

  • From the synonym of the given word, we can say that the word 'Intensify' is the most similar in meaning.
  • The word 'Intensify' means 'to become or to make something greater or stronger. (तीव्रतर, प्रबल या उग्रतर हो जाना या कर देना)

Hence, the correct answer is option 3. Let's see the meaning of other given options:-

textEn:
Identify the segment in the sentence which contains a grammatical error.

I asked Dhruv / when he has plan / to settle / the dues.
textHi:
Identify the segment in the sentence which contains a grammatical error.

I asked Dhruv / when he has plan / to settle / the dues.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'when he has plan'.

Key Points

  • The given sentence is grammatically incorrect.
  • Here, 'when he had planned' should be used instead of 'when he has plan'.
  • The given sentence has two errors.
  • First, 'has' should be changed into 'had' and 'plan' should be changed into 'planned'.
  • 'When two actions occur in the past, one after the other, the first action is expressed in Past perfect tense and the second action is expressed in Simple past tense'.
  • For Examples:-
    • The patient had died before the doctor came.
    • He came after she had gone.
  • It is so because the sentence is in the past tense. The action of Dhruv planning to settle the dues happened before the person asked him.
  • Thus, the action of him asking Dhruv will be expressed in simple past tense and the action of Dhruv planning to settle the dues will be expressed in the past perfect tense.
  • Therefore, as per the points mentioned above, we find that the correct answer is Option 3.
    ​

Correct Sentence: I asked Dhruv when he had planned to settle the dues.

Hinglish

  • दिया गया वाक्य व्याकरण की दृष्टि से गलत है।
  • यहां, 'when he has plan' के बजाय 'when he had planned' का उपयोग किया जाना चाहिए।
  • दिए गए वाक्य में दो errors हैं।
  • 'जब दो क्रियाएं अतीत में होती हैं, तो एक के बाद एक, पहली क्रिया को Past perfect tense में और दूसरी क्रिया को Simple past tense में व्यक्त किया जाता है।'
  • उदाहरण के लिए:-
    • The patient had died before the doctor came.
    • He came after she had gone.
  • ऐसा इसलिए है क्योंकि वाक्य भूतकाल में है। ध्रुव की बकाया राशि का निपटान करने की योजना की कार्रवाई उस व्यक्ति के पूछने से पहले ही हो गई।
  • इस प्रकार, ध्रुव से पूछने की उसकी क्रिया simple past tense में व्यक्त की जाएगी और ध्रुव की बकाया राशि का निपटान करने की योजना को past perfect tense में व्यक्त किया जाएगा।
  • पहले 'has' को 'had' में और 'plan' को 'planned' में बदलना चाहिए।
  • इसलिए, ऊपर वर्णित बिंदुओं के अनुसार, हम पाते हैं कि सही उत्तर विकल्प 3 है।

textEn:
Select the correct passive form of the given sentence.

They agreed that the elections should be postponed.
textHi:
Select the correct passive form of the given sentence.

They agreed that the elections should be postponed.
optionsEn: It has been agreed by them that the elections should be postponed.
It had been agreed by them that the elections should be postponed.
It was agreed by them that the elections should be postponed.
It is agreed by them that the elections should be postponed.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'It was agreed by them that the elections should be postponed.'

Key Points

  • The given sentence is in Active Voice. As per the given question we have to change it into Passive Voice.
  • The structure of the given sentence is as follows:
    • The given sentence has two clauses.
    • When a principle clause is followed by a noun clause as an object, the passive voice is made as follows:
      • Principle clause + conjunction + noun clause. (Active Voice)
      • It + was/were + verb + by + object + conjunction + noun clause. (Passive Voice)
    • Example:
      • We hope that Hansa will clear the exam.
      • It is hoped that Hansa will clear the exam.
  • Therefore, as per the points mentioned above, we find that the correct answer is Option 3.
    ​

Correct Sentence: It was agreed by them that the elections should be postponed.

Hinglish

  • ​दिया गया वाक्य Active Voice में है। दिए गए प्रश्न के अनुसार हमें इसे Passive Voice में बदलना है।
  • दिए गए वाक्य की संरचना इस प्रकार है:
    • दिए गए वाक्य में दो उपवाक्य हैं।
    • जब Principle Clause के बाद Noun Clause Object के रूप में होता है तो Passive Voice निम्नानुसार बनाई जाती है:
      • Principle clause + conjunction + noun clause. (Active Voice)
      • It +was/were + verb + by + object + conjunction + noun clause. (Passive Voice)
    • Example:
      • We hope that Hansa will clear the exam.
      • It is hoped that Hansa will clear the exam.
  • इसलिए, ऊपर वर्णित बिंदुओं के अनुसार, हम पाते हैं कि सही उत्तर विकल्प 3 है।

Additional Information

  • Transformation of Infinitives:
  • Structure of it is as follows
    • Subject + is/am/are/was/were/has/have/had + to + V1 + Object.
    • Subject(Objective case) + is/am/are/was/were/has/have/had + to + be + V3 + by + object(subjective case).
    • Example:
      • Your child is to learn chess. (Active Voice)
      • Chess is to be learnt by your child. (Passive Voice)

The correct answer is 'It was agreed by them that the elections should be postponed.'

textEn:
Select the most appropriate option to substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. If there is no need to substitute it, select 'No substitution'.

He replied mine letter after a month.
textHi:
Select the most appropriate option to substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. If there is no need to substitute it, select 'No substitution'.

He replied mine letter after a month.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'replied to my letter'.

Key Points

  • The given sentence is grammatically incorrect.
  • It should be improved from 'replied mine letter' to 'replied to my letter'.
  • In the given sentence 'my' should be used in place of 'mine' because possessive adjectives are used to show ownership of an item or an idea.
  • Hence, the use of the possessive pronoun 'mine' is wrong.
  • Possessive pronouns include those used as adjectives (my, your, his, her, its, our, their, and whose) and those that take the place of nouns (mine, yours, his, hers, ours, and theirs). Note that possessive pronouns do not use an apostrophe to indicate a possessive form.
  • After the verb 'replied' the preposition 'to' should be used because the verb "respond" requires a prepositional phrase if in the sentence, for one reason or another, it becomes necessary to explain "to whom." Hence, 'to' should be used.
  • Therefore, as per the points mentioned above, we find that the correct answer is Option 2.
    ​

Correct Sentence: He replied to my letter after a month.

Hinglish

  • दिया गया वाक्य व्याकरण की दृष्टि से गलत है।
  • इसे 'replied mine letter' से 'replied to my letter' कर दिया जाए।
  • दिए गए वाक्य में 'mine' के स्थान पर 'my' का प्रयोग किया जाना चाहिए क्योंकि किसी वस्तु या विचार के स्वामित्व को दर्शाने के लिए possessive adjectives का प्रयोग किया जाता है।
  • अत: possessive pronoun 'mine' का प्रयोग गलत है।
  • Possessive Pronous में वे शामिल होते हैं जो adjective के रूप में उपयोग किए जाते हैं (my, your, his, her, its, our, their, and whose) और वे जो संज्ञाओं (mine, yours, his, hers, ours, and theirs) का स्थान लेते हैं। ध्यान दें कि स्वामित्व वाले सर्वनाम एक स्वत्वबोधक रूप को इंगित करने के लिए एक एस्ट्रोफ़े का उपयोग नहीं करते हैं।
  • Verb 'replied' के बाद preposition 'to' का उपयोग किया जाना चाहिए क्योंकि क्रिया "respond" के लिए एक prepositional phrase की आवश्यकता होती है यदि वाक्य में, एक कारण या किसी अन्य के लिए, "to whom" समझाना आवश्यक हो जाता है। अतः 'to' का प्रयोग करना चाहिए।
  • इसलिए, ऊपर वर्णित बिंदुओं के अनुसार, हम पाते हैं कि सही उत्तर विकल्प 2 है।
explanationHi:
The correct answer is 'replied to my letter'.

Key Points

  • The given sentence is grammatically incorrect.
  • It should be improved from 'replied mine letter' to 'replied to my letter'.
  • In the given sentence 'my' should be used in place of 'mine' because possessive adjectives are used to show ownership of an item or an idea.
  • Hence, the use of the possessive pronoun 'mine' is wrong.
  • Possessive pronouns include those used as adjectives (my, your, his, her, its, our, their, and whose) and those that take the place of nouns (mine, yours, his, hers, ours, and theirs). Note that possessive pronouns do not use an apostrophe to indicate a possessive form.
  • After the verb 'replied' the preposition 'to' should be used because the verb "respond" requires a prepositional phrase if in the sentence, for one reason or another, it becomes necessary to explain "to whom." Hence, 'to' should be used.
  • Therefore, as per the points mentioned above, we find that the correct answer is Option 2.
    ​

textEn:
Select the most appropriate meaning of the underlined idiom in the given sentence.

He knows the ins and outs of the case and has been chosen to defend the accused.
textHi:
Select the most appropriate meaning of the underlined idiom in the given sentence.

He knows the ins and outs of the case and has been chosen to defend the accused.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'complete details'.

Key Points

  • The meaning of the given idiom 'ins and outs' is 'complete details'.
  • Ins and outs: the detailed or complicated facts of something. (पूरा विवरण)
    • Example: I know how to use computers, but I don't really understand the ins and outs of how they work.
  • The given sentence talks about the person who knows complete details of the case and has been chosen to defend the accused (a person who is on trial in a law court).
  • Therefore, as per the points mentioned above, we find that the correct answer is Option 4.
    ​

Correct Answer: Complete details

Additional Information

  • Let's look at the meaning and examples of some idioms of the word 'Play':
Idiom Meaning Example
play (merry) hell with sth to seriously damage or confuse something The power cuts played hell with our computers.
play (sth) by ear to play a piece of music by remembering the notes He did not need sheet music. He could play the piece by ear.
play a joke/trick on sb to confuse someone or cause a problem for them Fate played a cruel trick on him when he was injured in his first game.

The correct answer is 'complete details'.

textEn:
Identify the segment in the sentence which contains a grammatical error.

His loyalty and honesty / are above / suspect/ No error
textHi:
Identify the segment in the sentence which contains a grammatical error.

His loyalty and honesty / are above / suspect/ No error
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'suspect'.

Key Points

  • The given sentence is grammatically incorrect.
  • Here, 'suspicion' should be used instead of 'suspect'.
  • A word family is a group of words that are derived from a common stem, such as choose, choice, chosen, and choosable.
  • Words in a family may belong to different parts of speech, but they usually have closely related meanings.
  • Some families contain multiple words from the same part of speech, such as relation and relationship.
  • While the meanings of these words may be similar, they are usually not interchangeable.
  • Selecting the right word from the family depends on context.
  • Let's see the examples below:
    • There is a different between love and attachment.​
    • There is a difference between love and attachment.
  • In the given context a 'noun' form should be used.
  • The word 'suspicion' means 'a feeling or belief that something is wrong or that somebody has done something wrong.'
    • Example: She was arrested on suspicion of murder.
  • Therefore, as per the points mentioned above, we find that the correct answer is Option 3.
    ​

Correct Sentence: His loyalty and honesty are above suspicion.

Hinglish

  • दिया गया वाक्य व्याकरण की दृष्टि से गलत है।
  • यहां 'suspect' के स्थान पर 'suspicion' का प्रयोग करना चाहिए।
  • एक शब्द परिवार शब्दों का एक समूह है जो एक common stem से लिया गया है, जैसे कि choose, choice, chosen, and choosable.

  • कुछ परिवारों में भाषण के एक ही हिस्से से कई शब्द होते हैं, जैसे relation and relationship.
