textEn:
Given below are four sentences in jumbled order. Pick the option that gives their correct order.

A. Suddenly in the evening, a dark and menacing storm quickly gathered.

B. Prashant had gone to Ersama, a coastal town in Odisha, to spend the day with a friend.

C. The destruction wrought by the storm continued for the next 36 hours, although the wind speed reduced.

D. Winds beat against the houses with a fury never witnessed before.
textHi:
Given below are four sentences in jumbled order. Pick the option that gives their correct order.

A. Suddenly in the evening, a dark and menacing storm quickly gathered.

B. Prashant had gone to Ersama, a coastal town in Odisha, to spend the day with a friend.

C. The destruction wrought by the storm continued for the next 36 hours, although the wind speed reduced.

D. Winds beat against the houses with a fury never witnessed before.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'BADC'.

Key Points

  • The given question is an example of a Para Jumble.
  • We know that the first statement of a para jumbled question is usually an independent general statement, a noun, a universal fact, starting off a story, starting of an incident or an event, or it starts with 'most' or 'once'.
  • Sentence B in the given question is the starting of a story. The author tells us that 'Prashant' had gone to 'Ersama' which is located in Odisha to meet his friend.
  • Sentence A will be the next sentence because it tells us about the weather of 'Ersama' when he was there.
  • Sentence D will be the next sentence because it further explains how the storm-ravaged the situation there.
  • Lastly, Sentence C will be used.
  • Thus, the sequence becomes:
    • B: Prashant had gone to Ersama, a coastal town in Odisha, to spend the day with a friend.
    • A: Suddenly in the evening, a dark and menacing storm quickly gathered.
    • D: Winds beat against the houses with a fury never witnessed before.
    • C: The destruction wrought by the storm continued for the next 36 hours, although the wind speed reduced.
  • Therefore, as per the points mentioned above, we find that the correct answer is Option 3.


Correct Sequence: Prashant had gone to Ersama, a coastal town in Odisha, to spend the day with a friend. Suddenly in the evening, a dark and menacing storm quickly gathered. Winds beat against the houses with a fury never witnessed before. The destruction wrought by the storm continued for the next 36 hours, although the wind speed reduced.
explanationHi:
The correct answer is 'BADC'.

Key Points

  • The given question is an example of a Para Jumble.
  • We know that the first statement of a para jumbled question is usually an independent general statement, a noun, a universal fact, starting off a story, starting of an incident or an event, or it starts with 'most' or 'once'.
  • Sentence B in the given question is the starting of a story. The author tells us that 'Prashant' had gone to 'Ersama' which is located in Odisha to meet his friend.
  • Sentence A will be the next sentence because it tells us about the weather of 'Ersama' when he was there.
  • Sentence D will be the next sentence because it further explains how the storm-ravaged the situation there.
  • Lastly, Sentence C will be used.
  • Thus, the sequence becomes:
    • B: Prashant had gone to Ersama, a coastal town in Odisha, to spend the day with a friend.
    • A: Suddenly in the evening, a dark and menacing storm quickly gathered.
    • D: Winds beat against the houses with a fury never witnessed before.
    • C: The destruction wrought by the storm continued for the next 36 hours, although the wind speed reduced.
  • Therefore, as per the points mentioned above, we find that the correct answer is Option 3.


Correct Sequence: Prashant had gone to Ersama, a coastal town in Odisha, to spend the day with a friend. Suddenly in the evening, a dark and menacing storm quickly gathered. Winds beat against the houses with a fury never witnessed before. The destruction wrought by the storm continued for the next 36 hours, although the wind speed reduced.

textEn:
Given below are six sentences A-F, of which A and F are static and B-E have been jumbled. Pick the option that gives the correct order of the jumbled sentences.

A. Suddenly I noticed smoke pouring in through the seams of the ceiling.

B. Groping our way to the front door we all ran out into the front yard.

C. The smoke began to fill the room so quickly that we could barely see.

D. I ran to the neighbours to call the fire department.

E. By the time we made our way outside, the whole roof was engulfed in flames.

F. I saw mother run back into the house and I screamed, "Mom! No!"
textHi:
Given below are six sentences A-F, of which A and F are static and B-E have been jumbled. Pick the option that gives the correct order of the jumbled sentences.

A. Suddenly I noticed smoke pouring in through the seams of the ceiling.

B. Groping our way to the front door we all ran out into the front yard.

C. The smoke began to fill the room so quickly that we could barely see.

D. I ran to the neighbours to call the fire department.

E. By the time we made our way outside, the whole roof was engulfed in flames.

F. I saw mother run back into the house and I screamed, "Mom! No!"
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'ACBEDF'.

Key Points

  • The given question is an example of a Para Jumble.
  • We know that the first statement of a para jumbled question is usually an independent general statement, a noun, a universal fact, starting off a story, starting of an incident or an event, or it starts with 'most' or 'once'.
  • In the given question, sentence A is the first sentence. And sentence F is the last one.
  • We have to rearrange the rest of the sentences accordingly.
  • Sentence A is 'Suddenly I noticed smoke pouring in through the seams of the ceiling.' this is the starting of an incident.
  • Sentence A tells us that the author noticed the smoke coming from the ceiling. After it, Sentence C should be used because it expresses 'how smoke' began to fill the room.
  • After seeing the smoke in the room, the author ran out of the room to the front yard. Hence, Sentence B should be the next one.
  • Sentence E should be the next one because it tells us how the roof got engulfed in flames as soon as they ran out into the front yard.
  • Next, Sentence D should be used next because Sentence D tells us about what the author did after reaching the front yard i.e. he ran to the neighbours to call the fire department.
  • Thus the sequence becomes:
    • A: Suddenly I noticed smoke pouring in through the seams of the ceiling.
    • C: The smoke began to fill the room so quickly that we could barely see.
    • B: Groping our way to the front door we all ran out into the front yard.
    • E: By the time we made our way outside, the whole roof was engulfed in flames.
    • D: I ran to the neighbours to call the fire department.
    • F: I saw mother run back into the house and I screamed, "Mom! No!"
  • Therefore, as per the points mentioned above, we find that the correct answer is Option 2.
    ​

Correct Sequence: Suddenly I noticed smoke pouring in through the seams of the ceiling. The smoke began to fill the room so quickly that we could barely see. Groping our way to the front door we all ran out into the front yard. By the time we made our way outside, the whole roof was engulfed in flames. I ran to the neighbours to call the fire department. I saw mother run back into the house and I screamed, "Mom! No!"
explanationHi:
The correct answer is 'ACBEDF'.

Key Points

  • The given question is an example of a Para Jumble.
  • We know that the first statement of a para jumbled question is usually an independent general statement, a noun, a universal fact, starting off a story, starting of an incident or an event, or it starts with 'most' or 'once'.
  • In the given question, sentence A is the first sentence. And sentence F is the last one.
  • We have to rearrange the rest of the sentences accordingly.
  • Sentence A is 'Suddenly I noticed smoke pouring in through the seams of the ceiling.' this is the starting of an incident.
  • Sentence A tells us that the author noticed the smoke coming from the ceiling. After it, Sentence C should be used because it expresses 'how smoke' began to fill the room.
  • After seeing the smoke in the room, the author ran out of the room to the front yard. Hence, Sentence B should be the next one.
  • Sentence E should be the next one because it tells us how the roof got engulfed in flames as soon as they ran out into the front yard.
  • Next, Sentence D should be used next because Sentence D tells us about what the author did after reaching the front yard i.e. he ran to the neighbours to call the fire department.
  • Thus the sequence becomes:
    • A: Suddenly I noticed smoke pouring in through the seams of the ceiling.
    • C: The smoke began to fill the room so quickly that we could barely see.
    • B: Groping our way to the front door we all ran out into the front yard.
    • E: By the time we made our way outside, the whole roof was engulfed in flames.
    • D: I ran to the neighbours to call the fire department.
    • F: I saw mother run back into the house and I screamed, "Mom! No!"
  • Therefore, as per the points mentioned above, we find that the correct answer is Option 2.
    ​

Correct Sequence: Suddenly I noticed smoke pouring in through the seams of the ceiling. The smoke began to fill the room so quickly that we could barely see. Groping our way to the front door we all ran out into the front yard. By the time we made our way outside, the whole roof was engulfed in flames. I ran to the neighbours to call the fire department. I saw mother run back into the house and I screamed, "Mom! No!"

textEn:
Select the correct passive form of the given sentence.

The committee has discussed the matter for several days.
textHi:
Select the correct passive form of the given sentence.

The committee has discussed the matter for several days.
optionsEn: The matter is being discussed by the committee for several days.
The matter has discussed by the committee for several days.
The matter was discussed by the committee for several days.
The matter has been discussed by the committee for several days.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'The matter has been discussed by the committee for several days.'

Key Points

  • The given sentence is in Active Voice. As per the given question, we have to change it into Passive Voice.
  • The structure of the given sentence is as follows:
    • Subject + has/have + V3 + Object. (Active Voice)
    • Subject (Objective Case) + has/have + been + V3 + by + Object (Subjective Case). (Passive Voice)
  • Example:
    • We have finalized the site for our new factory. (Active Voice)
    • The site for our new factory has been finalized by us. (Passive Voice)
  • The subject of the given sentence is 'The committee' and the object of the given sentence is 'the matter'.
  • The subject will be put in place of the object and the object will be put in place of the subject.
  • And 'has discussed' will be changed into 'has been discussed'.
  • Therefore, as per the points mentioned above, we find that the correct answer is Option 4.
    ​

Correct Sentence: The matter has been discussed by the committee for several days.

Hinglish

  • दिया गया वाक्य Active Voice में है। दिए गए प्रश्न के अनुसार हमें इसे Passive Voice में बदलना है।
  • दिए गए वाक्य की संरचना इस प्रकार है:
    • Subject + has/have + V3 + Object. (Active Voice)
    • Subject (Objective Case)+ has/have + been + V3 + by + Object (Subjective Case). (Passive Voice)
  • Example:
    • We have finalized the site for our new factory. (Active Voice)
    • The site for our new factory has been finalized by us. (Passive Voice)
  • दिए गए वाक्य का subject 'The committee' है और दिए गए वाक्य का object 'the matter' है।
  • Object के स्थान पर Subject रखा जाएगा और Subject के स्थान पर Object रखी जाएगी।
  • और 'has discussed' को 'has been discussed' में बदल दिया जाएगा।
  • इसलिए, ऊपर वर्णित बिंदुओं के अनुसार, हम पाते हैं कि सही उत्तर विकल्प 4 है।

textEn:
Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank.

I have nobody ______ I can confide in.
textHi:
Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank.

I have nobody ______ I can confide in.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'whom'.

Key Points

  • Here, in the given fill-in-the-blank the most appropriate answer is 'whom'.
  • 'Whom' is used instead of 'who' as the object of a verb or preposition. ('Whom' preposition या verb के object के रूप में 'who' के स्‍थान पर प्रयुक्त होता है)
    • Example: I met a man with whom I used to work.
  • Therefore, as per the points mentioned above, we find that the correct answer is Option 4.

Correct Sentence: I have nobody whom I can confide in.

Additional Information

  • Let's look at the meanings of other words given in the options:
Words Meaning
Which used to add extra information to a previous clause, in writing usually after a comma. (आमतौर पर अल्पविराम के बाद लिखित रूप में, पिछले खंड में अतिरिक्त जानकारी जोड़ने के लिए उपयोग किया जाता है)

Whoever

any person who; used in questions as a way of expressing surprise. (कोई भी व्यक्ति जो; प्रश्नों में आश्चर्य व्यक्त करने के तरीके के रूप में उपयोग किया जाता है)

textEn:

textHi:

She told me that she had given my message to my mother.
optionsEn: She said to me, "She gave my message to my mother."

  • The given sentence is in Indirect Speech. As per the question we have to change it into Direct Speech.
  • The process of transformation is as follows:
    • 'told' will be changed into 'said to' because in the given sentence reporting Verb has an 'object'.
    • Comma and inverted commas will be added.
    • 'My' will be changed into 'your' because in 'direct to indirect' transformation second-person pronouns are changed according to the subject of the object of the reporting verb.
    • 'she' will be changed into 'I' because we know that it changes according to the subject of the reporting verb.
    • Here, the subject of the reporting verb is ''She''.
    • The past perfect tense 'Had given' is changed to simple past tense 'gave'.
    • Because we know that the given sentence is in the past perfect tense and 'Had + V3' is changed into 'V2' (past perfect tense changes to simple past tense).
  • Therefore, as per the points mentioned above, we find that the correct answer is Option 3.
    ​

  • परिवर्तन की प्रक्रिया इस प्रकार है:
    • 'Told' को 'said to' में बदल दिया जाएगा क्योंकि दिए गए वाक्य में रिपोर्टिंग वर्ब में 'ऑब्जेक्ट' है।

    • 'she' को 'I' में बदल दिया जाएगा क्योंकि हम जानते हैं कि यह reporting verb के subject के अनुसार बदलता है।
    • यहाँ, reporting verb का विषय ''she'' है।

    • क्योंकि हम जानते हैं कि दिया गया वाक्य past perfect tense ​​में है और 'Had + V3' को 'V2' में बदल दिया गया है।
  • इसलिए, ऊपर वर्णित बिंदुओं के अनुसार, हम पाते हैं कि सही उत्तर विकल्प 3 है।

textEn:
Select the most appropriate option to substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. If there is no need to substitute it, select 'No substitution'.

Accordingly to my opinion your essay is the best.
textHi:
Select the most appropriate option to substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. If there is no need to substitute it, select 'No substitution'.

Accordingly to my opinion your essay is the best.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'According to me'.

Key Points

  • The given sentence is grammatically incorrect.
  • Here, 'according to me' should be used instead of 'accordingly to my opinion'.
  • 'Accordingly' is an adverb, and never begins a sentence.
  • It means "in this way, following rules."
    • Example: The boss told me how to do the project, and I acted accordingly.
  • 'According to' means "following; in a way that agrees with" (similar meaning to accordingly) but it can begin a sentence and is usually followed by a noun or a noun phrase.
    • Example: According to the memo we have just received, all employees must now park in the company's parking lot.
  • We usually use an objective pronoun after a preposition, which serves as the object of a preposition or the object of a verb or a sentence.
  • Object pronouns are those pronouns that receive the action in a sentence.
  • They are me, you, him, her, us, them, and whom.
  • Any noun receiving action in the sentence, like these pronouns, is an object and is categorized as an objective case.
  • Hence, in the given sentence 'me' should be used instead of 'my'.
  • Therefore, as per the points mentioned above, we find that the correct answer is Option 3.
    ​

Correct Sentence: According to me your essay is the best.


Hinglish

  • दिया गया वाक्य व्याकरण की दृष्टि से गलत है।
  • यहाँ 'accordingly to my opinion' के स्थान पर 'according to me' का प्रयोग करना चाहिए।
  • 'Accordingly' एक adverb है, और कभी भी एक वाक्य शुरू नहीं करता है।
  • इसका अर्थ है "इस तरह; नियमों का पालन करना।"
    • Example: The boss told me how to do the project, and I acted accordingly.
  • 'According to' का अर्थ है "निम्नलिखित; के अनुसार" (इसका वही अर्थ है जो 'accordingly' का है) लेकिन यह एक वाक्य शुरू कर सकता है।
    • Example: According to the memo we have just received, all employees must now park in the company's parking lot.
  • हम आमतौर पर एक preposition के बाद एक obejctive pronoun का उपयोग करते हैं, जो object of a preposition या object of a verb के रूप में कार्य करता है।
  • Object pronouns वे सर्वनाम होते हैं जो एक वाक्य में क्रिया प्राप्त करते हैं।
  • वे me, you, him, her, us, them, और whom हैं।
  • इन सर्वनामों की तरह, वाक्य में क्रिया प्राप्त करने वाली कोई भी संज्ञा एक object है और इसे एक objective case के रूप में वर्गीकृत किया जाता है।
  • अतः दिए गए वाक्य में 'my' के स्थान पर 'me' का प्रयोग होना चाहिए।
  • इसलिए, ऊपर वर्णित बिंदुओं के अनुसार, हम पाते हैं कि सही उत्तर विकल्प 3 है।

textEn:
Given below are four sentences in jumbled order. Pick the option that gives their correct order.

A. The zip on the bag got jammed and wouldn't open no matter how hard I pulled at it.

B. We were flying to England for a week.

C. I also remembered that I had put the frequent flyer card in the carry-on bag hanging around my neck.

D. As we were checking in, I remembered that I was a member of the frequent flyer programme.
textHi:
Given below are four sentences in jumbled order. Pick the option that gives their correct order.

A. The zip on the bag got jammed and wouldn't open no matter how hard I pulled at it.

B. We were flying to England for a week.

C. I also remembered that I had put the frequent flyer card in the carry-on bag hanging around my neck.

D. As we were checking in, I remembered that I was a member of the frequent flyer programme.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'BDCA'.

Key Points

  • The given question is an example of a Para Jumble.
  • We know that the first statement of a para jumbled question is usually an independent general statement, a noun, a universal fact, starting of an incident, or it starts with 'most' or 'once'.
  • Sentence B in the given question is the starting of an incident. It talks about the author flying to England for a week.
  • Sentence D will be the next sentence because it tells about the events that happened when the author was checking in at the airport.
  • Sentence C will be the next sentence because it is connected with Sentence D and further tells that the author had kept the frequent flyer card in the carry-on bag hanging around his neck.
  • Lastly, sentence A will be used because it tells about the bag's zip getting jammed, and the author couldn't open it.
  • Thus, the sequence becomes:
    • B: We were flying to England for a week.
    • D: As we were checking in, I remembered that I was a member of the frequent flyer programme.
    • C: I also remembered that I had put the frequent flyer card in the carry-on bag hanging around my neck.
    • A: The zip on the bag got jammed and wouldn't open no matter how hard I pulled at it.
  • Therefore, as per the points mentioned above, we find that the correct answer is Option 1.

Correct Sequence: We were flying to England for a week. As we were checking in, I remembered that I was a member of the frequent flyer programme. I also remembered that I had put the frequent flyer card in the carry-on bag hanging around my neck. The zip on the bag got jammed and wouldn't open no matter how hard I pulled at it.
explanationHi:
The correct answer is 'BDCA'.

Key Points

  • The given question is an example of a Para Jumble.
  • We know that the first statement of a para jumbled question is usually an independent general statement, a noun, a universal fact, starting of an incident, or it starts with 'most' or 'once'.
  • Sentence B in the given question is the starting of an incident. It talks about the author flying to England for a week.
  • Sentence D will be the next sentence because it tells about the events that happened when the author was checking in at the airport.
  • Sentence C will be the next sentence because it is connected with Sentence D and further tells that the author had kept the frequent flyer card in the carry-on bag hanging around his neck.
  • Lastly, sentence A will be used because it tells about the bag's zip getting jammed, and the author couldn't open it.
  • Thus, the sequence becomes:
    • B: We were flying to England for a week.
    • D: As we were checking in, I remembered that I was a member of the frequent flyer programme.
    • C: I also remembered that I had put the frequent flyer card in the carry-on bag hanging around my neck.
    • A: The zip on the bag got jammed and wouldn't open no matter how hard I pulled at it.
  • Therefore, as per the points mentioned above, we find that the correct answer is Option 1.

Correct Sequence: We were flying to England for a week. As we were checking in, I remembered that I was a member of the frequent flyer programme. I also remembered that I had put the frequent flyer card in the carry-on bag hanging around my neck. The zip on the bag got jammed and wouldn't open no matter how hard I pulled at it.

textEn:
Select the correct active form of the given sentence.

Nobody was being ridiculed by me.
textHi:
Select the correct active form of the given sentence.

Nobody was being ridiculed by me.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'I was not ridiculing anybody.'

Key Points

  • The given sentence is in the Passive Voice. As per the given question we have to change the sentence into Active Form.
  • The given sentence is an example of 'past continuous tense'.
  • The structure of these kinds of sentences is:
    • Subject + was/were + V1 + ing + Object. (Active Voice)
    • Subject(Objective Case) + was/were + being + V3 + by + Object (subjective case). (Passive Voice)
  • Example:
    • He was riding a bike.
    • A bike was being ridden by him.
  • Here, in the given sentence 'was being ridiculed' will be changed into 'was not ridiculing'.
  • The preposition 'by' will be removed.
  • 'Nobody' changes to 'anybody'.
  • 'me' will be changed into 'I' (subject).
  • Therefore, as per the points mentioned above, we find that the correct answer is Option 1.

Correct Sentence: I was not ridiculing anybody.​

Hinglish

  • दिया गया वाक्य Passive Voice में है। दिए गए प्रश्न के अनुसार हमें वाक्य को Active Voice में बदलना है।
  • दिया गया वाक्य 'past continuous tense' का उदाहरण है।
  • इस प्रकार के वाक्यों की संरचना है:
    • Subject + was/were + V1 + ing + Object. (Active Voice)
    • Subject(Objective Case) + was/were + being + V3 + by + Object (subjective case). (Passive Voice)
  • उदाहरण:
    • He was riding a bike.
    • A bike was being ridden by him.
  • यहाँ दिए गए वाक्य में 'was being ridiculed' को 'was not ridiculing' में बदल दिया जाएगा।
  • Preposition 'by' हटा दिया जाएगा।
  • 'me' को 'I' (subject) में बदल दिया जाएगा।
  • 'Nobody' 'anybody' में बदल जाता है।
  • इसलिए, ऊपर वर्णित बिंदुओं के अनुसार, हम पाते हैं कि सही उत्तर विकल्प 1 है।

Additional Information

  • Transformation of Infinitives:
    • Subject + is/am/are/was/were/has/have/had + to + V1 + Object. (Active Voice)
    • Subject (objective case) + is/am/are/was/were/has/have/had + to + be + V3 + by + object (Subjective case). (Passive Voice)
  • Example:
    • The teacher is to solve the doubt of every student.
    • The doubt of every student is to be solved by the teacher.

textEn:
Select the correct direct form of the given sentence.

Anu said that she wanted to see if she could do a back-flip.
textHi:
Select the correct direct form of the given sentence.

Anu said that she wanted to see if she could do a back-flip.
optionsEn: Anu said, "I wanted to see her doing a back-flip."
Anu said, "I want to see if I can do a back-flip."
Anu said, "I want to do a back-flip if I can."
Anu said, "Let her see if I could do a back-flip."
explanationEn:
The correct answer is Anu said, "I want to see if I can do a back-flip."

Key Points

  • The given sentence is in Indirect Speech. As per the question we have to change it into Direct Speech.
  • The process of transformation is as follows:
    • Comma and inverted commas will be added.
    • 'She' will be changed into 'I' because in 'direct to indirect' transformation the first-person pronouns are changed according to the subject of the reporting verb.
    • 'Wanted' will be changed into 'want'.
    • 'could' will be changed into 'can'.
  • Therefore, as per the points mentioned above, we find that the correct answer is Option 2.

​Correct Sentence: Anu said, "I want to see if I can do a back-flip".


Hinglish

  • दिया गया वाक्य इनडायरेक्ट स्पीच में है। प्रश्न के अनुसार हमें इसे डायरेक्ट स्पीच में बदलना होगा।

    • 'Wanted' को 'want' में बदल दिया जाएगा।
    • 'Could' को 'can' में बदल दिया जाएगा।
  • इसलिए, ऊपर वर्णित बिंदुओं के अनुसार, हम पाते हैं कि सही उत्तर विकल्प 2 है।

textEn:

The correct answer is 'We object to this unfair proposal.'

  • Example:

    • English is taught by Aastha. (Passive Voice)
  • The subject of the given sentence is 'This unfair proposal'.

  • 'is objected to' will be changed into 'object to'.
  • Therefore, as per the points mentioned above, we find that the correct answer is Option 2.

textEn:
Select the correct direct form of the given sentence.

The teacher asked the students if they could prepare for the debate by the following week.
textHi:
Select the correct direct form of the given sentence.

The teacher asked the students if they could prepare for the debate by the following week.
optionsEn: The teacher asked to the students, "Could you prepare for the debate by the following week?"
The teacher said to the students, "Will you prepare for the debate by the following week?"
The teacher said to the students, "Can you prepare for the debate by next week?"
The teacher said to the students, "Are you prepared for the debate by next week?"
explanationEn:
The correct answer is The teacher said to the students, "Can you prepare for the debate by next week?"

Key Points

  • The given sentence is in Indirect Speech. As per the question we have to change it into Direct Speech.
  • The process of transformation is as follows:
    • Here, 'asked' will be changed into 'said to'.
    • Comma and inverted commas will be added.
    • The conjunction 'if' will be removed.
    • We know that second-person pronouns are changed according to the object (the students) of the reporting verb.
    • 'Could' will be changed into 'can'.
    • The adverb 'the following week' will be changed into 'next week'.
  • ​Therefore, as per the points mentioned above, we find that the correct answer is Option 3.
    ​

Correct Sentence: The teacher said to the students, "Can you prepare for the debate by next week?"

textEn:
Select the most appropriate option to substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. If there is no need to substitute it, select 'No substitution'.

The prisoner was set at freedom early.
textHi:
Select the most appropriate option to substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. If there is no need to substitute it, select 'No substitution'.

The prisoner was set at freedom early.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'set free'.

Key Points

  • The given sentence is grammatically incorrect.
  • Here, 'set free' should be used instead of 'set at freedom'.
  • We know that 'set free' is an idiomatic expression.
  • Set free (from something): To liberate someone or something from imprisonment or confinement. (किसी को या किसी चीज को कारावास से मुक्त करना)
    • Example: The activists snuck into the lab and set free nearly 200 animals being used for biological experiments.
  • Therefore, as per the points mentioned above, we find that the correct answer is Option 3.

Correct Sentence: The prisoner was set free early.

textEn:
Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank.

He is both diligent ______ efficient.
textHi:
Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank.

He is both diligent ______ efficient.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'and'.

Key Points

  • Here, in the given fill-in-the-blank the most appropriate answer is 'and'.
  • We know that 'both' is followed by 'and'.
  • We use "both...and" to emphasize the link between two things.
    • Example: Both Britain and France agree on the treaty.
  • Therefore, as per the points mentioned above, we find that the correct answer is Option 1.

Correct Sentence: He is both diligent and efficient.

Hinglish

  • यहाँ, दिए गए रिक्त स्थान की पूर्ति में सबसे उपयुक्त उत्तर 'and' है।
  • हम जानते हैं कि 'both' के बाद 'and' आता है।
  • हम दो चीजों के बीच की कड़ी पर जोर देने के लिए "both ... and" का उपयोग करते हैं।
    • Example: Both Britain and France agree on the treaty.
  • इसलिए, ऊपर वर्णित बिंदुओं के अनुसार, हम पाते हैं कि सही उत्तर विकल्प 1 है।

Additional Information

  • 'Hardly/Scarcely' is followed by 'when'.
    • Example:​ Hardly did I see him when I stopped my car.

textEn:
Given below are four sentences in jumbled order. Pick the option that gives their correct order.

A. Some were big and floated around while others were small and popped immediately.

B. Nancy's uncle gifted her a bubble shooter for her birthday.

C. She chased them around, trying to hold them in her hands.

D. Now, Nancy could blow bubbles of all sizes.
textHi:
Given below are four sentences in jumbled order. Pick the option that gives their correct order.

A. Some were big and floated around while others were small and popped immediately.

B. Nancy's uncle gifted her a bubble shooter for her birthday.

C. She chased them around, trying to hold them in her hands.

D. Now, Nancy could blow bubbles of all sizes.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'BDAC'.

Key Points

  • The given question is an example of a Para Jumble.
  • We know that the first statement of a para jumbled question is usually an independent general statement, a noun, a universal fact, starting of an incident, or it starts with 'most' or 'once'.
  • Sentence B in the given question is the starting of an incident. It tells us that Nancy's uncle gifted her a bubble shooter on her birthday.
  • Sentence D will be the next sentence because it is connected with Sentence B.
  • Sentence A will be the next sentence because it tells us about the different types of bubbles and their respective sizes.
  • Lastly, Sentence C will be used because it tells how Nancy played with those bubbles.
  • Thus, the sequence becomes:
    • B: Nancy's uncle gifted her a bubble shooter for her birthday.
    • D: Now, Nancy could blow bubbles of all sizes.
    • A: Some were big and floated around while others were small and popped immediately.
    • C: She chased them around, trying to hold them in her hands.
  • Therefore, as per the points mentioned above, we find that the correct answer is Option 1.

Correct Sequence: Nancy's uncle gifted her a bubble shooter for her birthday. Now, Nancy could blow bubbles of all sizes. Some were big and floated around while others were small and popped immediately. She chased them around, trying to hold them in her hands.

The correct answer is 'BDAC'.

  • Sentence D will be the next sentence because it is connected with Sentence B.
  • Sentence A will be the next sentence because it tells us about the different types of bubbles and their respective sizes.
  • Lastly, Sentence C will be used because it tells how Nancy played with those bubbles.

    • A: Some were big and floated around while others were small and popped immediately.

Correct Sequence: Nancy's uncle gifted her a bubble shooter for her birthday. Now, Nancy could blow bubbles of all sizes. Some were big and floated around while others were small and popped immediately. She chased them around, trying to hold them in her hands.

textEn:
Select the most appropriate option to substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. If there is no need to substitute it, select 'No substitution'.

The porter denied to take the money.
textHi:
Select the most appropriate option to substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. If there is no need to substitute it, select 'No substitution'.

The porter denied to take the money.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'denied taking'.

Key Points

  • The given sentence is grammatically incorrect.
  • Here, 'denied taking' should be used instead of 'denied to take'.
  • A gerund is formed from a verb, always ends in -ing, and always acts as a noun.
  • When following a verb, a gerund or gerund phrase is the object of the verb, a subject complement, or the subject of an inverted sentence.
  • Certain verbs are almost always followed by a gerund. Unfortunately, there is no easy way to remember what those verbs are.
  • Let's look at some verbs:
    • Begin, start, love, like, hate, stop, finish, enjoy, prefer, fear, remember, forget, mind, miss, suggest, practice, understand, keep, admit, deny, detest, rest, defer, worth, risk, regret, consider.
  • Example:
    • She started singing.
  • Thus, the gerund 'taking' will be used in place of 'to take'.
  • Therefore, as per the points mentioned above, we find that the correct answer is Option 2.
    ​

Correct Sentence: The porter denied taking the money.

Hinglish

  • दिया गया वाक्य व्याकरण की दृष्टि से गलत है।

  • एक gerund एक verb से बनता है, हमेशा -ing में समाप्त होता है, और हमेशा एक noun के रूप में कार्य करता है।
  • एक Verb का पालन करते समय, एक gerund या gerund phrase verb का object है, एक subject complement, या एक उल्टे वाक्य का subject है।
  • कुछ क्रियाओं का लगभग हमेशा एक gerund द्वारा पालन किया जाता है। दुर्भाग्य से, उन क्रियाओं को याद रखने का कोई आसान तरीका नहीं है।

  • Example:
    • She started singing.
  • इस प्रकार, 'to take' के स्थान पर gerund 'taking' का उपयोग किया जाएगा।
  • इसलिए, ऊपर वर्णित बिंदुओं के अनुसार, हम पाते हैं कि सही उत्तर विकल्प 2 है।

textEn:
Select the most appropriate meaning of the underlined idiom in the given sentence.

You must seize the opportunity to make amends for your earlier curtness.
textHi:
Select the most appropriate meaning of the underlined idiom in the given sentence.

You must seize the opportunity to make amends for your earlier curtness.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'to correct a mistake'.

Key Points

  • The most appropriate meaning of the given idiom 'To make amends' is 'to correct a mistake'.
  • To make amends: to do something to correct a mistake that one has made or a bad situation that one has caused. (किसी गलती को सुधारने के लिए कुछ करने के लिए जो किसी ने की है या एक बुरी स्थिति जो किसी के कारण हुई है)
    • Example: She tried to make amends by apologizing to him.
  • Therefore, as per the points mentioned above, we find that the correct answer is Option 1.
    ​

Correct Answer: To correct a mistake.

Additional Information

  • Let's look at some idioms the word 'Get' are:
Idiom Meaning Example
Get socked with something to suddenly receive something that causes you problems. (अचानक कुछ ऐसा प्राप्त करना जिससे आपको परेशानी हो) If you don't pay your credit card bill on time, you'll get socked with a huge late fee.
Get someone's wires crossed to have a different understanding of the same situation. (एक ही स्थिति की एक अलग समझ रखने के लिए) We must have got our wires crossed – I thought she was arriving tomorrow, not today.
Get something out of your system

to do something enough so that you do not want to do it any longer. (इतना कुछ करने के लिए कि आप इसे अब और नहीं करना चाहते हैं)

 Let the kids run around a little longer, maybe they'll get it out of their system.
explanationHi:
The correct answer is 'to correct a mistake'.

Key Points

  • The most appropriate meaning of the given idiom 'To make amends' is 'to correct a mistake'.
  • To make amends: to do something to correct a mistake that one has made or a bad situation that one has caused. (किसी गलती को सुधारने के लिए कुछ करने के लिए जो किसी ने की है या एक बुरी स्थिति जो किसी के कारण हुई है)
    • Example: She tried to make amends by apologizing to him.
  • Therefore, as per the points mentioned above, we find that the correct answer is Option 1.
    ​

Correct Answer: To correct a mistake.

to do something enough so that you do not want to do it any longer. (इतना कुछ करने के लिए कि आप इसे अब और नहीं करना चाहते हैं)

textEn:
Select the correct direct form of the given sentence.

She exclaimed that I had grown very tall.
textHi:
Select the correct direct form of the given sentence.

She exclaimed that I had grown very tall.
optionsEn: She said to me, "How tall are you?"
She said to me, "You have grown very tall."
She said to me, "How tall I have grown!"
She said to me, "How tall you have grown!"
explanationEn:
The correct answer is She said to me, "How tall you have grown!"

Key Points

  • The given sentence is in Indirect Speech. As per the question we have to change it into Direct Speech.
  • The process of transformation is as follows:
    • The given sentence is an example of an Exclamatory Sentence.
    • The notion of these kinds of sentences is 'joy, sorrow, surprise, applause, detest, hate, etc.'
    • Reporting Verbs are changed according to the notion of Reported Speech. These verbs are: 'exclaimed with surprise, exclaimed with sorrow, exclaimed with joy, exclaimed with contempt, etc.'
    • Exclamatory words 'Ah!, Ha!, My God!, Oh!, Alas!, Bravo!' are removed in Indirect Speech.
    • 'That' is used in place of 'Inverted commas'.
    • 'Tense, Pronoun, and Adverbials' are changed according to assertive sentences.
    • Example:
      • Nitin said, "What a match it was!". (Direct Speech)
      • Nitin exclaimed that it was a good match. (Indirect Speech)
    • 'exclaimed' will be changed into 'said to'.
    • Comma and inverted commas will be added.
    • 'I' will be changed into 'you'.
    • 'had grown' will be changed into 'have grown'. (past perfect tense changes into present perfect tense)
  • Therefore, as per the points mentioned above, we find that the correct answer is Option 4.

Correct Sentence: She said to me, "How tall you have grown!"

Mistake Points

  • We tend to go for Option 2 but it would be wrong because the given sentence is an example of an exclamatory sentence. And Option 2 is an example of an assertive sentence. (हम विकल्प 2 के लिए जाते हैं लेकिन यह गलत होगा क्योंकि दिया गया वाक्य exclamatory sentence का एक उदाहरण है। और विकल्प 2 एक assertive sentence का एक उदाहरण है।)
  • Hence, Option 4 is the correct answer.

Hinglish

  • दिया गया वाक्य Indirect Speech में है। प्रश्न के अनुसार हमें इसे Direct Speech में बदलना होगा।
  • परिवर्तन की प्रक्रिया इस प्रकार है:
    • दिया गया वाक्य Exclamatory Sentence का उदाहरण है।
    • इस प्रकार के वाक्यों की धारणा 'joy, sorrow, surprise, applause, detest, hate, etc.' है।
    • Reported Speech की धारणा के अनुसार Reporting Verbs को बदल दिया जाता है। ये क्रियाएँ हैं: 'exclaimed with surprise, exclaimed with sorrow, exclaimed with joy, exclaimed with contempt, etc.'
    • विस्मयादिबोधक शब्द ''Ah!, Ha!, My God!, Oh!, Alas!, Bravo!' ' indirect speech में हटा दिया जाता है।
    • 'That' का प्रयोग 'Inverted commas' के स्थान पर किया जाता है।
    • 'Tense, Pronoun, and Adverbials' assertive sentences के अनुसार बदले जाते हैं।
      • उदाहरण:
      • Nitin said, "What a match it was!". (Direct Speech)
      • Nitin exclaimed that it was a good match. (Indirect Speech)
    • 'exclaimed' को 'said to' में बदल दिया जाएगा।
    • अल्पविराम और उल्टे अल्पविराम जोड़े जाएंगे।
    • 'I' को 'you' में बदल दिया जाएगा। Direct रूप से Indirect परिवर्तन में सर्वनाम के दूसरे रूप को रिपोर्टिंग क्रिया की object के अनुसार बदल दिया जाता है।
    • 'had grown' को 'have grown' में बदल दिया जाएगा।
  • इसलिए, ऊपर वर्णित बिंदुओं के अनुसार, हम पाते हैं कि सही उत्तर विकल्प 4 है।

textEn:
Given below are six sentences A-F, of which A and F are static and B-E have been jumbled. Pick the option that gives the correct order of the jumbled sentences.

A. Santosh's parents were affluent landowners who could afford to send their children to the best schools.

B. So, she decided to fight the matter in her own quiet way when the right moment arrived.

C. At sixteen, most of the girls in her village used to get married.

D. But in line with the custom of the family, Santosh was sent to the local village school.

E. And, the right moment came when she turned sixteen.

F. But a marriage at that age was the last thing on Santosh's mind.
textHi:
Given below are six sentences A-F, of which A and F are static and B-E have been jumbled. Pick the option that gives the correct order of the jumbled sentences.

A. Santosh's parents were affluent landowners who could afford to send their children to the best schools.

B. So, she decided to fight the matter in her own quiet way when the right moment arrived.

C. At sixteen, most of the girls in her village used to get married.

D. But in line with the custom of the family, Santosh was sent to the local village school.

E. And, the right moment came when she turned sixteen.

F. But a marriage at that age was the last thing on Santosh's mind.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'ADBECF'

Key Points

  • The given question is an example of a Para Jumble.
  • We know that the first statement of a para jumbled question is usually an independent general statement, a noun, a universal fact, starting off a story, starting of an incident or an event, or it starts with 'most' or 'once'.
  • In the given question, sentence A is the first sentence and sentence F is the last one.
  • We have to rearrange the rest of the sentences accordingly.
  • Sentence A is 'Santosh's parents were affluent landowners who could afford to send their children to the best schools.' This sentence tells us that Santosh's parents were rich landowners so they could send their children to the best schools.
  • Sentence D will be the next sentence because it is connected with Sentence A. As per 'Sentence A' 'Santosh' belonged to a rich family, still, she went to the local village school. Hence, both are connected.
  • Sentence B will be the next sentence because it tells what step Santosh took to fight the custom of her family, i.e. to study in the local village school.
  • Sentence E will be the next sentence because the right moment for which Santosh had been waiting came at the age of sixteen.
  • Sentence C will be the next sentence because it tells us that most of the village girls used to get married at the age of sixteen. She didn't want to get married at that age and therefore, this was the moment she was waiting for.
  • Thus, the sequence becomes:
    • A: Santosh's parents were affluent landowners who could afford to send their children to the best schools.
    • D: But in line with the custom of the family, Santosh was sent to the local village school.
    • B: So, she decided to fight the matter in her own quiet way when the right moment arrived.
    • E: And, the right moment came when she turned sixteen.
    • C: At sixteen, most of the girls in her village used to get married.
    • F: But a marriage at that age was the last thing on Santosh's mind.
  • Therefore, as per the points mentioned above, we find that the correct answer is Option 2.

Correct Sequence: Santosh's parents were affluent landowners who could afford to send their children to the best schools. But in line with the custom of the family, Santosh was sent to the local village school. So, she decided to fight the matter in her own quiet way when the right moment arrived. And, the right moment came when she turned sixteen. At sixteen, most of the girls in her village used to get married. But a marriage at that age was the last thing on Santosh's mind.
explanationHi:
The correct answer is 'ADBECF'

Key Points

  • The given question is an example of a Para Jumble.
  • We know that the first statement of a para jumbled question is usually an independent general statement, a noun, a universal fact, starting off a story, starting of an incident or an event, or it starts with 'most' or 'once'.
  • In the given question, sentence A is the first sentence and sentence F is the last one.
  • We have to rearrange the rest of the sentences accordingly.
  • Sentence A is 'Santosh's parents were affluent landowners who could afford to send their children to the best schools.' This sentence tells us that Santosh's parents were rich landowners so they could send their children to the best schools.
  • Sentence D will be the next sentence because it is connected with Sentence A. As per 'Sentence A' 'Santosh' belonged to a rich family, still, she went to the local village school. Hence, both are connected.
  • Sentence B will be the next sentence because it tells what step Santosh took to fight the custom of her family, i.e. to study in the local village school.
  • Sentence E will be the next sentence because the right moment for which Santosh had been waiting came at the age of sixteen.
  • Sentence C will be the next sentence because it tells us that most of the village girls used to get married at the age of sixteen. She didn't want to get married at that age and therefore, this was the moment she was waiting for.
  • Thus, the sequence becomes:
    • A: Santosh's parents were affluent landowners who could afford to send their children to the best schools.
    • D: But in line with the custom of the family, Santosh was sent to the local village school.
    • B: So, she decided to fight the matter in her own quiet way when the right moment arrived.
    • E: And, the right moment came when she turned sixteen.
    • C: At sixteen, most of the girls in her village used to get married.
    • F: But a marriage at that age was the last thing on Santosh's mind.
  • Therefore, as per the points mentioned above, we find that the correct answer is Option 2.

Correct Sequence: Santosh's parents were affluent landowners who could afford to send their children to the best schools. But in line with the custom of the family, Santosh was sent to the local village school. So, she decided to fight the matter in her own quiet way when the right moment arrived. And, the right moment came when she turned sixteen. At sixteen, most of the girls in her village used to get married. But a marriage at that age was the last thing on Santosh's mind.

textEn:
Select the most appropriate one-word substitution for the given group of words.

Introductory section of a literary work
textHi:
Select the most appropriate one-word substitution for the given group of words.

Introductory section of a literary work
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'Prologue'.

Key Points

  • The most appropriate word for the given group of words is 'Prologue'.
  • Prologue: a piece of writing or a speech that introduces a play, poem, etc. (नाटक, कविता आदि को प्रस्‍तुत करने वाला लेख या भाषण; प्रस्‍तावना)
    • Example: The prologue to the novel is written in the form of a newspaper account.
  • Therefore, as per the points mentioned above, we find that the correct answer is Option 4.

Correct Answer: Prologue

Additional Information

  • Let's look at the meaning of other words given in the options:
Words Meaning
Dialogue conversation between people in a book, play, etc.
(नाटक आदि में संवाद, लोगों के बीच वार्तालाप)
Epilogue a short piece that is added at the end of a book, play, etc., and that comments on what has gone before.
(किसी पुस्‍तक, नाटक आदि का अंतवचन, उपसंहार, भरत वाक्‍य)
Monologue

a long speech by one person, for example in a play.
(अकेले व्यक्ति का लंबा भाषण (जैसे नाटक में), एकालाप; स्‍वगत भाषण)

textEn:
Select the most appropriate option to substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. If there is no need to substitute it, select 'No substitution'.

There is no exception with these rule.
textHi:
Select the most appropriate option to substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. If there is no need to substitute it, select 'No substitution'.

There is no exception with these rule.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'to this rule'.

Key Points

  • The given sentence is grammatically incorrect.
  • Here, 'to this rule' should be used instead of 'with these rule'.
  • The word 'exception' is followed by the preposition 'to'.
  • It means 'To strongly disagree with or take offense at something'. (किसी बात पर दृढ़ता से असहमत होना या अपराध करना).'
    • Example: I know she takes exception to the way the newspaper represented the data from her study.
  • Here, 'these' should be changed into 'this' because 'these' refers to plural nouns. (here, the noun is singular i.e. rule)
  • Therefore, as per the points mentioned above, we find that the correct answer is Option 2.

Correct Sentence: There is no exception to this rule.
explanationHi:
The correct answer is 'to this rule'.

Key Points

  • The given sentence is grammatically incorrect.
  • Here, 'to this rule' should be used instead of 'with these rule'.
  • The word 'exception' is followed by the preposition 'to'.
  • It means 'To strongly disagree with or take offense at something'. (किसी बात पर दृढ़ता से असहमत होना या अपराध करना).'
    • Example: I know she takes exception to the way the newspaper represented the data from her study.
  • Here, 'these' should be changed into 'this' because 'these' refers to plural nouns. (here, the noun is singular i.e. rule)
  • Therefore, as per the points mentioned above, we find that the correct answer is Option 2.

Correct Sentence: There is no exception to this rule.

textEn:
Identify the segment in the sentence which contains a grammatical error.

The farmers are using / HYV seeds / so then they can get / a better yield.
textHi:
Identify the segment in the sentence which contains a grammatical error.

The farmers are using / HYV seeds / so then they can get / a better yield.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'so then they can get'.

Key Points

  • The given sentence is grammatically incorrect.
  • Here, 'so that they can get' should be used instead of 'so then they can get'.
  • 'So' is followed by 'that' when it answers the question 'why?'
  • In the given context, 'so that' means 'in order to'.
  • We use it to begin adverb clauses of purpose.
    • Example: It helps to lower blood sugar so that you feel less hungry.
  • The adverb clause is "so that you feel less hungry."
  • It shows the purpose of the action in the main clause.
  • Therefore, as per the points mentioned above, we find that the correct answer is Option 2.

Correct Sentence: The farmers are using HYV seeds so that they can get a better yield.

Hinglish

  • दिया गया वाक्य व्याकरण की दृष्टि से गलत है।
  • यहाँ, 'so then they can get' के स्थान पर 'so that they can get' का प्रयोग किया जाना चाहिए।
  • 'So' के बाद 'that' आता है जब यह 'why?' question का उत्तर देता है।
  • दिए गए संदर्भ में 'so that' का अर्थ है 'in order to'.
  • हम इसका उपयोग adverb clauses of purpose को शुरू करने के लिए करते हैं।
    • Example: It helps to lower blood sugar so that you feel less hungry.
  • Adverb clauses of purpose है "so that you feel less hungry".
  • यह मुख्य खंड में action के purpose को दर्शाता है।
  • इसलिए, ऊपर वर्णित बिंदुओं के अनुसार, हम पाते हैं कि सही उत्तर विकल्प 2 है।

textEn:
Select the correct direct form of the given sentence.

The little girl said that those butterflies were very colourful.
textHi:
Select the correct direct form of the given sentence.

The little girl said that those butterflies were very colourful.
optionsEn: The little girl said, "Those butterflies were very colourful."
The little girl says, "How colourful were these butterflies!'
The little girl said, "Are those butterflies very colourful?"
The little girl said, "These butterflies are very colourful."
explanationEn:
The correct answer is The little girl said, "These butterflies are very colourful."

Key Points

  • The given sentence is in Indirect Speech. As per the question, we have to change it into Direct Speech.
  • The process of transformation is as follows:
    • In the given sentence 'said' will not change because the reporting verb does not have an object.
    • Comma and inverted commas will be added.
    • The adverb 'those' will be changed into 'these'.
    • The auxiliary verb 'were' will be changed into 'are'.
  • Therefore, as per the points mentioned above, we find that the correct answer is Option 4.

Correct Sentence: The litter girl said, "These butterflies are very colourful."

Hinglish

  • दिया गया वाक्य Indirect Speech में है। प्रश्न के अनुसार हमें इसे Direct Speech में बदलना होगा।
  • परिवर्तन की प्रक्रिया इस प्रकार है:
    • दिए गए वाक्य में 'said' नहीं बदलेगा क्योंकि reporting verb में कोई object नहीं है।
    • अल्पविराम और उल्टे अल्पविराम जोड़े जाएंगे।
    • Adverb 'those' को 'these' में बदल दिया जाएगा।
    • सहायक क्रिया 'were' को 'are' में बदल दिया जाएगा।
  • इसलिए, ऊपर वर्णित बिंदुओं के अनुसार, हम पाते हैं कि सही उत्तर विकल्प 4 है।

textEn:
Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word.

CONDENSE
textHi:
Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word.

CONDENSE
explanationEn:
The correct antonym is 'expand'.Key Points

  • The word 'Condense' means 'to become smaller in size or volume through the drawing together of particles of matter.' (पदार्थ के कणों के एक साथ आरेखण के माध्यम से आकार या आयतन में छोटा होना)
  • The antonyms of the word 'Condense' are 'accumulate, grow, increase, inflate, expand'.
  • From the antonyms of the given word, we can say that the word 'Expand' is the opposite in meaning.
  • The word 'Expand' means 'to make greater in size, amount, or number.' (आकार, राशि या संख्या में बड़ा करना)

Hence, the correct answer is option 1. Let's see the meaning of other given options:-

Words Meaning
Reduce to make something less or smaller in quantity, price, size, etc.
(मात्रा, क़ीमत, आकार आदि की दृष्टि से घटाना; छोटा करना)
Constrict to become or make something tighter, narrower, or less.
(संकुचित होना या करना; सिकुड़ना, कसना)
Repel to send or push somebody/something back or away
(किसी व्‍यकि्त या वस्‍तु को पीछे या दूर भेज या धकेल देना)

Additional Information

  • The synonyms of the word 'Condense' are 'compress, constrict, contract, shrink.'
  • Example of 'Condense' in a sentence:
    • Over time the once-fluffy material in the pillow had condensed into a lumpy wad.

textEn:
Select the correct active form of the given sentence.

Let the medicine be administered every four hours.
textHi:
Select the correct active form of the given sentence.

Let the medicine be administered every four hours.
optionsEn: Let you administer the medicine every four hours.
You have administered the medicine every four hours.
You are administering the medicine every four hours.
Administer the medicine every four hours.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'Administer the medicine every four hours.'

Key Points

  • The given sentence is in Passive Voice. As per the given question we have to change it into Active Voice.
  • The structure of the given sentence is as follows:
    • The given sentence is an example of imperative sentences.
    • The transformation of imperative sentences:
    • If someone gives a command, does a request, expresses a desire or wish in sentences then such sentences are called an imperative sentences.
      • V1 + Object. (Active Voice)
      • Let + subject + be + V3. (Passive Voice)
    • Example:
      • Teach your students. (Active Voice)
      • Let your students be taught. (Passive Voice)
    • 'The medicine' is the subject.
    • 'be administered' will be changed into 'administer'.
  • Therefore, as per the points mentioned above, we find that the correct answer is Option 4.

Correct Sentence: Administer the medicine every four hours.

Hinglish

  • दिया गया वाक्य Passive Voice में है। दिए गए प्रश्न के अनुसार हमें इसे Active Voice में बदलना है।
  • दिए गए वाक्य की संरचना इस प्रकार है:
    • दिया गया वाक्य Imperative Sentence का एक उदाहरण है।
    • Imperative Sentence का परिवर्तन:
    • यदि कोई वाक्यों में आदेश देता है, एक अनुरोध करता है, एक इच्छा व्यक्त करता है, ऐसे वाक्यों को अनिवार्य वाक्य कहा जाता है।
      • V1 + Object. (Active Voice)
      • Let + subject + be + V3. (Passive Voice)
    • Example:
      • Teach your students. (Active Voice)
      • Let your students be taught. (Passive Voice)
    • 'The medicine' subject है।
    • 'be administered' को 'administer' में बदल दिया जाएगा।
  • इसलिए, ऊपर वर्णित बिंदुओं के अनुसार, हम पाते हैं कि सही उत्तर विकल्प 4 है।

textEn:
Select the most appropriate option to substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. If there is no need to substitute it, select 'No substitution'.

He lost his heart because he could not cope with the difficult situation.
textHi:
Select the most appropriate option to substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. If there is no need to substitute it, select 'No substitution'.

He lost his heart because he could not cope with the difficult situation.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'lost heart'.

Key Points

  • The given sentence is grammatically incorrect.
  • Here, 'lost heart' should be used instead of 'lost his heart'.
  • 'Lose heart' is a phrasal verb that means 'become discouraged'.
  • As the meaning of the sentence implies a past incident, the past form of 'Lose heart' will be used as 'Lost heart'.
  • It means 'to lose all hope or confidence'. (सारा आत्म विश्वास और आशा खो देना)
    • Example: He told his friend not to lose heart because they might still win the game.
  • Therefore, as per the points mentioned above, we find that the correct answer is Option 4.

Correct Sentence: He lost heart because he could not cope with the difficult situation.

textEn:
Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank.

I am your true friend, ______?
textHi:
Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank.

I am your true friend, ______?
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'aren't I'.

Key Points

  • Here, in the given fill-in-the-blank, the most appropriate option is 'aren't I'.
  • We know that a positive statement is followed by a negative question tag whereas, a negative statement is followed by a positive question tag.
    • Example: Jack is from Spain, isn't he?​
  • The given sentence is positive. Hence, it should be followed by a negative question tag.
  • The negative question tag of 'I am' is 'aren't I'.
  • Therefore, as per the points mentioned above, we find that the correct answer is Option 3.

Correct Sentence: I am your true friend, aren't I?


Hinglish

  • यहां, दिए गए रिक्त स्थान की पूर्ति में, सबसे उपयुक्त विकल्प 'aren't I' है।
  • हम जानते हैं कि एक सकारात्मक कथन के बाद एक नकारात्मक question tag होता है, जबकि एक नकारात्मक कथन के बाद एक सकारात्मक question tag होता है।
    • ​Example: Jack is from Spain, isn't he?​
  • दिया गया वाक्य सकारात्मक है। इसलिए, इसके बाद एक नकारात्मक question tag होना चाहिए।
  • 'I am' का नकारात्मक प्रश्न टैग है 'aren't I'.
  • इसलिए, ऊपर वर्णित बिंदुओं के अनुसार, हम पाते हैं कि सही उत्तर विकल्प 3 है।

textEn:
Identify the segment in the sentence which contains a grammatical error.

My neighbour is / an young businessman / who is very friendly / with everyone.
textHi:
Identify the segment in the sentence which contains a grammatical error.

My neighbour is / an young businessman / who is very friendly / with everyone.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'an young businessman'.

Key Points

  • The given sentence is grammatically incorrect.
  • Here, 'young businessman' should be used instead of 'an young businessman'.
  • Here in the given sentence, the indefinite article 'a' should be used because 'young' starts with a consonant sound.
    • Example: Uranium is a toxic metal.
  • Therefore, as per the points mentioned above, we find that the correct answer is Option 1.

Correct Sentence: My neighbour is a young businessman who is very friendly with everyone.

Additional Information

  • No article is used before the possessive case of the proper noun.
    • Example:
      • The Sukhdev's bike is blue with an orange seat.
      • Sukhdev's bike is blue with an orange seat.
  • ​​​​But when there is a possessive case of a common noun, then, the article can be used before it.
    • Example:
      • The Woman's Yoga Book.
      • Woman's Yoga Book.

Hinglish

  • दिया गया वाक्य व्याकरण की दृष्टि से गलत है।
  • यहां 'an young businessman' के स्थान पर 'young businessman' का प्रयोग किया जाना चाहिए।
  • यहाँ दिए गए वाक्य में, अनिश्चितकालीन article 'a' का प्रयोग करना चाहिए क्योंकि 'young' का प्रारम्भ consonant sound से होता है।
    • Example: Uranium is a toxic metal.
  • इसलिए, ऊपर वर्णित बिंदुओं के अनुसार, हम पाते हैं कि सही उत्तर विकल्प 1 है।

Key Points

textEn:
Select the correct passive form of the given sentence.

How can I forget your generosity?
textHi:
Select the correct passive form of the given sentence.

How can I forget your generosity?
optionsEn: How your generosity can be forgot by me?
How has your generosity been forgotten by me?
How can your generosity be forgot by me?
How can your generosity be forgotten by me?
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'How can your generosity be forgotten by me?'

Key Points

  • The given sentence is an example of Active Voice. As per the given question we have to change it into Passive Voice.
  • The process of transformation as follows:
    • In 'active-passive' transformation except for (who and whom) rest of the Wh-family Pronouns will remain unchanged.
    • Here, in the given question 'How many languages' will remain unchanged.
    • Since modal verb has been used in the active voice, therefore, the passive voice will follow the given structure:-
      • Active Voice - Modal verb(can) + V1
      • Passive Voice - Modal verb(can) + be + V3
    • 'can forget' will be changed into 'can be forgotten'.
    • 'I' will be changed into 'me'.
    • Lastly, the preposition 'by' will be added.
  • Therefore, as per the points mentioned above, we find that the correct answer is Option 4.

Correct Sentence: How can your generosity be forgotten by me?

Hinglish

  • दिया गया वाक्य Active Voice का उदाहरण है। दिए गए प्रश्न के अनुसार हमें इसे Passive Voice में बदलना है।
  • परिवर्तन की प्रक्रिया इस प्रकार है:
    • 'Active-Passive' परिवर्तन में (who और whom) को छोड़कर शेष Wh- परिवार pronouns अपरिवर्तित रहेंगे।
    • यहां, दिए गए प्रश्न में 'How many languages' अपरिवर्तित रहेंगी।
    • चूंकि, Active voice में modal verb का उपयोग किया गया है, इसलिए, passive voice दी गई संरचना का अनुसरण करेगी: -
      • Active Voice - Modal verb(can) + V1
      • Passive Voice - Modal verb(can) + be + V3
    • 'can forget' को 'can be forgotten' में बदल दिया जाएगा।
    • 'I' को 'me' में बदल दिया जाएगा।
    • अंत में, preposition 'by' जोड़ा जाएगा।
  • इसलिए, ऊपर वर्णित बिंदुओं के अनुसार, हम पाते हैं कि सही उत्तर विकल्प 4 है।
explanationHi:
The correct answer is 'How can your generosity be forgotten by me?'

Key Points

    • In 'active-passive' transformation except for (who and whom) rest of the Wh-family Pronouns will remain unchanged.
    • Here, in the given question 'How many languages' will remain unchanged.
    • Since modal verb has been used in the active voice, therefore, the passive voice will follow the given structure:-

      • Passive Voice - Modal verb(can) + be + V3
    • 'can forget' will be changed into 'can be forgotten'.

  • दिया गया वाक्य Active Voice का उदाहरण है। दिए गए प्रश्न के अनुसार हमें इसे Passive Voice में बदलना है।
  • परिवर्तन की प्रक्रिया इस प्रकार है:
    • 'Active-Passive' परिवर्तन में (who और whom) को छोड़कर शेष Wh- परिवार pronouns अपरिवर्तित रहेंगे।
    • यहां, दिए गए प्रश्न में 'How many languages' अपरिवर्तित रहेंगी।
    • चूंकि, Active voice में modal verb का उपयोग किया गया है, इसलिए, passive voice दी गई संरचना का अनुसरण करेगी: -
      • Active Voice - Modal verb(can) + V1
      • Passive Voice - Modal verb(can) + be + V3
    • 'can forget' को 'can be forgotten' में बदल दिया जाएगा।
    • 'I' को 'me' में बदल दिया जाएगा।
    • अंत में, preposition 'by' जोड़ा जाएगा।
  • इसलिए, ऊपर वर्णित बिंदुओं के अनुसार, हम पाते हैं कि सही उत्तर विकल्प 4 है।

textEn:
Select the correct indirect form of the given sentence.

You said, "I scored four goals."
textHi:
Select the correct indirect form of the given sentence.

You said, "I scored four goals."
optionsEn: You said that I had scored four goals.
You said that you have scored four goals.
You said that you scored four goals.
You said that you had scored four goals.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'You said that you had scored four goals.'

Key Points

  • The given sentence is in Direct Speech. As per the given question we have to change it into Indirect Speech.
  • The process of transformation is as follows:
    • In the given sentence the reporting verb has no object. Hence, 'said' will not be changed.
    • Comma and inverted commas will be removed.
    • The conjunction 'that' will be added.
    • 'I' will be changed into 'you' because the first person pronouns are changed according to the subject of the reporting verb.
    • V2 will be changed into 'had + V3'. (simple past tense changes into past perfect tense)
  • Therefore, as per the points mentioned above, we find that the correct answer is Option 4.

Correct Sentence: You said that you had scored four goals.

Hinglish

  • दिया गया वाक्य Direct Speech में है। दिए गए प्रश्न के अनुसार हमें इसे Indirect Speech में बदलना है।

    • अल्पविराम और उल्टे अल्पविराम हटा दिए जाएंगे।
    • संयोजन 'that' जोड़ा जाएगा।
    • 'I' को 'you' में बदल दिया जाएगा क्योंकि रिपोर्टिंग क्रिया के विषय के अनुसार पहले व्यक्ति सर्वनाम बदल दिए जाते हैं।

textEn:
Select the correct indirect form of the given sentence.

She said to Madhu, "Please post this letter."
textHi:
Select the correct indirect form of the given sentence.

She said to Madhu, "Please post this letter."
optionsEn: She said Madhu to post that letter.
She ordered Madhu that post this letter.
She told Madhu to post this letter.
She requested Madhu to post that letter.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'She requested Madhu to post that letter'.

Key Points

  • The given sentence is in Direct Speech. As per the question we have to change it into Indirect Speech.
  • The process of transformation is as follows:
    • The given sentence is an example of an imperative sentence.
    • Let's look at the rules of transformation:
      • While changing the narration of an imperative sentence, we need to follow the given steps:
      • The notion of the given sentence is 'request'.
      • The conjunction 'to' should be used in place of a comma (,) and inverted commas (" ").
      • 'said to' will be changed into 'requested'.
      • 'to' is followed by 'V1'.
      • Lastly, the adverb 'this' will be changed into 'that'.
    • Example:
      • The queen said to his servant, "Cook me some delicious food today." (Direct Speech)
      • The queen ordered his servant to cook her some delicious food that day. (Indirect Speech)
  • Therefore, as per the points mentioned above, we find that the correct answer is Option 4.
    ​

Correct Sentence: She requested Madhu to post that letter.

Hinglish

  • दिया गया वाक्य Direct Speech में है। प्रश्न के अनुसार हमें इसे Indirect Speech में बदलना होगा।
  • परिवर्तन की प्रक्रिया इस प्रकार है:
    • दिया गया वाक्य Imperative Sentence का उदाहरण है।
    • आइए परिवर्तन के नियमों को देखें:
      • एक Imperative Sentence का Narration बदलते समय, हमें दिए गए चरणों का पालन करना होगा:
      • दिए गए वाक्य की धारणा 'request' है।
      • संयोजन 'to' का प्रयोग अल्पविराम (,) और उल्टे अल्पविराम (" ") के स्थान पर किया जाना चाहिए।
      • 'said to' को 'requested' में बदल दिया जाएगा।
      • 'to' के बाद 'V1' आता है।
      • अंत में, Adverb 'this' को 'that' में बदल दिया जाएगा।
    • Example:
      • The queen said to his servant, "Cook me some delicious food today." (Direct Speech)
      • The queen ordered his servant to cook her some delicious food that day. (Indirect Speech)
  • इसलिए, ऊपर वर्णित बिंदुओं के अनुसार, हम पाते हैं कि सही उत्तर विकल्प 4 है।

textEn:
Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word.

LANGUID
textHi:
Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word.

LANGUID
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'energetic'.

Key Points

  • The word 'Languid' means 'weak or faint from illness or fatigue; (of a person, manner, or gesture) having or showing a disinclination for physical exertion or effort.' (बीमारी या थकान से कमजोर या बेहोश; (किसी व्यक्ति, तरीके, या हावभाव का) शारीरिक परिश्रम या प्रयास के प्रति उदासीनता होना या दिखाना)
  • The antonyms of the word 'Languid' are 'ambitious, animated, energetic, enterprising, motivated'.
  • From the antonyms of the given word, we can say that the word 'Energetic' is the opposite in meaning.
  • The word 'Energetic' means 'showing or involving great activity or vitality.' (ऊर्जावान)

Hence, the correct answer is option 2. Let's see the meaning of other given options:-

Words Meaning
Lethargic affected by lethargy; sluggish and apathetic. (सुस्ती से प्रभावित; सुस्त और उदासीन)
Sluggish slow-moving or inactive. (धीमी गति से चलने वाला या निष्क्रिय)
Indolent wanting to avoid activity or exertion; lazy. (गतिविधि या परिश्रम से बचना चाहते हैं; काम चोर)

Additional Information

  • The synonyms of the word 'Languid' are 'enervated, lackadaisical, languishing, languorous, limp, listless, spiritless'.
  • Example of 'Languid' in a sentence:
    • A slow, languid smile crossed his features, one that made her body flush and ache for him.

textEn:
Given below are four sentences in jumbled order. Pick the option that gives their correct order.

A. She looked very excited when she saw me get off the bus.

B. It was quite a long time since I had met my grandmother.

C. I found her standing at the village bus stand to receive me.

D. She hugged me and tears of joy flowed down her cheeks.
textHi:
Given below are four sentences in jumbled order. Pick the option that gives their correct order.

A. She looked very excited when she saw me get off the bus.

B. It was quite a long time since I had met my grandmother.

C. I found her standing at the village bus stand to receive me.

D. She hugged me and tears of joy flowed down her cheeks.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'BCAD'.

Key Points

  • The given sentence is an example of a Para Jumble.
  • We know that the first statement of a para jumbled question is usually an independent general statement, a noun, a universal fact, starting of an incident, or it starts with 'most' or 'once'.
  • Sentence B in the given question is the starting of an incident. It talks about the author meeting his grandmother after a long while.
  • Sentence C will be the next sentence because when the author went to meet his grandmother, he saw her standing at the village bus stand to receive him.
  • Sentence A will be the next sentence because it explains the expressions on the face of the author's grandmother when he saw her standing at the village bus stand to receive him.
  • Lastly, Sentence D will be used because it expresses what her grandmother did when she received him.
  • Thus, the sequence becomes:
    • B: It was quite a long time since I had met my grandmother.
    • C: I found her standing at the village bus stand to receive me.
    • A: She looked very excited when she saw me get off the bus.
    • D: She hugged me and tears of joy flowed down her cheeks.
  • Therefore, as per the points mentioned above, we find that the correct answer is Option 3.

Correct Sequence: It was quite a long time since I had met my grandmother. I found her standing at the village bus stand to receive me. She looked very excited when she saw me get off the bus. She hugged me and tears of joy flowed down her cheeks.
explanationHi:
The correct answer is 'BCAD'.

  • The given sentence is an example of a Para Jumble.
  • We know that the first statement of a para jumbled question is usually an independent general statement, a noun, a universal fact, starting of an incident, or it starts with 'most' or 'once'.
  • Sentence B in the given question is the starting of an incident. It talks about the author meeting his grandmother after a long while.
  • Sentence C will be the next sentence because when the author went to meet his grandmother, he saw her standing at the village bus stand to receive him.
  • Sentence A will be the next sentence because it explains the expressions on the face of the author's grandmother when he saw her standing at the village bus stand to receive him.
  • Lastly, Sentence D will be used because it expresses what her grandmother did when she received him.
  • Thus, the sequence becomes:
    • B: It was quite a long time since I had met my grandmother.
    • C: I found her standing at the village bus stand to receive me.
    • A: She looked very excited when she saw me get off the bus.
    • D: She hugged me and tears of joy flowed down her cheeks.
  • Therefore, as per the points mentioned above, we find that the correct answer is Option 3.

Correct Sequence: It was quite a long time since I had met my grandmother. I found her standing at the village bus stand to receive me. She looked very excited when she saw me get off the bus. She hugged me and tears of joy flowed down her cheeks.

textEn:
Select the correct direct form of the given sentence.

He told you that he would discuss the matter that evening.
textHi:
Select the correct direct form of the given sentence.

He told you that he would discuss the matter that evening.
optionsEn: You said to him, "I will discuss the matter this evening."
You said to him, "He would discuss the matter today evening."
He said to you, "I will discuss the matter today evening."
He told to you, "He will discuss the matter that evening."
explanationEn:
The correct answer is He said to you, "I will discuss the matter today evening."

Key Points

  • The given sentence is in Indirect Speech. As per the question we have to change it into Direct Speech.
  • The process of transformation is as follows:
    • Here, in the given question 'told' will be changed into 'said to' because the reporting speech has an object.
    • The conjunction 'that' will be removed.
    • Comma and inverted commas will be added.
    • The pronoun 'he' will be changed into 'I' because we know that the first person pronouns are changed according to the subject of the reporting verb.
    • 'would' will be changed into 'will'. (Indirect to Direct Transformation)
    • The adverb 'that evening' will be changed into 'today evening'.
  • Therefore, as per the points mentioned above, we find that the correct answer is Option 3.

Correct Sentence: He said to you, "I will discuss the matter today evening."

Hinglish

  • दिया गया वाक्य Indirect Speech में है। प्रश्न के अनुसार हमें इसे Direct Speech में बदलना होगा।
  • परिवर्तन की प्रक्रिया इस प्रकार है:
    • यहां, दिए गए प्रश्न में 'told' को 'said to' में बदल दिया जाएगा क्योंकि reporting speech में एक object है।
    • संयोजन 'that' हटा दिया जाएगा।
    • अल्पविराम और उल्टे अल्पविराम जोड़े जाएंगे।
    • सर्वनाम 'he' को 'I' में बदल दिया जाएगा। क्योंकि हम जानते हैं कि reporting verb के विषय के अनुसार पहले व्यक्ति pronoun बदल जाते हैं।
    • 'would' को 'will' में बदल दिया जाएगा। (अप्रत्यक्ष से प्रत्यक्ष परिवर्तन)
    • Adverb 'that evening' को 'today evening' में बदल दिया जाएगा।
  • इसलिए, ऊपर वर्णित बिंदुओं के अनुसार, हम पाते हैं कि सही उत्तर विकल्प 3 है।

  • परिवर्तन की प्रक्रिया इस प्रकार है:
    • यहां, दिए गए प्रश्न में 'told' को 'said to' में बदल दिया जाएगा क्योंकि reporting speech में एक object है।
    • संयोजन 'that' हटा दिया जाएगा।
    • अल्पविराम और उल्टे अल्पविराम जोड़े जाएंगे।
    • सर्वनाम 'he' को 'I' में बदल दिया जाएगा। क्योंकि हम जानते हैं कि reporting verb के विषय के अनुसार पहले व्यक्ति pronoun बदल जाते हैं।
    • 'would' को 'will' में बदल दिया जाएगा। (अप्रत्यक्ष से प्रत्यक्ष परिवर्तन)
    • Adverb 'that evening' को 'today evening' में बदल दिया जाएगा।
  • इसलिए, ऊपर वर्णित बिंदुओं के अनुसार, हम पाते हैं कि सही उत्तर विकल्प 3 है।

textEn:
Select the most appropriate one-word substitution for the given group of words.

A fictitious name used by an author
textHi:
Select the most appropriate one-word substitution for the given group of words.

A fictitious name used by an author
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'Pseudonym'.

Key Points

  • The most appropriate meaning of the given group of words is 'pseudonym'.
  • Pseudonym: a name used by somebody, especially a writer, instead of his/her real name. (छद्मनाम, उपनाम; असली नाम के बदले प्रयुक्त, विशेषतः किसी लेखक द्वारा)
    • Example: He writes estimable poetry under a pseudonym.
  • Therefore, as per the points mentioned above, we find that the correct answer is Option 3.

Correct Answer: Pseudonym.
