textEn:
Select the correct direct form of the given sentence.

The Principal asked Kamal how it was that he was always late for school.
textHi:
Select the correct direct form of the given sentence.

The Principal asked Kamal how it was that he was always late for school.
optionsEn: The Principal said to Kamal, "How is it that you are always late for school?"
The Principal said to Kamal, "How it was that he was always late for school?"
The Principal said to Kamal, "How are you always late for school?"
The Principal said to Kamal, "How it is he was always late for school?"
explanationEn:
The correct answer is The Principal said to Kamal, "How is it that you are always late for school?"

Key Points

  • The given sentence is in Indirect Speech. As per the question we have to change it into Direct Speech.
  • The process of transformation is as follows:
    • 'Asked' will be changed into 'said to' because the given question is an example of an interrogative sentence with a question word.
    • Comma and inverted commas will be added.
    • 'was' will be changed into 'is'. Because in 'direct to indirect' transformation 'simple present' tense is changed into 'simple past tense'.
    • 'he' will be changed into 'you' because in 'direct to indirect' transformation second-person pronoun is changed according to the object of the reporting verb.
    • 'How' will be used as a conjunction because we know that in an interrogative sentence with a question word, the question word itself is used as a conjunction.
    • Therefore, as per the points mentioned above, we find that the correct answer is Option 1.

Correct Sentence: The Principal said to Kamal, "How is it that you are always late for school?"

Hinglish

  • दिया गया वाक्य Indirect Speech में है। प्रश्न के अनुसार हमें इसे Direct Speech में बदलना होगा।
  • परिवर्तन की प्रक्रिया इस प्रकार है:
    • 'asked' को 'said to' में बदल दिया जाएगा क्योंकि दिया गया प्रश्न interrogative sentence के साथ एक प्रश्नवाचक वाक्य का उदाहरण है।
    • अल्पविराम और उल्टे अल्पविराम जोड़े जाएंगे।
    • 'was' को 'is' में बदल दिया जाएगा। क्योंकि 'direct to indirect' transformation में 'simple present' tense को 'simple past tense' में बदल दिया जाता है।
    • 'he' को 'you' में बदल दिया जाएगा क्योंकि 'direct to indirect' transformation में reporting verb के object के अनुसार second person के pronouns को बदल दिया जाता है।
    • 'How' को एक conjunction के रूप में प्रयोग किया जाएगा क्योंकि हम जानते हैं कि एक interrogative sentence में प्रश्न शब्द का प्रयोग conjunction के रूप में किया जाता है।
    • इसलिए, ऊपर वर्णित बिंदुओं के अनुसार, हम पाते हैं कि सही उत्तर विकल्प 1 है।

textEn:
Identify the segment in the sentence which contains a grammatical error.

He could not / get a job / despite of / his best efforts.
textHi:
Identify the segment in the sentence which contains a grammatical error.

He could not / get a job / despite of / his best efforts.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'despite of'.

Key Points

  • The given sentence is grammatically incorrect.
  • Here, 'despite' should be used instead of 'despite of'.
  • The word 'despite' is never followed by 'of'.
  • We should not say 'despite of something', say 'despite something'.
    • Example: Despite having very little money, they enjoy life.
  • The word 'despite' means 'without being affected by the thing mentioned.' (के बावजूद)
  • Therefore, as per the points mentioned above, we find that the correct answer is Option 1.

Correct Sentence: He could not get a job despite his best efforts.

Hinglish

  • दिया गया वाक्य व्याकरण की दृष्टि से गलत है।
  • यहाँ पर 'despite of' के स्थान पर 'despite' का प्रयोग किया जाना चाहिए।
  • 'Despite' शब्द का कभी भी 'of' के साथ प्रयोग नहीं किया जाता है।
  • हमें 'despite of something' नहीं कहना चाहिए, 'despite something' कहना चाहिए।
    • Example: Despite having very little money, they enjoy life.
  • 'Despite' शब्द का अर्थ है 'बिना उल्लेख की गई बात से प्रभावित हुए'।
  • इसलिए, ऊपर वर्णित बिंदुओं के अनुसार, हम पाते हैं कि सही उत्तर विकल्प 1 है।

textEn:
Given below are six sentences A-F, of which A and F are static and B-E have been jumbled. Pick the option that gives the correct order of the jumbled sentences.

A. When the train pulled in at Chennai Central, it took Velu some time to get off.

B. So, he sat down on a nearby bench that was unoccupied.

C. In all his eleven years he had not seen so many people except at the village fair.

D. When he finally stood on the platform, his legs felt wobble.

E. He put his small bundle beside him on the bench and looked around.

F. Near him, a group of people sat on their luggage staring at a TV hanging from the roof.
textHi:
Given below are six sentences A-F, of which A and F are static and B-E have been jumbled. Pick the option that gives the correct order of the jumbled sentences.

A. When the train pulled in at Chennai Central, it took Velu some time to get off.

B. So, he sat down on a nearby bench that was unoccupied.

C. In all his eleven years he had not seen so many people except at the village fair.

D. When he finally stood on the platform, his legs felt wobble.

E. He put his small bundle beside him on the bench and looked around.

F. Near him, a group of people sat on their luggage staring at a TV hanging from the roof.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'ADBECF'.

Key Points

  • The given question is an example of a Para Jumble.
  • We know that the first statement of a para jumbled question is usually an independent general statement, a noun, a universal fact, starting off a story, starting of an incident or an event, or it starts with 'most' or 'once'.
  • In the given question, sentence A is the first sentence. And sentence F is the last one.
  • We have to rearrange the rest of the sentences accordingly.
  • Sentence A is the starting of an incident.
  • Sentence D will be the next sentence because it is connected with Sentence A.
  • Sentence B will be the next sentence because Sentence D tells us that Velu's legs wobbled and because of this, he sat down on the nearby bench.
  • Sentence E will be the next sentence because after sitting down on the bench, he put his small bundle beside him on the bench and looked around.
  • Lastly, sentence C will be used because when he looked around, he saw a sight that he had never seen in all his eleven years.
  • Thus, the sequence becomes:
    • A: When the train pulled in at Chennai Central, it took Velu some time to get off.
    • D: When he finally stood on the platform, his legs felt wobble.
    • B: So, he sat down on a nearby bench that was unoccupied.
    • E: He put his small bundle beside him on the bench and looked around.
    • C: In all his eleven years he had not seen so many people except at the village fair.
    • F: Near him, a group of people sat on their luggage staring at a TV hanging from the roof.
  • Therefore, as per the points mentioned above, we find that the correct answer is Option 1.

Correct Sequence: When the train pulled in at Chennai Central, it took Velu some time to get off. When he finally stood on the platform, his legs felt wobble. So, he sat down on a nearby bench that was unoccupied. He put his small bundle beside him on the bench and looked around. In all his eleven years he had not seen so many people except at the village fair. Near him, a group of people sat on their luggage staring at a TV hanging from the roof.
explanationHi:
The correct answer is 'ADBECF'.

Key Points

  • The given question is an example of a Para Jumble.
  • We know that the first statement of a para jumbled question is usually an independent general statement, a noun, a universal fact, starting off a story, starting of an incident or an event, or it starts with 'most' or 'once'.
  • In the given question, sentence A is the first sentence. And sentence F is the last one.
  • We have to rearrange the rest of the sentences accordingly.
  • Sentence A is the starting of an incident.
  • Sentence D will be the next sentence because it is connected with Sentence A.
  • Sentence B will be the next sentence because Sentence D tells us that Velu's legs wobbled and because of this, he sat down on the nearby bench.
  • Sentence E will be the next sentence because after sitting down on the bench, he put his small bundle beside him on the bench and looked around.
  • Lastly, sentence C will be used because when he looked around, he saw a sight that he had never seen in all his eleven years.
  • Thus, the sequence becomes:
    • A: When the train pulled in at Chennai Central, it took Velu some time to get off.
    • D: When he finally stood on the platform, his legs felt wobble.
    • B: So, he sat down on a nearby bench that was unoccupied.
    • E: He put his small bundle beside him on the bench and looked around.
    • C: In all his eleven years he had not seen so many people except at the village fair.
    • F: Near him, a group of people sat on their luggage staring at a TV hanging from the roof.
  • Therefore, as per the points mentioned above, we find that the correct answer is Option 1.

Correct Sequence: When the train pulled in at Chennai Central, it took Velu some time to get off. When he finally stood on the platform, his legs felt wobble. So, he sat down on a nearby bench that was unoccupied. He put his small bundle beside him on the bench and looked around. In all his eleven years he had not seen so many people except at the village fair. Near him, a group of people sat on their luggage staring at a TV hanging from the roof.

textEn:
Select the correct active form of the given sentence.

The animals in the zoo should not be fed.
textHi:
Select the correct active form of the given sentence.

The animals in the zoo should not be fed.
optionsEn: Don't fed the animals in the zoo.
Don't be feeding the animals in the zoo.
Feed not the animals in the zoo.
Don't feed the animals in the zoo.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'Don't feed the animals in the zoo'.

Key Points

  • The given sentence is in Passive Voice. As per the given question we have to change it into Active Voice.
  • The structure of the given sentence is as follows:
    • V1 + Object. (Active Voice)
    • Subject (Objective Case) + should + be + V3. (Passive Voice)
    • The given sentence is an example of an imperative sentence.
    • If someone gives a command, does request, expresses a desire or wish, suggestion, in sentences then such sentences are called an imperative sentence.
    • The notion of the given sentence is a suggestion.
    • Example:
      • Help the poor people. (Active Voice)
      • The poor people should be helped. (Passive Voice)
    • The subject of the given sentence is 'The animals'.
    • 'Should not be fed' should be changed into 'Don't feed'.
  • Therefore, as per the points mentioned above, we find that the correct answer is Option 4.
    ​

Correct Sentence: Don't feed the animals in the zoo.

Hinglish

  • दिया गया वाक्य Passive Voice में है। दिए गए प्रश्न के अनुसार हमें इसे Active Voice में बदलना है।
  • दिया गया वाक्य Imperative Sentence का उदाहरण है।
  • यदि कोई वाक्य में आदेश देता है, अनुरोध करता है, इच्छा व्यक्त करता है, सुझाव देता है, तो ऐसे वाक्यों को Imperative Sentence कहा जाता है।
    • दिए गए वाक्य की धारणा सुझाव है।
    • इस प्रकार के वाक्यों की संरचना इस प्रकार है:
      • V1 + Object. (Active Voice)
      • Subject (Objective Case) + should + be + V3. (Passive Voice)
    • Example:
      • Help the poor people. (Active Voice)
      • The poor people should be helped. (Passive Voice)
    • दिए गए वाक्य का Subject 'The animals' है।
    • 'Should not be fed' को 'Don't feed' में बदलना चाहिए।
  • इसलिए, ऊपर वर्णित बिंदुओं के अनुसार, हम पाते हैं कि सही उत्तर विकल्प 4 है।

textEn:
Select the most appropriate option to substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. If there is no need to substitute it, select 'No substitution'.

His fine voice has never heard in this hall again.
textHi:
Select the most appropriate option to substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. If there is no need to substitute it, select 'No substitution'.

His fine voice has never heard in this hall again.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'will never be heard'.

Key Points

  • The given sentence is grammatically incorrect.
  • Here, 'will never be heard' should be used instead of 'has never heard'.
  • The given sentence is in passive voice. And the notion of the given sentence is futuristic.
  • Hence, future tense will be used.
  • The passive verb form in the simple future tense is made by putting "will/shall + be before the past participle form of the verb."
    • Example: I will be helped by her.
  • Therefore, as per the points mentioned above, we find that the correct answer is Option 1.

textEn:

explanationEn:

Key Points

  • With these terms, you should pluralize only the noun.

textEn:
Select the correct passive form of the given sentence.

Do not allow strangers into the society complex.
textHi:
Select the correct passive form of the given sentence.

Do not allow strangers into the society complex.
optionsEn: Strangers should not be allowed into the society complex.
Let strangers must not allow into the society complex.
Strangers should not have been allowed into the society complex.
Let strangers should be not allowed into the society complex.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'Strangers should not be allowed into the society complex.'

Key Points

  • The given sentence is in Active Voice. As per the given question we have to change it into Passive Voice.
  • The structure of the given sentence is as follows:
    • V1 + Object. (Active Voice)
    • Subject (Objective Case) + should + be + V3. (Passive Voice)
    • The given sentence is an example of an imperative sentence.
    • If someone gives a command, do request, expresses a desire or wish, suggestion, in sentences then such sentences are called an imperative sentence.
    • The notion of the given sentence is a suggestion.
    • Example:
      • Help the poor people. (Active Voice)
      • The poor people should be helped. (Passive Voice)
    • The subject of the given sentence is 'Strangers'.
    • 'do not allow' should be changed into 'should not be allowed'.
  • Therefore, as per the points mentioned above, we find that the correct answer is Option 1.

Correct Sentence: Strangers should not be allowed into the society complex.

  • दिए गए वाक्य की संरचना इस प्रकार है:
    • दिया गया वाक्य Imperative Sentence का उदाहरण है।
    • यदि कोई वाक्य में आदेश देता है, अनुरोध करता है, इच्छा या इच्छा व्यक्त करता है, सुझाव देता है, तो ऐसे वाक्यों को Imperative Sentence कहा जाता है।
    • दिए गए वाक्य की धारणा सुझाव है।
    • इस प्रकार के वाक्यों की संरचना इस प्रकार है:
      • V1 + Object. (Active Voice)
      • Subject (Objective Case) + should + be + V3. (Passive Voice)
    • Example:
      • Help the poor people. (Active Voice)
      • The poor people should be helped. (Passive Voice)

  • इसलिए, ऊपर वर्णित बिंदुओं के अनुसार, हम पाते हैं कि सही उत्तर विकल्प 1 है।

textEn:
Select the most appropriate option to substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. If there is no need to substitute it, select 'No substitution'.

It is time we should have done something useful.
textHi:
Select the most appropriate option to substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. If there is no need to substitute it, select 'No substitution'.

It is time we should have done something useful.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is Did.

Key Points

  • We use the expression it's time + subject + past verb form to refer to the present moment.
    • Ex: Gosh! It's almost midnight. It's time we went home.
  • Should have + past participle talks about past events, actions that did not happen but it would be better if they had happened. A mistake or regret is implied.
    • Ex: You should have checked your report thoroughly before you handed it in.
  • Here, in the sentence, we need to use the past tense of the verb(did) because the sentence refers to the present moment.
  • Hence, the correct answer is option 4.​

Additional Information

  • It's time with a verb in the to-infinitive form can refer to the speaker and the listener together:
    • Ex: Come on. It's time to start packing. We have to leave in two hours. (or It's time we started packing.)
explanationHi:
The correct answer is Did.

Key Points

  • We use the expression it's time + subject + past verb form to refer to the present moment.
    • Ex: Gosh! It's almost midnight. It's time we went home.
  • Should have + past participle talks about past events, actions that did not happen but it would be better if they had happened. A mistake or regret is implied.
    • Ex: You should have checked your report thoroughly before you handed it in.
  • Here, in the sentence, we need to use the past tense of the verb(did) because the sentence refers to the present moment.
  • Hence, the correct answer is option 4.​

Additional Information

  • It's time with a verb in the to-infinitive form can refer to the speaker and the listener together:
    • Ex: Come on. It's time to start packing. We have to leave in two hours. (or It's time we started packing.)

textEn:
Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank.

Poverty was staring her ______ the face.
textHi:
Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank.

Poverty was staring her ______ the face.
explanationEn:
The most suitable preposition is "in".

Key Points

  • The expression "staring in the face" is an English idiom, meaning something is very obvious or imminent.
  • In the sentence, it implies that poverty was an immediate and clear problem for 'her', there was no avoiding it.

Therefore, the correct answer is "Option 4".

Complete Sentence: Poverty was staring her in the face.

Additional Information

  • At: We use at to refer to time or place. We also use it to refer to activities.
    • Ex: I was up at 6 am this morning.
  • To: The preposition 'to' is used as an infinitive of purpose to mean 'in order to'.
    • Ex: I spent some money (in order) to get some help.
  • On: The preposition 'on' indicates that something is already in the position.
    • Ex: The book is on the table.
explanationHi:
The most suitable preposition is "in".

Key Points

  • The expression "staring in the face" is an English idiom, meaning something is very obvious or imminent.
  • In the sentence, it implies that poverty was an immediate and clear problem for 'her', there was no avoiding it.

Therefore, the correct answer is "Option 4".

Complete Sentence: Poverty was staring her in the face.

Additional Information

  • At: We use at to refer to time or place. We also use it to refer to activities.
    • Ex: I was up at 6 am this morning.
  • To: The preposition 'to' is used as an infinitive of purpose to mean 'in order to'.
    • Ex: I spent some money (in order) to get some help.
  • On: The preposition 'on' indicates that something is already in the position.
    • Ex: The book is on the table.

textEn:
Identify the segment in the sentence which contains a grammatical error.

The news / of his involvement / in illegal activities / have shocked everybody.
textHi:
Identify the segment in the sentence which contains a grammatical error.

The news / of his involvement / in illegal activities / have shocked everybody.
explanationEn:
The erroneous part is 'have shocked everybody'.

Key Points

  • The error lies in Part 3 of the sentence.
  • Replace have with has.
  • As the subject 'the news' is a singular noun, we need the helping verb according to it.
  • Hence, has is the correct verb here.

The correct sentence: The news in illegal activities has shocked everybody of his involvement.

Additional Information

  • Some singular uncountable nouns end in –s. They have no plural forms. Examples: news, billiards, draughts, measles etc.
    • Ex: Measles is highly contagious.
  • Most words ending in –ics are also usually singular uncountables and have no plurals. Examples: mathematics, physics, athletics, politics.
    • Ex: Politics is a dirty game.
    • Mathematics isn't the easiest of all subjects.
  • Other nouns which do not change in the plural are craft, sheep, fish and deer.
explanationHi:
The erroneous part is 'have shocked everybody'.

Key Points

  • Replace have with has.
  • As the subject 'the news' is a singular noun, we need the helping verb according to it.
  • Hence, has is the correct verb here.

The correct sentence: The news in illegal activities has shocked everybody of his involvement.

Additional Information

  • Some singular uncountable nouns end in –s. They have no plural forms. Examples: news, billiards, draughts, measles etc.

    • Mathematics isn't the easiest of all subjects.
  • Other nouns which do not change in the plural are craft, sheep, fish and deer.

textEn:

textHi:
Select the most appropriate option to substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. If there is no need to substitute it, select 'No substitution'.

He is determined in expanding his business.
explanationEn:

Key Points

  • Determine to do something: to decide to do something
    • Ex: We determined to leave at once.
  • Here, in the sentence, because he decided to expand his business, the correct preposition to be used here is to.
  • Hence, the correct answer is option 1.

The correct sentence: He is determined to expand his business.

Additional InformationBare Infinitives (When Not Preceded by "To")

  • Most infinitives are preceded by "to," but after certain verbs, the "to" is dropped.
  • The most obvious example is when an infinitive follows "can," "could," "may," "might," "must," "shall," "should," "will," or "would" (i.e., a modal verb).
    • For example: You must take a break.

The correct sentence: He is determined to expand his business.

textEn:
Select the correct active form of the given sentence.

The breakfast will have been finished by nine.
textHi:
Select the correct active form of the given sentence.

The breakfast will have been finished by nine.
optionsEn: They will finish the breakfast by nine.
They will be finishing the breakfast by nine.
They will have finished the breakfast by nine.
They will have finish the breakfast by nine.
explanationEn:
The active voice of the given sentence is They will have finished the breakfast by nine

Key Points

Future perfect tense
The Future perfect tense is used to talk about something that will be completed before a specific time in the future.
Passive
Active
Object + will/shall + have+ been + V3 + by + subject
Subject + will/shall + have + V3 + object.
  • The instructions given below should be followed while changing a passive voice to an active voice.
    • Find the subject and object of the sentence and exchange their places; make changes in their cases as well if subject and object are pronouns.
    • Remove the preposition 'by' before the agent.
    • Change will/shall + have + been of the passive voice into will/shall + have to make it active voice.
    • At last line up the remaining part.
So, the final sentence is - They will have finished the breakfast by nine.
Additional Information

Impersonal Passive

  • Verbs without an object (intransitive verb) normally cannot form a personal passive sentence (as there is no object that can become the subject of the passive sentence). If you want to use an intransitive verb in passive voice, you need an impersonal construction – therefore this passive voice is called Impersonal Passive.
  • Example: he says – it is said
explanationHi:
The active voice of the given sentence is They will have finished the breakfast by nine

Key Points

Future perfect tense
The Future perfect tense is used to talk about something that will be completed before a specific time in the future.
Passive
Active
Object + will/shall + have+ been + V3 + by + subject
Subject + will/shall + have + V3 + object.

    • Find the subject and object of the sentence and exchange their places; make changes in their cases as well if subject and object are pronouns.
    • Remove the preposition 'by' before the agent.
    • Change will/shall + have + been of the passive voice into will/shall + have to make it active voice.
    • At last line up the remaining part.
So, the final sentence is - They will have finished the breakfast by nine.
Additional Information

Impersonal Passive

  • Verbs without an object (intransitive verb) normally cannot form a personal passive sentence (as there is no object that can become the subject of the passive sentence). If you want to use an intransitive verb in passive voice, you need an impersonal construction – therefore this passive voice is called Impersonal Passive.
  • Example: he says – it is said

textEn:
Select the correct passive form of the given sentence.

You have to see it to believe it.
textHi:
Select the correct passive form of the given sentence.

You have to see it to believe it.
optionsEn: It has to be seen to be believed.
It has to be seen to being believed.
It needs to being seen to be believed.
It have to be seen to believe.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is It has to be seen to be believed.

Key Points

  • While changing the voice of an infinitive sentence, we change the verb as follows;
    • To catch----- To be caught,
    • To help------ to be helped

So, the final sentence is - It has to be seen to be believed.

Key Points

I said to the postman

Is there a letter for me.

Reporting clause Reported speech

  • When the reporting speech is in Simple present tense then all the speech Changes to Simple past tense.
    • is----- was
  • Said to of the reporting verb changed into asked.
  • Replace comma (,) and inverted commas (" ") with the conjunction 'if'.
  • Treat reported speech as an affirmative sentence.

​So, the final sentence is: ​I asked the postman if there was a letter for me.

Additional Information

What is Direct & Indirect Speech?
  • Direct speech – reporting the message of the speaker in the exact words as spoken by him.
    • Direct speech example: Maya said, "I am busy now".
  • Indirect speech: reporting the message of the speaker in our own words
    • Indirect speech example: Maya said that she was busy then.

textEn:
Identify the segment in the sentence which contains a grammatical error.

Her health has being / steadily improving / since she started / practising yoga.
textHi:
Identify the segment in the sentence which contains a grammatical error.

Her health has being / steadily improving / since she started / practising yoga.
explanationEn:
The erroneous part is 'Her health has being'

Key Points

  • The words 'being' and 'been' are sometimes confused, the word 'been' is always used after 'have' whereas 'being' is never used after 'have'. It is used after 'be'.
  • 'Been' is the past participle of the verb 'be' and is usually used with the perfect aspect with 'have' in all its forms i.e. had and has
    • Ex: I have been busy.
  • 'being' is the present participle of the verb 'be' and can be used with the continuous form of the verb 'be' is all its forms i.e. am, is, was, are and were.
    • Ex: When I arrived at the scene of the accident the victim was being placed in an ambulance.
    • I don't know why but John is being really difficult today.
  • Hence, the correct answer is option 4.
The correct sentence: Her health has been steadily improving since she started practising yoga.
Additional Information

Being as a noun

  • The word 'being' can also be used as a noun.
    • Ex: A human being.

Being as a gerund

  • The word 'being' can be used as a gerund which is a type of noun.
    • Ex: Do all actors like being famous?
    • His being clumsy caused the accident.
explanationHi:
The erroneous part is 'Her health has being'

Key Points

  • The words 'being' and 'been' are sometimes confused, the word 'been' is always used after 'have' whereas 'being' is never used after 'have'. It is used after 'be'.

    • Ex: I have been busy.

    • Ex: When I arrived at the scene of the accident the victim was being placed in an ambulance.
    • I don't know why but John is being really difficult today.
  • Hence, the correct answer is option 4.
The correct sentence: Her health has been steadily improving since she started practising yoga.

    • Ex: A human being.

textEn:
Select the correct indirect form of the given sentence.

"Let us go to the hills for a vacation," Mother said to us.
textHi:
Select the correct indirect form of the given sentence.

"Let us go to the hills for a vacation," Mother said to us.
optionsEn: Mother suggested that let us go to the hills for a vacation.
Mother ordered us to go to the hills for a vacation.
Mother said that should we go to the hills for a vacation.
Mother suggested that we should go to the hills for a vacation.
explanationEn:
The indirect speech of the given sentence is 'Mother suggested that we should go to the hills for a vacation.'

Key Points

Mother said to us,

Let us go to the hills for a vacation

Reporting clause Reported speech

  • The sentence which is used to convey a command, a request, or a forbiddance is called an imperative sentence. So the given speech is an imperative one.
  • While changing the narration of an imperative sentence, we need to follow the given steps-
    • Change 'said to' in reporting clause into ordered/forbade/suggested/advised/requested etc.
    • The word 'let us' indicates that the sentence is a kind of suggestion so we used suggested in place of said to.
    • Replace comma (,) and inverted commas (" ") with the conjunction 'that'.
​​​​So, the correct sentence is: Mother suggested that we should go to the hills for a vacation.

Additional Information

  • Words like forbade, prohibit, interdict, etc. have 'not' hidden in their meanings. So, the use of 'not' with these words should be avoided.
  • Example:
    • He forbade me to have ice cream.
      Or
    • He asked me not to have ice cream.

textEn:

textHi:
Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank.

I request the favour of ______ granting me two days' leave.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'Your'

Key Points

  • Before a gerund, we need a possessive adjective of noun or pronoun.
    • Ex: I didn't like his going to New York without me.
  • Here, granting is a gerund noun and we need possessive case 'your' before it.
  • Hence, option 3 is the correct answer.

The correct answer: I request the favour of your granting me two days' leave.

Additional InformationUsing the possessive before a gerund

  • We often put a noun or pronoun in front of a gerund to show who or what is doing the action in the gerund. This noun or pronoun is called the subject of the gerund. In formal writing, the subject of the gerund should be in the possessive form:
    • Your leaving early was a wise decision.
    • We celebrated Gord's winning the contest.
    • Natalie objected to my borrowing her hockey stick.

textEn:
Given below are six sentences A-F, of which A and F are static and B-E have been jumbled. Pick the option that gives the correct order of the jumbled sentences.

A. One day I went to school with four rupees in my pocket to pay the school fees.

B. All through the day, the coins sat quietly in my pocket.

C. So, I was told that the fees would be collected the next day.

D. When school got over, the coins in my pocket began to speak.

E. But the teacher who was to collect the fees was absent.

F. They said that money was meant to be spent on good things like hot 'jalebis'.
textHi:
Given below are six sentences A-F, of which A and F are static and B-E have been jumbled. Pick the option that gives the correct order of the jumbled sentences.

A. One day I went to school with four rupees in my pocket to pay the school fees.

B. All through the day, the coins sat quietly in my pocket.

C. So, I was told that the fees would be collected the next day.

D. When school got over, the coins in my pocket began to speak.

E. But the teacher who was to collect the fees was absent.

F. They said that money was meant to be spent on good things like hot 'jalebis'.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is AECBDF.

Key Points

  • E connects the sentence A as 'but' contrasting what is mentioned in A. Hence, E is the first part.
  • C further connects the sentence 'E' with the conjunction 'so' which act as a result of sentence E. Hence, 'C' is the second part.
  • B and D are co-related where sentence B starts a new clause and part D completes that. Hence, B and D are the third and fourth parts of the sentence respectively.

The correct sentence after rearranging the parts: One day I went to school with four rupees in my pocket to pay the school fees, But the teacher who was to collect the fees was absent. So, I was told that the fees would be collected the next day. All through the day, the coins sat quietly in my pocket When school got over, the coins in my pocket began to speak. They said that money was meant to be spent on good things like hot 'jalebis'.

Hint

  • Important Rules to Solve the Para Jumbles:
    • Read the sentences attentively and try to comprehend the idea or theme of the sentence and eliminate the irrelevant options.
    • Try to identify the opening( independent sentence) and closing sentences ( concluding sentence).
    • Try to identify the mandatory pairs. Two sentences can be connected through various connectors and other determinants.
explanationHi:
The correct answer is AECBDF.

Key Points

  • E connects the sentence A as 'but' contrasting what is mentioned in A. Hence, E is the first part.
  • C further connects the sentence 'E' with the conjunction 'so' which act as a result of sentence E. Hence, 'C' is the second part.
  • B and D are co-related where sentence B starts a new clause and part D completes that. Hence, B and D are the third and fourth parts of the sentence respectively.

The correct sentence after rearranging the parts: One day I went to school with four rupees in my pocket to pay the school fees, But the teacher who was to collect the fees was absent. So, I was told that the fees would be collected the next day. All through the day, the coins sat quietly in my pocket When school got over, the coins in my pocket began to speak. They said that money was meant to be spent on good things like hot 'jalebis'.

Hint

  • Important Rules to Solve the Para Jumbles:
    • Read the sentences attentively and try to comprehend the idea or theme of the sentence and eliminate the irrelevant options.
    • Try to identify the opening( independent sentence) and closing sentences ( concluding sentence).
    • Try to identify the mandatory pairs. Two sentences can be connected through various connectors and other determinants.

textEn:

textHi:
Select the most appropriate option to substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. If there is no need to substitute it, select 'No substitution'.

What kind of the book do you want?
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'kind of book'.

Key Points

  • The phrase "kind of" does not take a definite article after it, no matter whether the noun that follows is countable or uncountable.
  • The use of the article 'the' after the phrase 'kind of' is incorrect.
  • Hence the article 'the' must be omitted to make the sentence grammatically correct.
  • Therefore the correct answer is option 2.

Correct sentence: What kind of book do you want?

Additional Information

  • In English there are three articles: a, an, and the.
  • Articles are used before nouns or noun equivalents and are a type of adjective.
  • The definite article (the) is used before a noun to indicate that the identity of the noun is known to the reader.
    • Example: This is the highest building in New York.

textEn:
Identify the segment in the sentence which contains a grammatical error.

Please switch off / all the lights / before you / are leaving the room.
textHi:
Identify the segment in the sentence which contains a grammatical error.

Please switch off / all the lights / before you / are leaving the room.
explanationEn:
The erroneous part is 'are leaving the room'

Key Points

  • In part ''are leaving the room" the usage of 'are leaving' is incorrect Instead, use 'leave'.
  • According to the rule of grammar, the usage of present continuous is correct when we want to use a temporary action but in the given, there is no temporary action is used. Hence, we need to use present indefinite tense. Also, in the main clause, the verb is in the present tense hence, we need to use a present tense in the subordinate clause too.

So the correct sentence is- Please switch off all the lights before you leave the room.

So the correct sentence is- Please switch off all the lights before you leave the room.

textEn:
Select the correct direct form of the given sentence.

Mother ordered the children to stop playing then.
textHi:
Select the correct direct form of the given sentence.

Mother ordered the children to stop playing then.
optionsEn: Mother said to the children, "Please stop playing then."
Mother said to the children, "Don't play then."
Mother said to the children, "Stop playing now."
Mother said to the children, "Can you stop playing now?"
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'Mother said to the children, "Stop playing now."'

Key Points

  • The given sentence is in Indirect speech.
  • Also, In Indirect speech the usage of 'infinitive' is given in the sentence, hence, from this we can say that the given reported speech should be an imperative one.
  • When you give someone an order, you use the imperative form, which means using just the verb without a subject.
  • Also, in Direct speech the reported verb will become 'said to' and 'that' will be removed
  • The adverb 'then' will be changed into 'now'

Hence, the correct speech is- Mother said to the children, "Stop playing now."

textEn:

A. The King and Queen of Siam had several daughters.

B. So, the King named his daughters January, February, March till he came to the youngest whom he named September.

C. The princesses were very proud of their parrots and spent time teaching them to talk.

D. One year on his birthday, the King gave each of his daughters a green parrot in a cage.

E. The Queen said it confused her to remember so many names.

F. In a short while all the parrots could say "God save the King".
textHi:

  • Hence the correct sequence is AEBDCF.
  • Therefore the correct answer is option 1.

  • The given phrase is to be replaced with 'overflowed its banks'
  • The verb forms of 'overflow' are- overflowed (past tense) and overflowed (past participle).
  • Also, in the given sentence 'It's' is incorrect we need to use the possessive case i.e. 'Its'
  • The contraction of 'it's' is 'It is'

Hence, the correct sentence is- The river overflowed its banks in the monsoon.

  • Also, the prepositional phrase to be written after the main verb.

So the correct passive sentence is- Free food grains are being distributed among the poor by the government.

textEn:
Identify the segment in the sentence which contains a grammatical error.

Once upon a time, / a beautiful princess / has fallen in love / with a poor farmer.
textHi:
Identify the segment in the sentence which contains a grammatical error.

Once upon a time, / a beautiful princess / has fallen in love / with a poor farmer.
explanationEn:
The incorrect part is 'has fallen in love'.

Key Points

  • The error lies in the part 'has fallen in love'.
  • The phrase 'Once upon a time' means at some time in the past (used as a conventional opening of a story).
    • Example: He was a famous actor once upon a time.
  • If something was "once upon a time" it is certainly in the past and complete, so it is normally followed by the simple past.
  • Hence 'has fallen' must be replaced with 'fell' to make the sentence grammatically correct.
  • Therefore the correct answer is option 3.

Correct sentence: Once upon a time, a beautiful princess fell in love with a poor farmer.

Correct sentence: Once upon a time, a beautiful princess fell in love with a poor farmer.

textEn:
Select the correct indirect form of the given sentence.

He said to his employee, "Do not provoke me unnecessarily."
textHi:
Select the correct indirect form of the given sentence.

He said to his employee, "Do not provoke me unnecessarily."
optionsEn: He requested his employee do not provoke him unnecessarily.
He told his employee not to provoke me unnecessarily.
He warned his employee not to provoke him unnecessarily.
He told to his employee to not provoke him unnecessarily.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'He warned his employee not to provoke him unnecessarily.'

Key Points

  • When the reported speech is in an imperative form, we follow the steps given below:
    • Change the sentence in direct speech from imperative to assertive.
    • The reporting verb said to is converted into warned - He is giving a warning to his employee.
    • The tense of reported speech is changed only when the reporting verb is in the past tense.
    • 'To infinitive' is used in place of comma and inverted commas followed by the 1st form of the verb.
    • 'Do not provoke' changes into 'not to provoke'.
    • The first person pronoun 'me' changes to 'him'.

So, the correct sentence will be: He warned his employee not to provoke him unnecessarily.

The correct answer is 'He warned his employee not to provoke him unnecessarily.'

    • 'To infinitive' is used in place of comma and inverted commas followed by the 1st form of the verb.
    • 'Do not provoke' changes into 'not to provoke'.
    • The first person pronoun 'me' changes to 'him'.

So, the correct sentence will be: He warned his employee not to provoke him unnecessarily.

textEn:
Select the correct direct form of the given sentence.

The young man exclaimed that it was a great feast.
textHi:
Select the correct direct form of the given sentence.

The young man exclaimed that it was a great feast.
optionsEn: The young man said, " What a great feast it is!"
The young man says, " It is a great feast."
The young man said, " It has being a great feast!"
The young man said, " Is it a great feast?"
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'The young man said, "What a great feast it is!"'

Key Points

  • The given sentence is an indirect speech.
  • The basic rules for changing or converting indirect speech into direct speech:​
    • The commas, inverted commas and exclamatory mark are added.
    • In the "direct speech", the exclamatory phrase or word (interjection) replaces 'exclaimed with joy, sorrow, regret, surprise, contempt etc'.
    • The full stop (.) in indirect speech, changes into the exclamation mark (!) in direct speech.
    • The conjunction 'that' is removed.
    • The past tense format 'Subject + was + Object' will be changed into the present tense format 'Subject + is + Object'.
  • Therefore the correct answer is option 1.

So the correct sentence will be: The young man said, "What a great feast it is!"

The correct answer is 'The young man said, "What a great feast it is!"'

  • The basic rules for changing or converting indirect speech into direct speech:​

    • In the "direct speech", the exclamatory phrase or word (interjection) replaces 'exclaimed with joy, sorrow, regret, surprise, contempt etc'.
    • The full stop (.) in indirect speech, changes into the exclamation mark (!) in direct speech.
    • The conjunction 'that' is removed.
    • The past tense format 'Subject + was + Object' will be changed into the present tense format 'Subject + is + Object'.
  • Therefore the correct answer is option 1.

So the correct sentence will be: The young man said, "What a great feast it is!"

textEn:
Select the correct indirect form of the given sentence.

Sania said to me, "Tomorrow is my convocation."
textHi:
Select the correct indirect form of the given sentence.

Sania said to me, "Tomorrow is my convocation."
optionsEn: Sania told me that the next day was her convocation.
Sania told me that tomorrow was her convocation.
I told Sania that her convocation was the next day.
Sania told to me that the next day is her convocation.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'Sania told me that the next day was her convocation.'

Key Points

  • The given sentence is in Direct Speech. As per the given question we have to change it into Indirect Speech.
  • The process of transformation is as follows:
    • Here, in the given question 'said to' will be changed into 'told' because the reporting speech has an object.
    • The conjunction 'that' will be added and comma and inverted commas will be removed.
    • The pronoun 'my' will be changed into 'her' because the first person pronouns are changed according to the subject of the reporting verb.
    • 'is' will be changed into 'was'.
    • Lastly, the adverb 'tomorrow' will be changed into 'the next day'.
  • Therefore the correct answer is option 1.

Correct sentence: Sania told me that the next day was her convocation.

Additional Information

  • Change in adverbs and determines in Direct To Indirect Speech.
Adverbs in 'direct speech' Adverbs in 'indirect speech'
Now Then
Today That day
Here There
This That
These Those
Ago Before
Today That day
Tomorrow The next day

textEn:
Identify the segment in the sentence which contains a grammatical error.

Parul is / ten years / elder than / her sibling.
textHi:
Identify the segment in the sentence which contains a grammatical error.

Parul is / ten years / elder than / her sibling.
explanationEn:
The incorrect part is 'elder than'.

Key Points

  • The error lies in the part 'elder than'.
  • We need to remember older than is the correct form.
    • Example: He is older than all his friends.
  • But usually, among siblings we generally use "elder".
  • The word 'elder' is always followed by the preposition "to".
    • Example: My son John is two years elder to my daughter.
  • So remember the point- Older than / Elder to.
  • Hence 'than' must be replaced with 'to' to make the sentence grammatically correct.
  • Therefore the correct answer is option 3.

Correct sentence: Parul is ten years elder to her sibling.

The incorrect part is 'elder than'.

  • Hence 'than' must be replaced with 'to' to make the sentence grammatically correct.
  • Therefore the correct answer is option 3.

Correct sentence: Parul is ten years elder to her sibling.

textEn:
Select the correct passive form of the given sentence.

They will cancel the football tournament this year.
textHi:
Select the correct passive form of the given sentence.

They will cancel the football tournament this year.
optionsEn: The football tournament has been cancelled this year.
The football tournament was cancelled this year.
The football tournament will be cancelled this year.
The football tournament is being cancelled this year.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'The football tournament will be cancelled this year'.

Key Points

  • The subject of the given sentence is 'They' and the object is 'the football tournament'.
  • In the passive, the subject of the sentence becomes the object and the object of the sentence becomes the subject.
  • The use of 'will' indicates simple future.
  • The passive verb form in the simple future tense is made by putting will / shall + be before the past participle form of the verb.
  • Hence 'will cancel' becomes 'will be cancelled'.
  • In passive voice we may omit the agent (They) because we don't know who they are, or it's implied or unimportant, or we'd rather not say.
  • Therefore the correct answer is option 3.

So the correct passive sentence is: The football tournament will be cancelled this year.
